Summary: MATLAB crashes when updating a model with Embedded MATLAB functions in a Stateflow link chart
Description: MATLAB crashes when updating a model with a Stateflow link chart. The linked library chart contains Embedded MATLAB functions that define large persistent array data. The top of the stack trace has the following signature. [0] sf.mexw32:0x182175bd(...)[1] cg_ir.dll:private: bool __thiscall CG::EnforceStackLimit::trim_off_stack_usage_along_path(struct PtrXarray_struct *)(...)[2] cg_ir.dll:public: bool __thiscall CG::EnforceStackLimit::enforce_stack_limit(void)(...)
Workaround: Instead of defining large persistent data inside Embedded MATLAB functions, define them as chart-level Local scope data.

Summary: Incorrect code generation: build process generates incorrect code for Rate Limiter block
Description: The Real-Time Workshop build process generates incorrect code for a Rate Limiter block when: The source signal is a scalar signal. The source signal is connected to Demux or virtual Selector block.
Workaround: At the input port of the Rate Limiter block, insert a Signal Convestion block.In the Signal Conversion block parameters dialog box, select Exclude this block from 'Block reduction' optimization.

Summary: Incorrect code generation: for Data-Type Conversion block when Block reduction parameter is selected
Description: The Real-Time Workshop build process might generate incorrect code for the Data Type conversion block under the following conditions: The Data Type Conversion block parameter Input and output to have equal  is set to Stored Integer (SI) . The input and output data type of the Data Type Conversion block have the same size and signedness. In the Optimization  pane of the Configuration parameters dialog box, the Block reduction  checkbox is selected.  ?
Workaround: In the Optimization pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box, clear the Block reduction parameter.

Summary: After installing a mix of R2008a and R2008a+ products on a Unix platform MATLAB can SegV when starting Simulink.
Description: Installing the R2008a products Signal Processing Toolbox 6.9 or Image Processing Toolbox 6.1 after installing the R2008a+ product Simulink 7.1.1, disables the Simulink Library Browser. This could occur if the R2008a version of these products is downloaded an installed at the same time as the R2008a+ version of Simulink.Clicking the Simulink icon or typing simulink at the MATLAB prompt causes MATLAB to throw a segmentation violation.The problem can occur when you download and install the current versions of all licensed products from the MathWorks Web site.
Workaround: Avoid the problem by installing all licensed R2008a products before installing R2008a+ products.

Summary: After installing a mix of R2008a and R2008a+ products on a Windows platform, Simulink library browser doesn't open.
Description: Installing the R2008a products Signal Processing Toolbox 6.9, Image Processing Toolbox 6.1, or Embedded IDE Link MU 1.0.1 after installing the R2008a+ product Simulink 7.1.1, disables the Simulink Library Browser. This could occur if the R2008a version of these products is downloaded an installed at the same time as the R2008a+ version of Simulink.Clicking the Simulink icon or typing simulink at the MATLAB prompt displays the following error, Error: Simulink library browser failed to initialize  in the MATLAB Command Window.The problem can occur when you download and install the current versions of all licensed R2008a products from the MathWorks Web site.
Workaround: Avoid the problem by installing all licensed R2008a products before installing R2008a+ products.

Summary: Why do I get a build error when I use S-function Builder blocks in my model?
Description: When S-function Builder blocks are used in a model, the model build process may fail with an error message similar to the following: The call to ccslink_ert_make_rtw_hook, during the exit hook generated the following error:  Error using ==> fevalError using ==> c6000_validateModelExit>checkForNoninlinedSFunctionBlocks at 38The model contains non-inlined s-function blocks, which cannot be used with the Link for CCS. Remove the following blocks from the model: berlekamp crcgen galois main rs   ?
Workaround: Download the attached patch installer patch_installer.m and the ZIP archive patch_491184.zip. This patch applies only to MATLAB Version 7.5 (R2007b).To install the patch, follow these steps.Start MATLAB.Enter the following command at the prompt to install the patch: patch_installer('patch_491184.zip', 'install')Follow the instructions on the MATLAB command line to complete the patch installation.

Summary: Error issued on entering a variable as a parameter in Battery block
Description: If you define a variable in the MATLAB workspace and then use it to specify the value for the Nominal Voltage  parameter of a Battery block, you get an error similar to: Error evaluating 'MaskCallback' callback of Battery block (mask) 'Battery'. Error using ? eval Undefined function or variable ...

Summary: A configurable subsystem configured as a model reference confusingly displays both its own icon and a model reference icon
Description: A configurable subsystem configured as a model reference displays both its own icon and a model reference icon, with the two icons overlapping. This can be confusing.
Workaround: Open the library containing the configurable subsystem.Unlock the library.Select the configurable subsystem.Execute set_param(gcb, 'ShowPortLabels', 'off')This prevents drawing of the subsystem block ports over the model reference block icon.Save the library.

Description: The Real-Time Workshop build process might crash MATLAB during code generation if any of the following blocks are used in an inlined atomic subsystem: Sum blocks with all operations specified as minuses Product blocks with all operations specified as divisions Sum blocks with more than two inputs using fixed-point data types and unequal biases Product blocks with more than two inputs using fixed-point data types and unequal biases

Summary: Workers on Macintosh sometimes stall or cause a kernel panic when starting
Description: When MATLAB workers are starting on a Macintosh computer, sometimes the Macintosh experiences a kernel panic. Other times, the workers might not start correctly, and can enter an "uninterruptible wait" (in a process listing from ps , the state displays as U+ ). This problem can occur for workers running under the local scheduler or under a job manager. This problem has appeared only on Macintosh systems running OS X versions 10.4.8 and later (Darwin kernel versions 8.8.*).

Summary: Referenced models containing blocks that detect zero crossings and are driven by discrete signals can cause errors
Description: Referenced models containing blocks that detect zero crossings and are driven by discrete signals can cause simulation and code generation errors. A block that can detect zero crossings, such as an Abs block, can contribute to these errors if the block's Enable zero-crossing detection  parameter is selected (ZeroCross  is set to on ). Simulation Errors:  When a referenced model simulates in any accelerated mode, incorrect simulation results or a segmentation violation can occur if the referenced model contains either of the following: A block that detects zero crossings and is driven by a discrete signal and a continuous signal. Two or more blocks that detect zero crossings, with one block driven by a discrete signal and another by a continuous signal.  Code Generation Errors:  When a referenced model that simulates in Normal mode contains one or more blocks that detect zero crossings, and every such block is driven only by discrete signals, code generated by Real-Time Workshop for the parent model might not compile.

Summary: Logging the output of a mixed-rate Model block or Subsystem block can give wrong time values
Description: When Simulink logs the output of a Model or Subsystem block with a hybrid sample time, and you enabled logging by right-clicking the signal and selecting Signal Properties > Log signal data , the values saved in the Time  field of the Simulink.Timeseries  object can be wrong, as follows: If a Model block references a model with multiple sample times, and one of the sample times is not represented by an output signal, the logged Time  values will be incorrect. If a Subsystem block uses multiple sample times, the block output is a non-virtual bus, and the output port sample time differs from the subsystem block sample time, the logged Time  values will be incorrect.

Summary: Incorrect code generation: Bus Selector block with output signal to certain blocks
Description: The Real Time-Workshop and Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder build process generate incorrect code when the output signal of a Bus Selector block connects to any of the following blocks: Vector Concatenation block Matrix Concatenation block, which is concatenating the last dimension of its input signals Merge block  ?
Workaround: Designate the output signal of the Bus Selector block as a test point.

Summary: Defining persistent variables in an Embedded MATLAB function that returns multiple outputs may produce incorrect results
Description: When you compile an Embedded MATLAB function in a Simulink model, or by using emlc  or emlmex , an attempt is made to eliminate redundant computation of expressions. This optimization can produce wrong answers under the following circumstances: You define a persistent variable in a function that returns multiple outputs, and The function is inlined in one call site, but not inlined in a different call site.
Workaround: Do not inline functions that return multiple outputs and define persistent variables. Disable inlining by using the eml.inline('never') construct in the function.

Summary: Incorrect code generation: Embedded MATLAB Functions in a Simulink model or compiled with emlc or emlmex may produce wrong answers when performing bitwise or and xor mathematical operations.
Description: When compiling an Embedded MATLAB Function, an optimization pass attempts to use the most efficient variable type by analyzing the possible range of values of variables and expressions. This optimization can produce incorrect results if the function performs bitwise or  or bitwise xor  mathematical operations. An obvious example is calling the MATLAB functions bitor  and bitxor . A more subtle example is calling the MATLAB sqrt  function for fixed-point data; in this case, sqrt  performs the bitwise or  operation under the hood. This bug is rare. In most cases, the incorrect analysis results will not lead to any change in the generated code. This bug does not affect simulations where debugging is enabled (debug simulations). One way to determine whether this bug occurred is to check whether a non-debug simulation gives different answers from a debug simulation. This bug also affects code generated using Real-Time Workshop.

Summary: Incorrect coverage results for Saturation block when using fixed-point data
Description: If you use fixed-point data with a Saturation block, the coverage that is reported after the simulation might not be accurate.

Summary: On a deployed UNIX machine, when trying to integrate with a C/C++ shared library by compiling driver source(s) against the MATLAB Component Library, headers are missing.?
Description: On a deployed UNIX machine, when trying to integrate with a C/C++ shared library by compiling driver source(s) against the MATLAB Component Library, headers are missing. Specifically, compiling generates errors for missing header files mclmcr.h  and mclcppclass.h  ?
Workaround: For R2007b, download the attached files and place them in the mcr_root/external/include directory.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder generates code for models containing reusable subsystems
Description: Generating code for a model causes MATLAB to crash under all of the following condition: The model contains a reusable subsystem. The reusable subsystem contains either: A Constant block with a custom parameter object A signal that references a custom signal object.   A Mux block connects to either: The output of the Constant block. The signal that references the custom signal object.   At least one input signal of the Mux block is an input to the reusable subsystem.
Workaround: On the input signal of the Mux block that is also an input to the reusable subsystem:Insert a Signal Conversion block.In the Signal Conversion block parameters dialog box, select the Override optimizations and always copy signal check box.

Summary: Saving a simulation animation to AVI file fails when Compress AVI File option selected
Description: Recording a simulation and saving it as an AVI file (using the Store in AVI File  option) fails when you select the Compress AVI File  option.
Workaround: To work around this bug:Make a back-up of the file $MATLABROOT\toolbox\matlab\audiovideo\@avifile\set.m by renaming it. $MATLABROOT is the MATLAB root directory. You can obtain it by entering matlabroot at the MATLAB command line.Save the attached file (set.m) in the same directory as the preceding file.Enter the following command: rehash toolboxcache

Summary: Transfer Fcn First Order and Unit Delay blocks: generated code gives wrong result when block parameter 'Initial condition' is complex
Description: Generated code gives the wrong numerical result for the Transfer Fcn First Order block and the Unit Delay block. This can happen when you set the block parameter 'Initial condition' on the dialog box as a complex number, and when the real part of the complex number is zero or negative.

Summary: Incorrect code generation: Nested conditionally executed subsystems generate code that gives wrong answer
Description: The Real-Time Workshop code generator might create code that gives the wrong answer for a model that contains a conditionally executed subsystem (for example, Enabled, Triggered, Function-Call, or Action Subsystem Blocks) with all of the following conditions: The conditionally executed subsystem contains If Action Subsystem Blocks, one of which connects to an else port. A Merge block is placed outside of the parent conditionally executed subsystem and combines all of the output signals.  ?

Summary: If you enter "sim(gcs)" or "rtwbuild(gcs)" at the command line, Stateflow coder errors are not reported correctly in the MATLAB Command Window.
Description: If you use a command (for example, "sim(gcs)" or "rtwbuild(gcs)") to simulate or generate code for a model, Stateflow coder errors are not reported correctly in the MATLAB Command Window. Instead, the following internal error message appears: Error using ==> rethrow Input must be a structure. ?
Workaround: A single MATLAB file patch, "targetman.m", is provided for R2007b to fix this problem.Download and extract the attached ZIP file and replace the following MATLAB file with the downloaded version.[MATLABROOT]/toolbox/stateflow/stateflow/private/targetman.m

Summary: Subsystem incorrectly inherits constant sample time causing blocks inside not to run
Description: Sample time propagation incorrectly assigns a constant sample time to a subsystem block if: the subsystem is inside a triggered-type subsystem (e.g., triggered, function-call, etc.) the subsystem contains only: blocks with constant sample time subsystem blocks that contain blocks with triggered sample time   This subsystem should receive a triggered sample time through sample time propagation. The constant sample time of the outer subsystem causes the triggered blocks inside the inner subsystem to never run.
Workaround: Add any nonsubsystem block that has a triggered sample time (cyan sample time color) to the subsystem which is incorrectly inheriting a constant sample time.?

Summary: External mode simulation may display incorrect data for uploaded Merge block signals
Description: During external mode simulation of a model in which a Merge block is driving an uploading block (a block such as Scope or Display that uploads target signal data to the Simulink host), the displayed signal data may be incorrect.
Workaround: To work around this bug, add a nonvirtual block that does not alter the signal value between the Merge block and the uploading block. For example, add an atomic subsystem through which the signal value passes unchanged.

Summary: Code generated from Mean and Variance blocks can produce inaccurate results for fixed-point inputs
Description: The elements of the output vector produced by the Mean and Variance blocks are in reverse order under the following conditions: The model is running RTW generated code The block input has a fixed-point data type The block is not in running mode
Workaround: Follow these steps to apply the patch:Download the attached file for the release you are running. For example, if you are running R2007b, download the file sdspstatfcns_r2007b.tlcRename the file sdspstatfcns.tlc by removing the characters starting with "_" and ending at "."Replace the file with the same name in the MATLAB directory matlab/toolbox/dspblks/dspmex/tlc_c/

Summary: Embedded MATLAB functions assert when calling polyfit, mldivide, mrdivide, or qr functions with odd-sized inputs
Description: Simulink or Real-Time Workshop may throw an assertion for models containing Embedded MATLAB functions that meet the following conditions: Call the function polyfit , mldivide , mrdivide , or qr  with odd-sized inputs.The bug is trigged by the following size combinations (where k  is a positive integer, and n  = 2*k +1): R = polyfit(ones(1,n),ones(1,n),k);  R = mldivide(ones(n,k+1),ones(n,1));  R = mrdivide(ones(1,n),ones(k+1,n));  [Q,R,P] = qr(ones(n,k+1),0);   (Inputs can have any value. The function ones  is used for illustration.) Get compiled with optimizations (for non-debug simulation or code generation with Real-Time Workshop)  Assertions caused by this bug contain the following message: CG::ReachingDefinitions::associateNumDefsWithStmts
Workaround: Change your algorithm to avoid the size combinations described above or patch your installation of MATLAB R2008a as follows:Use the ver command in MATLAB to check your version of MATLAB. This patch applies only to R2008a.Back-up $matlabroot$/toolbox/eml/lib/matlab/eml/eml_zlaqp2.m.Replace $matlabroot$/toolbox/eml/lib/matlab/eml/eml_zlaqp2.m with the version in the attached zip file attachment attachment_470596_20090422.zip.Rebuild the model.

Summary: Incorrect code generation: code generation for multitasking model may incorrectly apply for-loop fusion optimization to multitask protection code
Description: During code generation, the Real-Time Workshop software selectively applies for -loop fusion optimizations, such as combining code for multiple blocks into the same for -loop, improving code efficiency and readability. (For a demonstration of the capability, see the demo model rtwdemo_forloop .) However, code generation for a multitasking model may inappropriately apply the fusion optimization to a for -loop in which a global variable accessed between two tasks is used to control a critical code section, thus potentially removing the multitask protection. For example, in the following code, global flag semaphoreTaken  is set to 1  to protect resources used by Code body 1 , such as shared memory, from being modified by code in another thread while the for -loop containing Code body 1  is executing. semaphoreTaken = 1 for(i=1; i<N; i++) {/* Code body 1 */...}semaphoreTaken = 0for(i=1; i<N; i++) {/* Code body 2 */...} However, after for -loop fusion optimization, the two for -loops may be combined in such a way that Code body 1  loses its semaphore protection. For example: semaphoreTaken = 1semaphoreTaken = 0for(i=1; i<N; i++) {/* Code body 1 */.../* Code body 2 */...} This might allow shared memory protected by the semaphore to be modified from another thread while Code body 1  is executing, potentially resulting in data corruption. This bug can cause incorrect code to be generated for the Rate Transition block, if all of the following conditions are true: Rate Transition block option Ensure data integrity during data transfer  is selected. Rate Transition block option Ensure deterministic data transfer (maximum delay)  is cleared. The input port has a faster sample time or a higher priority than the output port. The input signal is a vector (signal width is greater than 1). The model contains at least one other signal with the same width, such that the for -loop fusion optimization can occur.
Workaround: To work around this bug, you can select the Rate Transition block option Ensure deterministic data transfer (maximum delay), which causes the generated code for the Rate Transition block to use an alternative data transfer approach that is not adversely affected by the for-loop fusion optimization. Note that selecting this option generates code that transfers data at the sample rate of the slower of the two blocks connected to the Rate Transition block, which can alter model behavior.

Summary: R2007b Simulink? Design Verifier is incompatible with some R2007b+ Design Automation Products
Description: If you install any of the following R2007b+ products on top of an R2007b installation that includes Simulink? Design Verifier: Simulink? 7.0.1 Stateflow? 7.0.1 Stateflow? Coder 7.0.1 Simulink Fixed Point 5.5.1 Real-Time Workshop? 7.0.1 Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder 5.0.1 Simulink? Verification and Validation 2.2.1 Fixed-Point Toolbox 2.1.1  Simulink? Design Verifier may operate incorrectly, causing errors.

Summary: R2007b Simscape is incompatible with some R2007b+ Design Automation Products
Description: If you install any of the following R2007b+ products on top of an R2007b installation that includes Simscape: Simulink? 7.0.1 Stateflow? 7.0.1 Stateflow? Coder 7.0.1 Simulink Fixed Point 5.5.1 Real-Time Workshop? 7.0.1 Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder 5.0.1 Simulink? Verification and Validation 2.2.1 Fixed-Point Toolbox 2.1.1  Simscape may operate incorrectly, causing errors.

Summary: MATLAB might crash during code generation for a model containing a data store with a data type defined by a Simulink.StructType object
Description: MATLAB might crash when Real-Time Workshop generates code for a model that contains a data store that defines: The data type using a Simulink.StructType  object. State properties using a Simulink.Signal  object with initial value 0 .
Workaround: Specify [] as the initial value for the Simulink.Signal object. The generated code is the same as when you specify 0 for the initial value.

Summary: Models with algebraic loops that are driven by atomic subsystems may cause a SEGV during an update diagram.
Description: This bug occurs in the following situation: Let B1 be a block in an algebraic loop that is driven by an atomic subsystem, AS1. Let B2 be the block in the atomic subsystem that drives B1. If the index of the output port of B2 that drives B1 is equal to or greater than the total number of output ports on AS1, a SEGV may occur during an update diagram.
Workaround: For the situation described in the bug, a workaround is to add a Signal Conversation block between block B2 and the output port of AS1, such that the Signal Conversion block is inside AS1.In the attached model, example.mdl: B1 is the "example/Switch"; AS1 is the "example/Atomic Subsystem"; and B2 is "example/Atomic Subsystem/Embedded MATLAB Function". Thus, adding a Signal Conversion block between "example/Atomic Subsystem/Embedded MATLAB Function" and "example/Atomic Subsystem/Out2" will serve as a workaround.

Summary: Using tstool to generate time plots of Simulink Timeseries objects with more than 5000 points can raise an error
Description: Creating a time plot for a Simulink timeseries object from tstool  which has more than 5000 points can produce the following error: ??? Subscript indices must either be real positive integers or logicals. Error in ==> tsguis.timeplot.timeplot>localUpdateLims at 97 h.updatelims; The error can occur either on clicking the Display  button on the Time Series Tab after clicking on the Simulink timeseries object  node in tstool , or upon dragging and dropping a Simulink timeseries onto the timeplots  node. ?
Workaround: In MATLAB 7.5 (2007b), remove line 96 containing the text "drawnow" from the file:(MATLABROOT)\toolbox\matlab\timeseries\@tsguis\@timeplot\addTs.mYou might need to restart MATLAB for the change to take effect.

Summary: Stateflow data type settings can be corrupted after the model is saved, closed, and reloaded.
Description: Settings for Stateflow data with "Bus" or "Fixed point" types can be corrupted after the model is saved, closed, and reloaded.

Summary: Incorrect code generation: generated code results for Bus Selector block does not match simulation results
Description: Real-Time Workshop and Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder software might generate incorrect code if all of the following apply to your model: The model has a Stateflow chart that contains a bus output signal. The Stateflow chart has exported graphical functions that write to the bus output signal. The bus output signal of the Stateflow chart is directly connected to a Bus Selector block  For example, the attached model gives different simulation and code generation results. The simulation result is correct while the code generation result is wrong.
Workaround: Make the output of the Bus Selector block a test point.

Summary: Generated code fails to compile when using data type replacements in conjunction with AUTOSAR target
Description: The Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder generated code fails to compile if all of the following conditions apply to your model: The model is configured to use an AUTOSAR system target file. The code generator creates the ERT S-function wrapper. The generated code replaces built-in data type names with user-defined replacement data type names.

Summary: Build process failure produces CGIR assertion error message
Description: The Real-Time Workshop build process might fail to generate code and reports the following error message: CGIR assertion in rtwcg_utils/rtwcg_tag.cpp(1001): 'getNodeRTWCGTag(startNode) == getNodeRTWCGTag(endNode) && getNodeRTWCGTag(startNode) == rtwTag'  [0] libmwsimulink.so:assertion_func(void*, char const*, int, char const*)()  [1] libmwrtwcg.so:RTWCG::RTWCGTagCtx::updateTagRegion(RTWCG::RTWCGNodeTag*)()  [2] libmwrtwcg.so:RTWCG::RTWCGTagCtx::traverseUnstructuredCfg(CG_Node*)()  [3] libmwrtwcg.so:RTWCG::RTWCGTagCtx::fcnRefreshTags(CG_Const*)()  [4] libmwrtwcg.so:RTWCG::RTWCGRefreshTags(CG_Scope*)()
Workaround: In the Configuration Parameters dialog, clear the Simulink Block / Stateflow object comments check box in the Real-Time Workshop > Comments pane.?

Summary: Error during code generation for models with nested conditionally executed subsystems
Description: The Real-Time Workshop build process might fail for models with nested, conditionally executed subsystems, if any of the output signals of the subsystems reset on disable. The build process reports the failure as an error message in the MATLAB Command Window similar to the following: %closefile, %selectfile or %flushfile argument must be a valid open file
Workaround: Change the Output when disabled parameters of the subsystem Outport blocks to held.

Summary: Generated code for a model that is referenced in Normal mode and uses a global parameter may be uncompilable
Description: When Real-Time Workshop generates code for a model, the code may be uncompilable if all of the following are true: The model contains a Model block that is set to Normal mode. The referenced model has a function-call port at the root level. A block in the referenced model is using a Simulink.Parameter that has a global storage class (for example, SimulinkGlobal  or ExportedGlobal ).  No warning or error message appears during code generation, but trying to compile the generated code causes an error.

Summary: Generated ERT example main function may incorrectly report overrun if multirate multitasking model has only one subrate
Description: If you generate code for a multirate multitasking model that has only one subrate, the generated ERT example main function will incorrectly report an overrun error when the subrate task is preempted by the base rate task. Specifically, in the function rt_OneStep  in the generated ert_main.c  or .cpp , the following code if (OverrunFlags[1]) return; is missing before the code /* Step the model for subrate */  if (eventFlags[1]) {  ...}
Workaround: To work around this bug in R2008a, perform the following steps:Note: In the following instructions, matlabroot represents your MATLAB installation root, as returned by the matlabroot command.In your MATLAB installation tree, rename the following files, for example, by adding .old to the end of each file name.matlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/mw/bareboard_mrmain.tlcmatlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/mw/ertcppmainlib.tlcmatlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/mw/ertmainlib.tlcDownload the attached files and place them at the same location as the files you renamed.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Model reference build generates error for model with no inputs or outputs
Description: The model reference build process generates an error for a model with no inputs or outputs when the model utilizes function prototype control. The error is similar to the following: Error using ==> tlc_new Error: File: C:\matlab\rtw\c\tlc\mw\codeinfomdlreflib.tlc Line: 119 Column: 28 Arguments to ISFIELD must be records

Summary: Generating code for a model that is referenced in Accelerator mode and uses a global parameter may cause a segmentation violation
Description: When Real-Time Workshop generates code for a model, a segmentation violation may occur if all of the following are true: The model contains a Model block that is set to Accelerator mode. The referenced model has a function-call port at the root level. A block in the referenced model uses a Simulink.Parameter  that has a global storage class (for example, SimulinkGlobal  or ExportedGlobal ).

Summary: Compiling functions with structure variables using emlc or emlmex may cause segment violation
Description: Using emlc  or emlmex  to compile M-functions with structure variables may cause a segment violation under the following conditions: The function has inputs or outputs of structure type There are more than 16 structure fields in all inputs and outputs Some structure fields are assigned, but never used by the function  This bug occurs during optimization when emlc  and emlmex  attempt to generate efficient code by splitting each structure variable into several scalar variables. The bug causes MATLAB to quit with an "assertion failed" message and produces a stack trace that refers to StructExplosion.cpp .
Workaround: Identify the problematic function myFcn and structure parameter myStruct. One method is to incrementally comment out parts of your source code until compilation succeeds.Apply one of the workarounds described below to isolate the structure parameter myStruct from the optimizer.Workaround #1. If you are generating a MEX file using emlmex or emlc -T MEX:Download the attached workaround_mex.zip, extract files workaround.m and dummy.m, and save them to the directory where you run emlmex or emlc.Add the statement workaround(myStruct); at the beginning of the function myFcn (see example below).Compile the program again; if the problem persists, apply the workaround to other suspcious functions.Workaround #2. If you are generating a C executable or library using emlc:Download the attached workaround_c.zip, extract files workaround.m and dummy.h, and save them to the directory where you run emlc.Add the statement workaround(myStruct); at the beginning of the function myFcn.Compile the program again, adding dummy.h to the emlc command:emlc myFcn.m myOptions dummy.hExample of using the workaround: suppose you have a function foo with two structure inputs in1 and in2. To isolate both of them from the optimizer, edit function header to be similar to the following:function foo(in1, in2)  workaround(in1);  workaround(in2); % Function body follows.

Summary: Incorrect code generation: incorrect code for Bus Selector block
Description: The Real-Time Workshop and Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder software might generate incorrect code for the Bus Selector block if the Reuse block outputs  check box is selected in the Configuration Parameters dialog box.
Workaround: To generate correct code for the Bus Selector block:In the Configuration Parameters > Optimization pane, clear the Reuse block outputs check box.Regenerate the code for your model.

Summary: Build process failure when generating code for model with SimViewing devices
Description: The Real-Time Workshop build process might fail while generating code for a model with subsystems or blocks that have the SimViewingDevice block parameter enabled. The build process reports the failure message in the MATLAB Command Window similar to the following: [2] libmwsimulink.dll:class slsvErrMsgQueue * __cdecl WritePrmReferencedBy(struct RTWInfo *,struct slAggParamTableEl_tag *,struct slParamBlockRefNode_tag * * *,int * *,int * *,int *)[3] libmwsimulink.dll:class slsvErrMsgQueue * __cdecl WriteModelParameters(struct RTWInfo *)[4] libmwsimulink.dll:class slsvErrMsgQueue * __cdecl WriteRTWFile(struct RTWInfo *)
Workaround: Find all blocks that have the SimViewingDevice parameter enabled by entering the following at the MATLAB command line: find_system(bdroot, 'LookUnderMasks', 'on', 'FollowLinks', 'on', 'Type', 'block', 'SimViewingDevice', 'on')Delete the blocks.

Summary: Build process failure when generating code for model with nested If Action or Switch Case Action Subsystems
Description: The Real-Time Workshop build process might fail to generate code for a model under all of the following conditions: An If or Switch Case block triggers a subsystem. The subsystem contains a Switch Case block that specifies a range of case conditions, for example, case [2 4] . A model option specifies that Simulink block comments are not inserted in the generated code.  The build process reports the failure message in the MATLAB Command Window similar to the following: CGIR assertion in .\dom_core\cg_lower_switch.cpp(67): 'region != 0'
Workaround: Turn on Simulink block comments by selecting the Simulink block / Stateflow object comments check box in the Real-Time Workshop > Comments pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box.

Summary: Unexpected results in Signal Builder block when changing signal ordering
Description: When using a Signal Builder block with multiple signal groups, changing the index of a signal in the Signal Builder block produces the expected results in the active signal group. However, it does not correctly reorder the waveforms in other groups. With multiple groups in a Signal Builder block, reordering signals in the active group should rename signals and adjust the line properties in other groups.
Workaround: To work around this issue, download and install the attached files.Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB>/toolbox/shared/sigbldr/private/ folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file: /toolbox/shared/sigbldr/private/change_channel_index.p/toolbox/shared/sigbldr/private/new_channel.pTo see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/toolbox/shared/sigbldr/private/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB>/toolbox/shared/sigbldr/private/ folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>/toolbox/shared/sigbldr/private/unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB>/toolbox/shared/sigbldr/private/ folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start ˇ°WinZipˇ± in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting ˇ°Run as administratorˇ±.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB>/toolbox/shared/sigbldr/private/ folder.

Summary: Segmentation violation occurs during code generation when model contains Math Function and Matrix Concatenation blocks
Description: A segmentation violation might occur when generating code with Real-Time Workshop or Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder software for a model that contains both a Math Function block and a Matrix Concatenation block if all of the following conditions are true: The Matrix Concatenation block is in vector mode. The output of the Math Function block is directly connected to the Matrix Concatenation block.  ?
Workaround: To prevent the segmentation violation, do the following:Insert a Signal Conversion block between the Math Function and Matrix Concatenation blocks.Select the Override optimizations and always copy signal check box in the Signal Conversion Block Parameters dialog box.

Summary: Simulink may exit with a segmentation violation when updating a mask parameter while simulation is running
Description: Simulink may exit with a segmentation violation under the following circumstances: The model has a masked subsystem with at least one tunable mask parameter. The subsystem has at least one Inport block, which may be nested several levels deep within the subsystem. A mask parameter of the masked subsystem is changed while simulation is running.  A stack trace similar to the following may be displayed: Stack Trace:[0] libmwsimulink.dll:bool __cdecl InportIsABusSource(class slBlock const *)(0x1d6ff720, 0, 0x1d6ff720, 0x00d3e8fc) + 97 bytes[1] libmwsimulink.dll:class slsvErrMsgQueue * __cdecl InportEvalParamsFcn(class slBlock *)(0x1d6ff720, 0x1d739a00, 0x1d6ff720, 0x1f810020) + 265 bytes[2] libmwsimulink.dll:class slsvErrMsgQueue * __cdecl EvalBlockAndPortParams(class slBlock *)(0, 0x1d6ff720, 0x048fbc20, 0x6faeef01) + 219 bytes[3] libmwsimulink.dll:class slsvErrMsgQueue * __cdecl BlockEvalParams(class slBlock *)(0x1d6ff720, 0x00d3e980, 0x6f626443, 0x1d6ff720) + 986 bytes[4] libmwsimulink.dll:class slsvErrMsgQueue * __cdecl EvalSubsystemChild(struct slBlockDiagram_tag *,struct slGraph_tag *,class slBlock *,double)(0x1d6ff720,0, 0x1d6ff580, 0x047d4300) + 94 bytes[5] libmwsimulink.dll:class slsvErrMsgQueue * __cdecl EvalSubsystemChildren(class slBlock *)(0x1d6ff580, 0, 0x1d6ff580, 0x00d3e9b0) + 307 bytes[6] libmwsimulink.dll:class slsvErrMsgQueue * __cdecl SubsystemEvalParamsFcn(class slBlock *)(0x1d6ff580, 0x047d4300, 0x1d6ff580, 0x1f810020) + 78 bytes[7] libmwsimulink.dll:class slsvErrMsgQueue * __cdecl EvalBlockAndPortParams(class slBlock *)(0x1d6ff580, 0x1d6ff580, 0x00d3ea2c, 0x27fd3ee0 "__ehhandler$??1RTWInfo@@QAE@XZ") + 219 bytes[8] libmwsimulink.dll:class slsvErrMsgQueue * __cdecl BlockEvalParams(class slBlock *)(0x1d6ff580, 0x27fd3ee0 "__ehhandler$??1RTWInfo@@QAE@XZ", 0x00d3ea88 "?????"", 0x6f6873df) + 834 bytes[9] libmwsimulink.dll:class slsvErrMsgQueue * __cdecl BlockEvalParamsOutsideDiagramUpdating(class slBlock *)(0x1d6ff580, 0x046c0230, 0xffffffff, 0x00d3eb64) + 54 bytes[10] libmwsimulink.dll:void __cdecl matl_set_param(int,struct mxArray_tag * * const,int,struct mxArray_tag * * const)(0, 0x00d3eb64, 0x1a146520, 0x00d3ebc4) + 1727 bytes
Workaround: Do not update mask parameters for subsystems with Inport blocks while simulation is running.

Summary: Generated code for model containing reusable subsystem might not compile
Description: The Real-Time Workshop code generator implements a subsystem as a function with arguments if either of the following is true: The Real-Time Workshop system code  parameter for the subsystem is set to Reusable function . The Real-Time Workshop system code  parameter for the subsystem is set to Auto  and Simulink reuses the subsystem for multiple instances  When a subsystem is implemented as a function with arguments, the generated code is invalid if all of the following are true: One of the inputs to the subsystem is connected to a block output that is defined as a nonaddressable storage class. The storage class of that block output is a signal with a custom storage class that is not addressable, such as BitField  or GetSet . The subsystem input is passed to its function by pointer.  The generated code does not compile because it attempts to take the address of the variable or expression corresponding to the input of the Subsystem block.
Workaround: Do one of the following:Use a Signal Converstion block: Place a Signal Conversion block at the forementioned input to the Subsystem block.Select the Override optimizations and always copy signal check box for the Signal Conversion block.Change the Real-Time Workshop system code parameter for the subsystem to Inline or Function.
Fixed In: R2007b web release, R2008a web release, R2008b

Summary: Possible code generation failure due to quantities with word size greater than 32 bits
Description: Simulink releases before R13SP1 included partial support of code generation for fixed-point signals and intermediate variables with word size greater than 32 bits. This support decreased in R13SP1, and continued to decrease through subsequent releases. Consequently, code generation for a model that has signals or blocks with intermediate quantities (like accumulators) that have more than 32 bits can fail in later releases even though it succeeded in earlier releases. The resulting error message resembles the following: Error building Real-Time Workshop target for block diagram 'model40bit'. MATLAB error message:Error using ==> rtwgenError reported by S-function 'sdspmsumprod' in 'model40bit/Matrix Sum':The maximum word length supported for code generation is 32 bits.  Neither EML nor Stateflow has this problem: it can occur only in Simulink blocks and blocksets. All support was restored in R2008a.
Workaround: Limit all word sizes, data types, and intermediate quantities in the model to be at most 32 bits long.

Summary: Noncompilable code generated for model containg nonvirtual bus
Description: The Real-Time Workshop and Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder software might generate noncompilable code if both of the following apply to a block in your model: An output port of the block is a nonvirtual bus and it is connected to the root outport. The RTW storage class  property of the output signal of the same block is set to ImportedExternPointer .

Summary: Incorrect code generation: Code fails to link models with exported graphical functions in Stateflow charts
Description: The Real-Time Workshop build process might not generate code for exported graphical functions in Stateflow charts when the model or subsystem only contains either of the following: Stateflow charts with exported graphical functions that call function-call subsystems Stateflow charts with exported graphical functions
Workaround: Add a Real-Time Workshop custom code block that contains a comment to the subsystem that contains the Stateflow chart with the missing exported graphical functions.

Summary: Build process failure when generating code for model with Stateflow charts that export graphical functions
Description: The Real-Time Workshop build process might fail to generate code for a model under all of the following conditions: The model contains multiple Stateflow charts that are in different, noninlined Subsystem blocks (Subsystem blocks with Real-Time Workshop system code  parameter set to Function  or Reusable function ). The Stateflow charts use and export graphical functions.  The build process reports the failure message in the MATLAB Command Window similar to the following: Stack Trace:CGIR assertion in c:\work\acgir\matlab\src\cg_ir\bundles\../base_core/expr/AbstractCallExpr.cpp(74): 'f!=0' [0] libmwsimulink.dll:void __cdecl assertion_func(void *,char const * const,int,char const * const)() + 46 bytes[1] cg_ir.dll:void __cdecl client_assertion_failed(char const *,int,char const *)() + 30 bytes
Workaround: Enter the following at the MATLAB command line:sf('Feature', 'Coder Unification', 0)Note: Entering this command disables enhanced inlining and cross block optimizations added for Stateflow charts in R2008a.

Summary: Incorrect code generation: Missing index expression for accessing N-D tunable parameter in Stateflow chart
Description: The Real-Time Workshop code generator eliminates code for second and higher dimension index expressions if the expressions represent a Stateflow chart accessing N-D tunable parameters. For example, the following matrix element access: y = P[2][3];  is generated as: y = P[2];  ?
Workaround: Avoid this by entering the following at the MATLAB command line:sf('Feature', 'Coder Unification', 0);

Summary: Stateflow charts using unary minus expressions may generate spurious errors
Description: The Stateflow debugger throws a spurious divide by zero  error and stops simulation under the following conditions: The chart uses a signed integer variable in a unary minus expression The integer has a run-time value of zero at some point in the simulation  The attached model divbyzero_bug.mdl  illustrates how this error occurs. Notice that the input u2  is of type int32  after type propagation is complete.
Workaround: Use the expression 0-data rather than the unary expression -data. The attached model divbyzero_bug_workaround.mdl illustrates this workaround.

Summary: Simulink mask icons using very large images may cause Library Browser slowdowns in R2008a.
Description: The new Simulink Library Browser can slow down very badly for masked block icons containing large image files.
Workaround: Use smaller images for masked block icons.

Summary: Stairs error encountered when viewer connected to a vector input
Description: The stairs error is encountered when a signal viewer is connected to a vector input signal. Prior to R2008a, the scope was used to handle matrix inputs; it errored out for vector inputs.

Summary: Simscape and SimHydraulics fail to generate code with Open Watcom 1.3 compiler
Description: Code generation with Open Watcom 1.3 compiler for a model containing Simscape or SimHydraulics blocks fails with the following error: Error(F38):(D:\R2007b\matlab\toolbox\physmod\network_engine\lib\win32\nesolver_openwatc13.lib) does not exist and cannot be made from existing files Error(E02): Make execution terminated s:\tmp\ssc_simple_mechanical_system_rsim_rtw>echo The make command returned an error of 4 The make command returned an error of 4 s:\tmp\ssc_simple_mechanical_system_rsim_rtw>An_error_occurred_during_the_call_to_make 'An_error_occurred_during_the_call_to_make' is not recognized as an internal or external command, operable program or batch file. ### Real-Time Workshop build procedure for model: 'ssc_simple_mechanical_system' aborted due to an error.
Workaround: Follow the steps below:Download the attached file pmsl_rtwmakecfg.p into your $MATLABROOT/toolbox/physmod/pm_util/pm_sli/ directory.Make a backup copy of the existing $MATLABROOT/toolbox/physmod/network_engine/ne_sli/rtwmakecfg.p file.Download the attached file rtwmakecfg.p into your $MATLABROOT/toolbox/physmod/network_engine/ne_sli/ directory to replace the existing rtwmakecfg.p file.$MATLABROOT is directory returned by running matlabroot command in your MATLAB command window.After making the changes described above you should be able to generate code with Open Watcom compilers.

Summary: SimDriveline fails to generate code with Open Watcom 1.3 compiler
Description: Code generation with the Open Watcom 1.3 compiler for a model containing SimDriveline blocks fails with the following error: Error(F38):($MATLABROOT \toolbox\physmod\drive\lib\win32\drive_openwatc13.lib) does not exist and cannot be made from  existing files Error(E02): Make execution terminated s:\tmp\drive_sgear_rsim_rtw>echo The make command returned an error of 4 The make command returned an error of 4 s:\tmp\drive_sgear_rsim_rtw>An_error_occurred_during_the_call_to_make 'An_error_occurred_during_the_call_to_make' is not recognized as an internal or external command, operable program or batch file. ### Real-Time Workshop build procedure for model: 'drive_sgear' aborted due to an error.
Workaround: If you are using R2007b version, follow these steps:Download the attached file pmsl_rtwmakecfg.p into your $MATLABROOT/toolbox/physmod/pm_util/pm_sli/ directory.From the directory $MATLABROOT/toolbox/physmod/drive/drive/, make a backup copy of the existing rtwmakecfg.m file.Download the attached file rtwmakecfg.m into your $MATLABROOT/toolbox/physmod/drive/drive/ to replace the existing rtwmakecfg.m file.If you are using a version older than R2007b, follow these steps instead:From the directory $MATLABROOT/toolbox/physmod/drive/drive/, make a backup copy of the existing rtwmakecfg.m file.Open the $MATLABROOT/toolbox/physmod/drive/drive/rtwmakecfg.m fileChange the line makeInfo.precompile = 1; to makeInfo.precompile = 0; and save the file.$MATLABROOT is the directory returned by entering the matlabroot command in your MATLAB command window.After making the changes described above, you should be able to generate code with Open Watcom compiler.

Summary: SimMechanics fails to generate code with Open Watcom 1.3 compiler
Description: Code generation with Open Watcom 1.3 compiler for a model containing SimMechanics blocks fails with the following error: Error(F38):($MATLABROOT \toolbox\physmod\mech\lib\win32\mech_openwatc13.lib) does not exist and cannot be made from existing files Error(E02): Make execution terminated s:\tmp\mech_spen_rsim_rtw>echo The make command returned an error of 4 The make command returned an error of 4 s:\tmp\mech_spen_rsim_rtw>An_error_occurred_during_the_call_to_make 'An_error_occurred_during_the_call_to_make' is not recognized as an internal or external command, operable program or batch file. ### Real-Time Workshop build procedure for model: 'mech_spen' aborted due to an error.
Workaround: If you are using the R2007b version, follow these steps:Download the attached file pmsl_rtwmakecfg.p into your $MATLABROOT/toolbox/physmod/pm_util/pm_sli/ directory.From the $MATLABROOT/toolbox/physmod/mech/mech/ directory, make a backup copy of the existing rtwmakecfg.m file.Download the attached file rtwmakecfg.m into your $MATLABROOT/toolbox/physmod/mech/mech/ directory to replace the existing rtwmakecfg.m file.If you are using a version older than R2007b, follow these steps instead:From the $MATLABROOT/toolbox/physmod/mech/mech/ directory, make a backup copy of the existing rtwmakecfg.m file.Open $MATLABROOT/toolbox/physmod/mech/mech/rtwmakecfg.m file.Change the line makeInfo.precompile = 1; to makeInfo.precompile = 0; and save the file.$MATLABROOT is directory returned by entering the matlabroot command in your MATLAB command window.After making the changes described above you should be able to generate code with Open Watcom compiler.

Summary: PolySpace verification stops with following message: "rte-find.x86-mingw32.exe: sanity check of the fnmatch()" or "This program cannot be run in standalone mode".
Description: When using PolySpace release  R2008a , version 5.1  on any Asian Windows based machine, or on PolySpace release R2007a+, version 4.2 , the verifications stops with one or the two previous error messages. The first message is displayed almost at the compilation phase and during the main generation: Generating the Main ...Generating call to function: RTEC:\DOCUME~1\user\LOCALS~1\Temp\PolySpace\C_R2008a\9A4_1204790849\PSTCACHE\rte-find.x86-mingw32.exe: sanity check of the fnmatch() library function failed.Doing code transformations ...**************************************************************** C sources verification done*************************************************************  When facing such message in the log file, verification could stop with an FFT message like the foll?owing: "@FFT: procedure __PST__MAIN__ENTRY__POINT__ is missing " The second message could be displayed at the begining of the intermediate language translation phase (il): *** Beginning C to intermediate language translation*************************************************************This program cannot be run in standalone mode  It could be also displayed at the end of the Control and Data Flow Analysis phase (CDFA): ?***  *** Control and Data Flow Analysis: part 2 done ***  ***  *** Control and Data Flow Analysis: beginning of part 3 ***  This program cannot be run in standalone mode
Workaround: Note that the following workaround is only available for a R2007a+ release:To workaround this issue on a Windows system:Browse to the folder %POLYSPACE_C%\Verifier\bin\x86-win32Create backup copies of the four following files: dme0.cx86-win32, iabc-c2if.cx86-win32, iabc-cpp2if.cx86-win32 and iabc-dfa.cx86-win32Download the four zip files 453453_Windows_R2007aplus_[1-4].zipExtract '*.cx86-win32' files from zip archives and copy them to %POLYSPACE_C%\Verifier\bin\x86-win32To workaround this issue on a Linux system:Browse to the folder $POLYSPACE_C/bin/x86-linuxCreate backup copies of the four following files: dme0.x86-linux, iabc-c2if.x86-linux, iabc-cpp2if.x86-linux and iabc-dfa.x86-linuxDownload the four zip files 453453_Linux_R2007aplus_[1-4].zipExtract '*.x86-linux' files from zip archives and copy them to $POLYSPACE_C/bin/x86-linux

Summary: Attempting to save, in an earlier version format of Simulink, a Simulink model that contains a Signal Builder block whose dialog box is open, creates a model that generates warnings when opened in earlier version of Simulink
Description: Attempting to save, in an earlier version format of Simulink, a Simulink model that contains a Signal Builder block whose dialog box is open, creates a model that generates warnings when you try to open it in earlier version of Simulink. The warning will be: Warning: Error evaluating 'LoadFcn' callback of Sigbuilder block block (mask) 'Signal Builder'. Reference to non-existent field 'allXData'.
Workaround: As a workaround, do one of the following:Close the Signal Builder block GUI, then save model in older version format.Download the attached files and update your MATLAB installation, as follows:Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file: /toolbox/shared/sigbldr/private/create_save_struct.p/toolbox/shared/sigbldr/private/dataSet_store.p/toolbox/simulink/simulink/slsaveassup.mTo see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start ˇ°WinZipˇ± in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting ˇ°Run as administratorˇ±.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Simulink printing does not print texts that are property keywords (in UI controls like figures, axes, text, etc.) such as 'Parent'
Description: Simulink printing does not print texts that are property keywords (in UI controls like figures, axes, text, etc.) such as 'Parent'

Summary: Incorrect code generation: Real-Time Workshop might generate incorrect code for nested action subsystems
Description: The Real-Time Workshop build process generates incorrect code for action subsystems if all of the following conditions are true: The model contains nested subystems with action blocks (for example, a Switch Case block with action subsystems, at least one of which contains another Switch Case block with action subsystems). Inside the action subsystems, the Action Port block parameter States when execution is resumed  is set to reset , which means that the states of the action subsystem is reinitialized when the subsystem is reenabled. The nested action subsystems contain blocks with states, such as Unit Delay blocks.  If all of the listed conditions apply, the resulting generated code does not reset the states of the nested action subsystems if the parent action subsystem resumes after being disabled.

Summary: Generated code of model with bus object noncompilable
Description: If a model contains a bus, and the bus object has a field which is a 1x1 or 1x1x...x1 matrix, the generated code might not compile. When you generate code for the attached model, the code generator creates the following extra casts, which violate the ISO C rule that casting to a structure type  is not allowed. bus_error_Y.Created_Bus = (Bus_Type3)(Bus_Type3)  rtb_BusConversion_InsertedFor_C;

Description: Creating and viewing data in the Signal Builder block takes longer than in previous releases.
Workaround: To work around this issue, download and install the attached files.Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file: /toolbox/shared/sigbldr/@SigSuite/SigSuite.p/toolbox/shared/sigbldr/@SigSuiteGroup/SigSuiteGroup.p/toolbox/shared/sigbldr/@SigSuiteSign?al/SigSuiteSignal.pTo see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file.
