Summary: SimEvents scopes issue a warning when used in user-defined libraries
Description: If a user-defined library contains a SimEvents scope block, then opening it in a model causes the following warning: Warning: Error occurred in 'mylib/Signal Scope'. Errorevaluating 'LoadFcn' callback of Signal Scope block (mask)'mylib/Signal Scope'. Error using ==> set_paramAttempt to modify a locked (read-only) library.Error found in 'LoadModel' callback for block:'mylib/Signal Scope'.

Summary: Real-Time Windows Target installer fails for the Windows Vista OS platform
Description: Real-Time Windows Target installer fails for the Windows Vista OS platform. Running the following command in the MATLAB Command Window: rtwintgt -setup generates the following errors: ??? Error using ==> rtwintgt>RunInstaller at 318Installer has failed with error code 2. Error in ==> rtwintgt>rtwt_setup at 118reboot = RunInstaller('install', srcpath); Error in ==> rtwintgt at 28 rtwt_setup(noprompt);
Workaround: To workaround this issue:Stop MATLAB.Download the attached zip file to any directory.Start MATLAB.Execute the following command in MATLAB: unzip('rtwinvista.zip',matlabroot)The patch will overwrite three files in the Real-Time Windows Target directory.Reinstall the Real-Time Windows Target software:rtwintgt -setup

Summary: Code for a reusable subsystem may be invalid if the subsystem is connected to a Merge block
Description: The code generated for a reusable subsystem is invalid if an output of the subsystem is connected to a Merge block whose output has a non-addressable custom storage class. Background:  Real-Time Workshop implements a subsystem as a function with arguments if either of the following is true: The subsystem's "Real-Time Workshop system code" parameter is "Reusable function" The subsystem's "Real-Time Workshop system code" parameter is "Auto" and Simulink reuses the subsystem along with at least one other indentical instance  Bug:  When a subsystem is implemented as a function with arguments, the generated code is invalid if both of the following are true: One of the subsystem's outputs is connected to a Merge block The output of the Merge block is a signal with a custom storage class that is not addressable, such as "BitField" or "GetSet"  In R2006b and earlier, the generated code compiles but the compiled code is incorrect and has the wrong behavior. In R2007a and later, the generated code does not compile, because it attempts to take the address of the variable or expression corresponding to the output of the Merge block.
Workaround: Two different workarounds exist. One workaround technique is:Place a Signal Conversion block after the Merge block.Move the label from the output of the Merge block to the output of the Signal Conversion block.Select the Signal Conversion block's parameter "Override optimizations and always copy signal"Alternatively, set the subsystem parameter "Real-Time Workshop system code" to "Inline" or "Function".

Summary: Top model may not simulate or update after a referenced model's configuration set is overwritten
Description: If a model hierarchy contains a submodel that executes in Normal mode and uses a configuration reference to access a configuration set, the following sequence of events will not succeed: Simulate the top model or execute Update Diagram on it. Overwrite the Normal mode submodel's configuration set with a different configuration set that has the same name as the original set. Simulate or update the top model  This sequence of events will fail with the followings warnings: The configuration set 'configset ' referred to by the active configuration reference 'configref ' was out of date. The configuration set has been updated, so model compilation was stopped to avoid inconsistencies. Restart the operation from the beginning. A configuration set reference does not allow writing to parameters in the source configuration set.  Generating code for a model does not initiate or encounter this problem.
Workaround: After overwriting the Normal mode submodel's configuration set, closing and reopening the submodel will avoid the warning. Alternatively, if you start the simulation or update the model a second time, you will not encounter this problem.

Summary: PolySpace does not recognize the environment variable LM_LICENSE_FILE/MLM_LICENSE_FILE under Windows/cygwin
Description: Even if you set up LM_LICENSE_FILE=port@host (or MLM_LICENSE_FILE) with a valid FLEXlm server, PolySpace does not recognize the environment variable and stops with a -1 FLEXlm error message. This problem is caused by Cygwin.

Summary: Build process failure with error message: Error: File: matlabroot\rtw\c\tlc\lib\blkiolib.tlc
Description: The Real-Time Workshop build process might fail to generate code and reports error messages similar to the following: Error: File: matlabroot \rtw\c\tlc\lib\blkiolib.tlc Line: 1329 Column: 26The specified index (2) was out of the range 0 - 1Error: File: matlabroot  \rtw\c\tlc\lib\blkiolib.tlc Line: 1329 Column: 33Ambiguous reference to DataOutputPort - must use array index to refer to one of multiple scopesError: File: matlabroot  \rtw\c\tlc\lib\blkiolib.tlc Line: 1329 Column: 33The left-hand side of a . operator must be a valid scope identifier  ? ?

Summary: Models that Contain the Simulink Signal Builder Block Might Cause Simulation Errors
Description: If Group 1 is not the active signal group when you save a model that contains a Signal Builder block, the last signal of Group 1 will be corrupted. If the length of the corrupted signal differs from the original signal length, the issue can manifest in the following ways: Simulation results are unexpected when using Group 1. For example, if you open a model and run Group 1 as the model input, an error like the following is generated: Error in 'mpc555_fuelsys_vv/Test Cases': Initialization commands cannot be evaluated. MATLAB error message: Error using ==> horzcat CAT arguments dimensions are not consistent.  Simulation results are unexpected when you click the Run all  button. For example, if you open the model and run all test cases from the Signal Builder block, an error like the following is generated: Simulation failed: Error using ==> cvsim at 65 Simulation failed   If the length of the corrupted signal is the same as the original signal, the simulation will be successful, but the resulting data will not be as expected. Check your data if you suspect corruption.  ?
Workaround: To work around this issue by download the attached files.Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB>/toolbox/shared/sigbldr/private/ folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file: /toolbox/shared/sigbldr/private/new_channel.p/toolbox/shared/sigbldr/private/restore_from_saveStruct.pTo see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/toolbox/shared/sigbldr/private/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB>/toolbox/shared/sigbldr/private/ folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>/toolbox/shared/sigbldr/private/unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB>/toolbox/shared/sigbldr/private/ folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start ˇ°WinZipˇ± in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting ˇ°Run as administratorˇ±.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB>/toolbox/shared/sigbldr/private/ folder.

Summary: PolySpace verification stops during C++ source normalization with the message: "exception Overflow(Overflow) raised"
Description: When launching PolySpace verification on C++ code, the analysis stops with the following message in the log file: **** C++ source normalization 7 (P_PRM)  Some stats on virtual calls (#a number):  Number of virtual functions: #a number  Number of virtual calls:  ...Stubbing undefined and wrote selected global variables ...* Generating definition for constant variable: A::a...* Generating definition for constant variable: MyClass::MyVariableN* Generating definition for constant variable: MyClass::MyVariableMexception Overflow(Overflow) raised.  raised at: <basis>/integer/int-inf.sml:line.col  iabc-cpp2if.sml:xxxx.yy...  This happens with code similar to the following example: class A{public:  static const unsigned int a = 0xFFFFFFFF;};  int main(){  unsigned int v = A::a;  v--;  return v;}

Summary: Stateflow may generate incorrect code for charts that contain loops with multiple entry and exit points
Description: Using the Stateflow graphical action language, you can create complex flow graphs that contain loops with multiple entry and exit points. Charts that contain these types of flow graphs may generate code that produces wrong answers in the following situations: When you simulate the chart with debugging and animation turned off When you generate code using Real-Time Workshop  The attached file, g436036_ebr.pdf, presents an example of a flow graph that produces this bug.
Workaround: Do not use control logic that contains multiple entry or exit points. For example, define control logic in Embedded MATLAB functions or by using standard constructs such as while loops and for loops.

Summary: Error message during code generation that refers to error in 'CGIR'
Description: When you try to generate code for a model that contains conditionally executed subsystems or Stateflow charts, you might encounter the following error message: Error building Real-Time Workshop target for block diagram 'mymodel'. MATLAB error message: Error using ==> rtwgen Unexpected or internal error encountered in "in CGIR: .\rtwcg_utils\rtwcg_tag.cpp line 938: getNodeRTWCGTag(startNode) == getNodeRTWCGTag(endNode) && getNodeRTWCGTag(startNode) == rtwTag
Workaround: To avoid this problem, clear the Include Comments check box in the Configuration Parameters > Real-Time Workshop > Comments pane.
Fixed In: R2007b web release, R2008a web release, R2008b

Summary: Large or long-running simulation may cause SimEvents solver error
Description: If your model includes any of the following SimEvents blocks and has many events, you might see an error message. FIFO Queue LIFO Queue Priority Queue Single Server N-Server Output Switch, with the Store entity before switching  option selected  Large models and long simulation durations can contribute to having many events. An example of the error message is as follows: Error occurred in 'mymodel/__DES_SL_IF__'. Error in SimEvents solver: Time of next hit, 1234.5678, returned byS-function 'vs_sl2des' in mymodel/__DES_SL_IF__/__vs_sfun__'during flag=4 call must be greater than previous timehit value of 1234.5678.
Workaround: To reduce the number of events during the simulation, reduce the Stop time parameter in the Configuration Parameters dialog to a value less than the time mentioned in the error message. If the resulting simulation duration is not sufficient to exercise your system adequately, then consider running many short simulations and combining the results.To learn how to run a simulation repeatedly and compute an ensemble average of a numerical result, see "Collecting Statistics from a Series of Simulations" in the SimEvents documentation. Enter the following in the MATLAB Command Window:web([docroot '/toolbox/simevents/ug/bp4wf77-1.html']); % R2007b documentation page

Summary: Permute Dimensions block might produce incorrect result when its input and output signal dimensions are identical
Description: The Permute Dimensions block might produce incorrect results when its input and output signal dimensions are identical. For example, suppose that you use the block to transpose the following matrix: 1 2 3 4 5 6 7 8 9 The block might incorrectly output 1 2 3 2 5 6 3 6 9 instead of the expected result 1 4 7 2 5 8 3 6 9
Workaround: To work around this issue, choose one of the following methods:Turn off the Signal storage reuse option on the Optimization pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box.Append artificial data to the matrix that the Permute Dimensions block receives, such that the block's input and output signal dimensions are different. At the output of the Permute Dimensions block, add a post-processing operation to remove the unwanted artificial data.For example, suppose you use the Permute Dimensions block to transpose a 3-by-3 matrix. Use a Matrix Concatenate block to append an extra column of artificial data to the block's input signal, generating a 3-by-4 matrix. After transposing this augmented matrix with the Permute Dimensions block, use a Selector block to remove the row of artificial data from the 4-by-3 matrix. The result is a correctly transposed 3-by-3 matrix.

Summary: applycform gives wrong answer when used with an 8-bit CLUT.
Description: If you have an 8-bit CLUT in an ICC profile and use it to transform colors, you will get incorrect results. For example, if swop8.icc  is a profile containing an 8-bit encoded CLUT, this code will lead to incorrect answers: p = iccread('swop8.icc');swop8cform = makecform('clut',swop8,'BToA0');applycform([50 0 0], swop8cform)
Workaround: To workaround this problem:At the MATLAB prompt, type the matlabroot command, and take note of the directory name returned. This directory will be referred to as ${MATLABROOT} in following steps.Quit MATLAB.Change directories into ${MATLABROOT}/toolbox/images/images/private.Make a backup copy of the file applyclut.m by copying it to applyclut.m.old.Download the attached file, extract applyclut.m, and store it in the current directory.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Generating code for a Math Function block with a 16-bit output signal might produce an error
Description: The Real-Time Workshop product might display an incorrect error message if you generate code for a model that contains a Math Function block whose Function  parameter specifies sqrt  and whose output signal specifies a 16-bit fixed-point or integer data type. For example, you encounter this error under all the following conditions: The output signal of the Math Function block specifies a word length of 16 bits, e.g., int16 . On the Hardware Implementation  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Number of bits  parameter specifies the following values: ? int  long  native word size   Number of bits  16 32 16     In this case, the Real-Time Workshop product displays an error message such as the following: Error using ==> rtwgenReal-Time Workshop can't generate code for this model because block'Math Function' is using the data type 'int32_T' which requires 32 bits,but the maximum supported for code generation is 32.  However, this message is erroneous because the Real-Time Workshop product should be able generate code in this situation.

Summary: Incorrect result: Generated code or simulation results for Bias block might be incorrect
Description: When you generate code or simulate an accelerated model, the generated code or simulation results for the Bias block might be incorrect under all of the following conditions: The input signal to the Bias block is a frame signal. The Signal storage reuse  parameter is selected.  The following is an example of an incorrect  code pattern you might generate for the attached model using the Real-Time Workshop R2007a product: for (i = 0; i < 3; i++) {  /* Bias: '<Root>/Bias1' */  tmp = (rtb_Bias1[inputIndexVar] + temp_P.Bias1_Bias[i]) +  temp_P.Bias1_Bias[i];  inputIndexVar = 3 * i + 2;  tmp += temp_P.Bias1_Bias[i];  rtb_Bias1[inputIndexVar] = tmp;  }
Workaround: Clear the Signal storage reuse check box in the Configuraiton Parameters > Optimization pane.

Summary: Corrects error in the Guided introduction demo for the Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder where the file "throttle.h" can not be found
Description: When trying to build parts of the Real-Time Workshop Guided Introduction an error message staying "unable to find 'throttle.h'" was displayed. The file could not be found because MATLAB was installed in a directory with spaces in the path, most commonly 'c:\Program Files\MATLAB\RXXXXX'
Workaround: The problem is due to spaces in the install directory path. To fix the problem take the following steps 1.) Open the model explorer 2.) Browse to the sfun target entry * Select the rtwdemo_PCG_Eval_P5 model in the model Hierarchy window * Then select the "sfun" icon in the "Contents of" pane 3.) In the Simulation Target window (right most pane) select Custom Code > Include Paths 4.) Modify the entry $matlabroot$\toolbox\rtw\rtwdemos\EmbeddedCoderOverview\ by adding double quotes around the path e.g. "$matlabroot$\toolbox\rtw\rtwdemos\EmbeddedCoderOverview\" 5.) Browse to the Real-Time Workshop configuration pane for the model 6.) Select Custom Code > Include Directories 7.) Modify the paths by adding double quotes around the paths e.g. $matlabroot$\toolbox\rtw\rtwdemos\EmbeddedCoderOverview\stage_5_files\ $matlabroot$\toolbox\rtw\rtwdemos\EmbeddedCoderOverview\ should read "$matlabroot$\toolbox\rtw\rtwdemos\EmbeddedCoderOverview\stage_5_files\" "$matlabroot$\toolbox\rtw\rtwdemos\EmbeddedCoderOverview\"

Summary: Certain Simulink blocks might throw segmentation violation when fed by discontiguous signals
Description: Simulink will throw a segmentation violation if the following conditions are met. 1. Discontiguous signals are fed into the Math Function, Gain, Prelookup, Interpolation using Prelookup, or Lookup Table n-D blocks. 2. Start simulation without first doing an Update Diagram.
Workaround: Update Diagram before starting a simulation.

Summary: xPC Target analog input (A/D) frame drivers for United Electronic Industries (UEI) boards do not work correctly in xPC Target version 3.3 (R2007b)
Description: xPC Target analog input (A/D) frame drivers for United Electronic Industries (UEI) boards do not work correctly. The following will occur when you use these driver blocks in R2007b: No buffer completion interrupts occur. You can start and stop the model, but nothing happens in the started state.
Workaround: As a workaround:Stop MATLAB.Download the attached file, powerdaq.h, to matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\target\build\xpcblocks\includeRestart MATLAB.Rebuild your model.This fix only applies to R2007b (xPC Target 3.3). The driver is only broken in this release.

Summary: makecform returns error with valid parametric grayscale TRC ICC profile.
Description: The makecform  function, when passed a parametric grayscale TRC ICC profile where the GrayTRC tag is in the form of a struct, returns an error claiming that the profile is invalid when the profile is actually valid. >> prof = iccread('GrayScale.icc');>> makecform('graytrc',prof,'Direction','Forward')??? Error using ==> makecform>make_graytrc_cform at 565GrayTRC structure is invalid. Error in ==> makecform at 145 c = make_graytrc_cform(varargin{2:end}); ?
Workaround: To workaround this issue, download the attached file, extract and copy the four files into your current MATLAB installation, as follows.At the MATLAB prompt, type the matlabroot command, and take note of the directory name returned. This directory will be referred to as ${MATLABROOT} in following steps.Quit MATLAB.Download and extract the four files contained in the attachment.Navigate into the ${MATLABROOT}/toolbox/images/images directory.Make back-up copies of the applycform and makecform functions. For example, rename the existing functions, adding ".old" to the filenames.Copy the updated versions of applycform and makecform, that you extracted from the attachment, and store them in the directory specified in the previous step.Navigate into the ${MATLABROOT}/toolbox/images/images/private directory.Make back-up copies of the applygraytrc_fwd and applygraytrc_inv functions. For example, rename the existing functions, adding ".old" to the filenames.Copy the updated versions of applygraytrc_fwd and applygraytrc_inv, that you extracted from the attachment, and store them in the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Model Discretizer tool might fail to load models
Description: Suppose you load a model in the Model Discretizer tool by selecting File > Load model  or clicking the Load model  button in the toolbar. The tool fails to load the specified model when all the following conditions apply: The full pathname of the model contains a space, e.g., c:\my files\vdp.mdl . The model is not accessible from the MATLAB search path. The model is not currently open.  In this case, the Model Discretizer tool displays the following error messages in the MATLAB Command Window: ??? Error using ==> cdToo many input arguments.??? Error using ==> getdiscdata at 26The model 'modelname' cannot be found, you must open the model first.
Workaround: To work around this issue, consider any of the following approaches:Eliminate all spaces from the full pathname of the model.Add the directory in which the model is located to the MATLAB search path.Open the model before loading it in the Model Discretizer tool.

Summary: PolySpace in One Click (POC) may not start if your "My Documents" folder (the personal folder of every Windows user) is located on a mounted drive.?
Description: PolySpace in One Click (POC) uses the "My Documents" folder (actually the variable USERPROFILE) to store log files. If this folder is not available, POC cannot start. ?
Workaround: To work around this issue, change the USERPROFILE to point to C:\WINNT\Profiles\username, by selectingControl panel > System >Advanced > Environment variables).

Summary: Simulation results may be incorrect in Rapid Accelerator mode when changes to the Solver Configuration block are made and the model is simulated again
Description: Simulation results may be incorrect when changes to the parameters for the Solver Configuration block are made and the model is simulated in the Rapid Accelerator mode. For example, if a model is simulated in Rapid Accelerator mode and the Start simulation from steady state check box  is not checked and then the model is simulated again and the Start simulation from steady state check box  is checked then the simulation results for the second simulation will be incorrect. The model incorrectly assumes that the target is up-to-date for the second simulation run and does not take into consideration the change in the check box. This applies to all parameters on the Solver Configuration block.

Summary: Block-to-code highlighting generates an error
Description: When you have enabled block-to-code highlighting, generated code, and try to trace from a block in a referenced model to the code for that block, the Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder software might generate an error. This occurs when the Model block for the referenced model is in a subsystem.

Summary: Unpacking logged time series data from a Simulink model with many events can take a long time initially
Description: Unpacking logged timeseries data from a ModelDataLogs object or using the time series tool for timeseries with many events can be slow. However, this effect is only noticeable the first time the timeseries are unpacked. It typically occurs when the model that generated it contains an enabled subsystem which experiences many enable/disable events.
Workaround: The attached file is valid for R2006b (MATLAB 6.5) to R2007b (MATLAB 7.0), inclusive.To work around this issue, replace the version of the initialize.m function in your current installation with the updated version attached, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file. matlabroot/toolbox/matlab/timeseries/@SimTimeseries. matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function.For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension, e.g., initialize.m.OLD.Download the attached file, initialize.m, storing it the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Using C++ and Stateflow library models with Embedded MATLAB functions may cause compilation error.
Description: Using C++ and Stateflow library models with Embedded MATLAB functions may cause a comilation error during simulation. This bug applies to Embedded MATLAB Function blocks in Simulink models and Embedded MATLAB functions in Stateflow charts. The error message looks something like this: c1_test_lib.cpp(594) : error C2664: 'c1_info_helper' : cannot convert parameter 1 from 'const mxArray *' to'SFc1_test_libInstanceStruct *'Types pointed to are unrelated; conversion requires reinterpret_cast, C-style cast or function-style cast  The exact text varies depending on your compiler and library model.
Workaround: Disable compiling MATLAB files on the path by typing this command at the MATLAB command line:sf('feature','EML MATLAB Path Support',0);?

Summary: Unnecessary start and terminate functions in generated code
Description: When a subsystem contains a System Outputs block, the Real-Time Workshop code generator might create unnecessary, empty start and terminate functions for the subsystem under the following conditions: The generated code of the subsystem is inlined (by setting the subsystem Real-Time Workshop system code  parameter to Inline , or Auto  and the Real-Time Workshop build process inlines the code). The subsystem is inside another subsystem whose generated code is not inlined.
Workaround: Set the subsystem Real-Time Workshop system code parameter to Function or Reusable function. This eliminates the unnecessary start and terminate functions. Note that the output function is no longer inlined.

Summary: DICOMINFO and DICOMREAD might fail reading files with floating-point data
Description: Attributes in DICOM files with value representations of "Other Float" (VR = OF) are either improperly read or empty. Attributes after the floating-point values may not be read at all.
Workaround: Customers running MATLAB R2007b can download a fix for this issue. After installing the fix, DICOMINFO will return the floating-point data for all attributes that contain it.Before updating, exit MATLAB. Customers on all platforms should download the attachment and extract dicominfo.m, back up the version that exists in $MATLAB/toolbox/images/medformats, and copy the extracted file into the directory.Then extract the updated dicomparse MEX-file for the platform you are running:dicomparse.mexa64 (Linux 64-bit)dicomparse.mexglx (Linux 32-bit)dicomparse.mexmaci (Macintosh)dicomparse.mexs64 (Solaris)dicomparse.mexw32 (Windows)Backup the original MEX-file in $MATLAB/toolbox/images/medformats/private and then copy the extracted file into the directory.Finally, restart MATLAB and issue the "rehash toolbox" command.

Summary: Simscape and SimHydraulics models configured for custom targets derived from non-malloc GRT incorrectly fail code generation
Description: For Simscape/SimHydraulics models, there are several conditions which must be met to succesfully generate code. If the target is based on non-malloc GRT, the model should succesfully generate code. A bug in Simulink prevents custom targets derived from non-malloc GRT from succesfully generating code.

Summary: Student Version: cannot save desktop layout
Description: In the Student Version of MATLAB?, when you select Desktop > Save Layout , the desktop is not saved and the following message appears in the Command Window: The desktop configuration was not saved successfully
Workaround: To resolve the problem, use the attached patch file that corresponds to your version of MATLAB, R2007a or R2007b. Download and unzip the patch file into the matlabroot/java/patch directory, where matlabroot is the directory in which MATLAB is installed, as determined by running the matlabroot function. After installing the patch, restart MATLAB.

Summary: When using deploytool, the build fails unless all toolboxes or no toolboxes are selected to be on the path.
Description: In deploytool , select a subset of toolboxes (not all) to be on the path and save the settings. Now, when the project is built, MATLAB Compiler will error out.
Workaround: Use the mcc command to compile the code in order to use a small subset of toolboxes on the path. When using deploytool, select all toolboxes to be on the path.

Summary: Microsoft Visual C++ 8.0 compiler: custom compiler optimization flags fail to override /fp:precise
Description: By default, the Real-Time Workshop template makefiles (TMFs) for the Microsoft Visual C++ 8.0 compiler specify the floating-point optimization flag value /fp:precise . If you use the Compiler optimization level  parameter on the Real-Time Workshop  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box to specify custom compiler optimization flags, the custom flags fail to override /fp:precise .
Workaround: To work around this bug, edit matlabroot/rtw/c/tools/vctools.mak (where matlabroot represents the root of your MATLAB directory tree, as returned by the command matlabroot). Change /fp:precise to the desired setting.

Summary: Function dted errors if padding follows elevation records
Description: We have come across at least one instance of a DTED (.dtN ) file which was padded to make the number of bytes it contains evenly divisible by 256. Files such as this lead to an error in function dted , originating from function readmtx .
Workaround: To work around this issue, replace the dted.m function file in your installation with the version in the attachment, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file.matlabroot/toolbox/map/mapformats/dted.mmatlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function.For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension, dted.m.OLD.Copy the version of dted.m from the attachment into the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Extract Bits block generates error when its parameters specify workspace variables
Description: Suppose your model contains an Extract Bits block whose Bits to extract  parameter specifies any of the following values: Range starting with most significant bit  Range ending with least significant bit  Range of bits   Subsequently, if you specify the value of either the Number of bits  or the Bit indices  parameter as a workspace variable and simulate the model, Simulink displays an error similar to the following: Error in 'modelname/Extract Bits': Initialization commands cannot be evaluated.MATLAB error message: Error using ==> evalUndefined function or variable 'a'.
Workaround: To work around this issue, do not specify the value of the block's Number of bits or Bit indices parameters as workspace variables.

Summary: Using INPUTDLG with instrument control interfaces may cause MATLAB to become unresponsive
Description: When you use the INPUTDLG function as shown, MATLAB may become unresponsive: >> answer = inputdlg('MATLAB will freeze')  >> s = serial('COM1')  >> fclose(s)  >> delete(s)
Workaround: One workaround is to pause after receiving the input:>> answer = inputdlg('MATLAB will freeze')>> pause;>> s = serial('COM1')>> fclose(s)>> delete(s)

Summary: Reuters.fetch returns an error when retrieving data from Reuters? with fields that begin with numbers
Description: Datafeed Toolbox? software is unable to correctly retrieve data from Reuters? if a field name starts with a number.
Workaround: To work around this issue, download the attached version of the MATLAB file. Here are the steps:1. Find the $MATLABROOT directory where $MATLABROOT is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by typingmatlabrootat the MATLAB? Command Prompt.2. Quit MATLAB.3. Rename the following MATLAB file:$MATLABROOT/toolbox/datafeed/datafeed/@reuters/fetch.m.Rename the file to fetch.m.old4. Download the attached file(s). Place the new file(s) in the same directory mentioned in step 3.5. Restart MATLAB6. After restarting MATLAB, issue the following command at the MATLAB Command Prompt:rehash toolboxcache

Summary: Simulink on Windows may crash in _MSW_DeleteBlockToolTip
Description: The MathWorks has been receiving reports of crashes on Windows with the following stack track: Stack Trace:   [0] libmwsimulink.dll:_MSW_DeleteBlockToolTip(0x0f6ef720, 0x022d8020, 0x022d8020, 0x00d0c8fc "D??????") + 131 bytes This is most likely the result of an interaction with block tool tips, but has not been reproduced by the MathWorks. If you experience a crash that starts with the above stack trace, you may be encountering this bug. If you can reproduce the problem on your system, please report it along with the circumstances under which it occurs.

Summary: MATLAB files created on Mac platform and later moved to a PC cannot be compiled by the MATLAB Compiler.
Description: MATLAB Compiler appears to discard all code after the first comment if the MATLAB file was originally created on a Mac and then moved to a PC. If the file contains multi-line comments, the MATLAB Compiler may crash when processing the file.
Workaround:
After first backing up your file, replace the  line terminators in the file with either 
 or 
 as in this Perl code:perl -e 's%%
%;' -p < originalFile.m > fixedFile.m It is also sometimes possible to workaround this problem simply by opening the file in an editor and saving it again, with the same name.?

Summary: Plots of two-stage models using local linear models are incorrect
Description: The line plots for two-stage models that use local linear models at the local and response nodes are incorrect. The evaluation of data points in the plots is correct. The normal model evaluations are correct and the models can be reliably used for optimization.
Workaround: If you are using Release R2007b, you can correct the the problem by downloading the attached file and restarting MATLAB.Copy the file to this directory:<matlabroot>/toolbox/mbc/mbcmodels/@xregtwostage

Summary: regionprops crashes MATLAB if the input label matrix is sparse.
Description: MATLAB exits abruptly if a user calls regionprops  with a sparse matrix.
Workaround: To workaround this issue, download the updated version of regionprops.m, contained in the attached file:At the MATLAB prompt, type the matlabroot command and take note of the directory returned. This directory will be referred to as ${MATLABROOT} in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Change directories into ${MATLABROOT}/toolbox/images/imagesCreate a back-up copy of the regionprops.m file, for example, calling it regionprops.m.old.Download the attached file, extract regionprops.m and copy it into the current directory.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Blocks in subsystems with hierarchical resolution disabled incorrectly see base workspace variables.
Description: When a subsystem disables hierarchical resolution, blocks inside it could still resolve a bus object name that happens to be the name of a data type variable defined in the base workspace.

Summary: Invalid connect code generated in the MDD file
Description: When you call MAKEMID using the instrument specific interface, class compliant interfaces may be included. This can generate invalid connect code in the MDD file. This is known to occur in these drivers: AgilentRFPowerMeter Agilent546XX AgilentInfiniim  However, it could affect other drivers as well.
Workaround: Comment out or remove the class compliant initialization code in the MDD-driver and do not use the class compliant groups in the driver.For example in the MDD-file generated from the AgilentRFPowerMeter driver, the last section of code under Initialization and Cleanup->Connect in MIDEDIT needs to be removed. For example you could follow these steps:Open the driver in MIDEDIT: midedit AgilentRFPowerMeter.mddSelect the "Connect" tab on the "Initialization and Cleanup" page.Comment out the following code:% collection = comobj;% collection = get(collection, 'IviPwrMeter');% collection = get(collection, 'Channels');% instrgate('privateIviComDriverHelper', 'group', obj, collection, 'Ivipwrmeterchannels');?

Summary: MATLAB might crash when generating code using custom target derived from grt_malloc.tlc
Description: MATLAB might crash when you generate code using a custom target that you derived from grt_malloc.tlc using: rtwgensettings.DerviedFrom = 'grt_malloc.tlc';  A typical stack trace might point to a memory fault in utility routines: Stack Trace:  [0] udd_mi.dll:public: __thiscall PropValuePair::PropValuePair(char const *)(0x2c569010 "dddddddddddddddddddddddddddddddd..", 0x0bdf7310, 0x00cece8c "<?", 0x79740c0e) + 36 bytes  [1] udd_mi.dll:public: __thiscall SimplePropValuePair::SimplePropValuePair(char const *,struct mxArray_tag const *)(0x2c569010 "dddddddddddddddddddddddddddddddd..", 0x0bdf0b20, 0x18e2ef2f, 0x1ff916b0) + 15 bytes  [2] udd_mi.dll:class Vector * __cdecl ParsePropValuePairs(int,int,struct mxArray_tag const * *,bool)(1, 1, 0x18e2f59f, 15) + 1166 bytes  [3] udd_mi.dll:void __cdecl mudiSet(int *,struct mxArray_tag * *,int,struct mxArray_tag const * *)(0x00ced540, 15, 0x18e2f5d7, 0x09cb1438) + 515 bytes  [4] udd_mi.dll:public: virtual void __thiscall SetMI::invokeMethod(class UDMethodSignature *,int *,void * *,int,void * *)(0x09c35d80, 0x00ced540, 0x00ced508, 0) + 171 bytes  [5] udd.dll:public: virtual void __thiscall UDMethodInfo::invokeMethodE(class UDMethodSignature *,int *,void * *,int,void * *,class UDErrorStatus *)(0x09c35d80, 0x00ced540, 0x00ced508, 15) + 77 bytes

Summary: Block data type parameters with case-insensitive equivalents evaluate to wrong data types
Description: When the data type parameter of a Simulink block is either an inheritance rule or a built-in type, Simulink treats the data type string case-insensitively. This behavior incorrectly implies that data type variables such as UINT8 cannot be used.

Summary: Printing many Simulink models in succession causes MATLAB to run out of memory
Description: Printing many Simulink models  to an image or printer in a MATLAB session causes MATLAB to run out of memory.

Summary: MCR captures output to System.out and System.err, resulting in unpredicatable behavior when using a MATLAB Builder JA component.
Description: The first MATLAB Builder JA component loaded into a Java application swaps out the Java runtime's System.out  and System.err  objects and installs its own output handlers. These replacement output streams route output through the MCR's thread, which eventually sends the output to the original output streams. This behavior can cause unintended side-effects when a MATLAB Builder JA component is used inside a Java application.

Description: hdfread  cannot read a Vdata dataset that is contained in a Vgroup. Vdata datasets in the root group can still be read, however.

Extract the files from the .zip file. ????????????? ???????????????????? Windows Systems:?????????????????????????? ????????????????????????????????? Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: houghlines fails to find clearly visible line in certain input matrices
Description: The houghlines  function fails to find a clearly visible line segment in certain input matrices.
Workaround: To work around this issue, replace the houghlines.m file in your current MATLAB installation with the updated version contained in the attached file.At the MATLAB prompt, type the matlabroot command and take note of the directory returned. This directory will be referred to as ${MATLABROOT} in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Change directories into ${MATLABROOT}/toolbox/images/images.Create a back-up copy of the houghlines.m file. For example, rename the file houghlines.m.old.Download the attached file, extract houghlines.m and copy it into the current directory.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: mclcppclass.h and mclmcr.h files are missing in the MCR 77 directory on Linux systems.
Description: After running MCRInstaller.bin  on Linux systems to install MCR 7.7, in folder v77/extern/include , I find ten files, but mclcppclass.h  and mclmcr.h  are missing. These two files are required to compile C++ shared libraries.
Workaround: Copy mclcppclass.h and mclmcr.h from MATLAB installation directory matlab/extern/include to MCR installation directory v77/extern/include.

Summary: Some Image Processing Toolbox functions cannot be deployed.
Description: If you try to use MATLAB Compiler to deploy code that depends on Image Processing Toolbox functions in toolbox/shared/imageslib , you won't have access to the shared functionality in the distribution package.

Summary: hough does not respect the 'RhoResolution' setting
Description: The hough  function does not respect the default 'RhoResolution'  or any 'RhoResolution'  value that you specify. For example, if you call hough  without specifying the 'RhoResolution'  parameter, as in the following: BW = false(4,5);[H, theta, rho] = hough(BW, 'ThetaResolution', 5) hough  returns rho  values with a spacing of 1.25, instead of 1 (the default value). If you specify the 'RhoResolution'  parameter, as in the following: [H, theta, rho] = hough(BW, 'ThetaResolution', 5, 'RhoResolution', 2) hough  returns rho  values with a spacing of 3, instead of 2. The difference between the expected and actual rho resolution decreases as the image size increases.
Workaround: To workaround this issue download the updated version of the hough function, contained in the attached file.At the MATLAB prompt, type the matlabroot command and take note of the directory returned. This directory will be referred to as ${MATLABROOT} in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Change directories into ${MATLABROOT}/toolbox/images/images.Create a back-up copy of the hough.m file, such as hough.m.old.Download the attachment and extract the updated version of the hough.m file into the current directory.Restart MATLAB.With this fix, the Standard Hough Transform returned by hough will have a different size and hence slightly different results than in versions prior to R2008a. The optional output argument, RHO, will also be different (values and length). The differences decrease as the image gets bigger.You can reproduce old results by using the getOldRhoResolution function and explicitly passing the old rho resolution to the hough function.Download the attachment and extract the getOldRhoResolution function, attached. This function takes the size of the image and the expected RhoResolution as inputs and returns the actual rho resolution used by hough.Pass the results of this function into hough. For example, if you ran the following code in MATLAB R2007b: BW = false(4,5);[H, theta, rho] = hough(BW, 'ThetaResolution', 5)You can reproduce these results using the updated hough function, as follows: BW = false(4,5);[H, theta, rho] = hough(BW, 'ThetaResolution', 5, 'RhoResolution', getRhoResolution(size(BW), 1))?

Summary: The Amplifier block ignores the value of the Initial seed parameter
Description: When adding noise, the Amplifier block (in the Mathematical library) is supposed to randomize the noise sequence using the Initial seed  parameter that you specify in the block dialog box. The block currently ignores this parameter and instead initializes the noise sequence with a seed value of 1. Hence, the block always adds the same noise (to within a scaling factor to reflect the specified noise temperature, figure, or factor) no matter what seed you specify. Multiple instances of this block will therefore produce correlated noise sequences that reinforce each other.

Summary: Simulating referenced models that use Stateflow library charts incorrectly requires Stateflow Coder license
Description: When you use Stateflow library charts in referenced models, a Stateflow Coder license is checked out unnecessarily for the Model Reference simulation target. If you do not have a Stateflow Coder license, you receive an error.

Summary: The "Curve Fitting for an Error Rate Plot" documentation example produces bad results
Description: The code used in the "Curve Fitting for an Error Rate Plot" documentation example produces results that are different from what is shown in the screen capture of the final output.

Summary: Code generation report not reporting nonvirtual subsystems in model
Description: The code generation report might not report nonvirtual subsystems in a model and their mapping to functions in generated code when the model references another model via a model reference block.

Summary: Model Advisor Check "Identify blocks that generate expensive saturation and rounding code" errors out on subsytem of a fixed-point model.
Description: This error occurs due to an internal error in how this check operates on subsystems.

Summary: Error in function geoshow when plotting a scalar point
Description: If you pass a pair of scalar latitude-longitude values to function geoshow  in which the latitude value happens to be 0 or the value of an open figure window handle, and you do not specify a map axes as the first argument to geoshow , an unexpected error results. The error message complains about the handle type of the first input, even though you intended geoshow  to interpret that input as a latitude, not as a handle. For example, the commands lat = 0;lon = 30;geoshow(lat,lon,'Color','r')  result in an error message that says: Function GEOSHOW expected its first input to be 'axes' handle type. Instead the handle type is 'root'. "
Workaround: You can work around this problem by specifying a map axes handle as the first argument to geoshow. For example,lat = 0;lon = 30;geoshow(gca,lat,lon,'Color','r')

Summary: Methods of some MATLAB classes limit the number of inputs and outputs to 50 parameters.
Description: Some classes limit the number of arguments to 50 inputs and can return no more than 50 outputs. The Statistics Toolbox dataset  class is an example of a MATLAB class with this limitation.

Summary: Unnecessary rounding operations in function limitm reduce accuracy and consistency
Description: After calculating the latitude and longitude limits of the geographic quadrangle bounding a regular data grid, function limitm  arbitrarily rounds those limits to the nearest one millionth of a degree (roughly 10 centimeters in latitude or equatorial longitude). Although it is small, this rounding operation in effect applies an arbitrary shift to points on or very near the edge of the grid. The direction of the shift, in addition to its magnitude, is arbitrary because rounding can either increase or decrease a number's value. What happens in any given case depends on the specific referencing vector and the number of columns and rows in the data grid. The result is an unnecessary degradation in the numerical accuracy of limitm  and the set of functions that depend on it, which include most of the functions that accept regular data grids referenced to latitude-longitude. It can also lead to inconsistencies with respect to independently-formulated computations. See Bug Report 173338  regarding a similar problem in function setpostn .
Workaround: To work around this issue, replace the limitm.m function file in your current installation with the version in the attachment, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file. matlabroot/toolbox/map/map/limitm.mmatlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function.For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension, limitm.m.OLD.Copy the version of limitm.m from the attachment into the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Incorrect error message about evaluation of data initial value appears in cases when data initial value is not needed.
Description: In some cases, an incorrect error message about evaluation of data initial value appears when data initial value is not needed. For example, chart input data does not need an initial value. In this case, the Initial value  in the Data properties dialog is disabled (or grayed out). However, the following error message appears: "Expression '[2 3]' for initial value of data 'input' (#32) must evaluate to a scalar." ?
Workaround: Follow these steps:1) Open the Data properties dialog.2) In the General pane, change a data property so that an initial value is required and the Initial value field in the Data properties dialog is enabled. For example, change the Scope to 'Local'.3) Click the Value Attributes tab.4) Clear the string in the Initial valuefield.5) Click Apply.6) Change the data property in step 2 back to the desired setting. For example, change the Scope back to the original setting.

Summary: To Workspace block leaks memory if a memory allocation error occurs
Description: The To Workspace block stores signal data in memory during a simulation. During simulations that run for a long time, or use very wide signals, Simulink may not be able to allocate the required memory. In such a case, Simulink displays an error message, terminates the simulation, and frees resources allocated during the simulation. Prior to Version 7.1 (Release R2008a) the To Workspace block did not handle the memory allocation error correctly, and failed to free the resources that it had allocated. As a result, each subsequent simulation ran out of memory in less time.

Summary: Generated code might crash due to excessive memory usage
Description: When you use a Simulink Math Function block to perform a transpose or hermitian function, the Real-Time Workshop code generator creates an unnecessary local variable that uses a lot of memory, and the generated code might crash when you execute it.

Summary: Simulink will crash if the consecutive number of zero crossings report contains strings longer than 2048 characters.?
Description: The error report that is generated when the number of consecutive zero crossings allowed threshold is exceeded contains a list of blocks, which includes the path names. If any of the strings exceeds 2048 characters in length, Simulink will crash.

Summary: Phase Delay plot incorrect in FDATool and phasedelay command line function displays incorrect units
Description: The Phase Delay plot in FDATool displays incorrect data. The phasedelay  function does not display the correct units. It always returns the phase delay scaled to samples, even if you supply a sampling frequency.

Summary: The 'Minimize algebraic loop' diagnostic does not work properly when it is set to 'error'.
Description: After setting the 'Minimize algebraic loop' diagnostic to 'error', this diagnostic fails to report an error.

Summary: GETBLAST gives an error when using the RTOE output from BLASTNCBI
Description: The second output from BLASTNCBI, request time of execution (RTOE), is returned as a character array. If you use this value directly in the 'WaitTime' parameter of GETBLAST you will receive the following error: ??? Error using ==> getblast at 132 The WAIT value must be a positive, real number.  This is because the output RTOE from BLASTNCBI is a string, while GETBLAST is expecting a numeric value.
Workaround: Use the command str2double to convert the RTOE string returned from BLASTNCBI to a numeric value.Example:Change this:[RID,rtoe]=blastncbi(Seq, Program, 'Database', 'est_human', 'Alignments', 75); report_human=getblast(RID,'Alignments',10,'ToFile','reporthuman.html','WaitTime',rtoe,'FileFormat','html');to:[RID,rtoe]=blastncbi(Seq, Program, 'Database', 'est_human', 'Alignments', 75);report_human=getblast(RID,'Alignments',10,'ToFile','reporthuman.html','WaitTime',str2double(rtoe),'FileFormat','html');

Summary: sprintf function produces incorrect results when used with '%0Ns' format, where N is a positive integer.
Description: The following command should produce the string '00000abc ' :     y = sprintf('%08s', 'abc')   Instead, it produces ' abc' .

Summary: Segmentation violation in function geoshow in rare cases involving polygon display
Description: Function geoshow  generates a segmentation violation for certain geographic polygons, due to numerical problems in an underlying algorithm that is used by the display function. The failure is relatively rare and sporadic. No generic workaround exists for this issue.

Summary: Saving and then loading an inputParser object causes a segmentation violation.
Description: There is a bug in MATLAB? that causes it to crash if you attempt to load a MAT-file that contains an inputParser  object. The bug also causes MATLAB? to crash if you attempt to examine the contents of a MAT-file containing an inputParser  object using the whos -file  command.

Summary: Pulse Generator block may generate incorrect output in a Normal mode referenced model
Description: A Pulse Generator block in a referenced model may generate incorrect output if the referenced model executes in Normal mode and the Pulse Generator block has a discrete sample time.

Summary: TeX string in print-frame not printed correctly while printing a model with a frame
Description: Specifying TeX string on printframe editor is not honored while printing.

Summary: Simulink Design Verifier may erroneously check out a MATLAB Report Generator concurrent license.
Description: Simulink Design Verifier may erroneously check out a MATLAB Report Generator concurrent license.
Workaround: No workarounds exist at this time.

Summary: MATLAB Report Generator incorrectly generates MATLAB-code when a given property has a value of "0".
Description: MATLAB Report Generator incorrectly generates MATLAB-code when a given property has a value of "0".
Workaround: To work around this issue:1. Rename the file $MATLAB/toolbox/matlab/codetools/@codegen/@momento/momento.m to momento.old,where $MATLAB is the root directory of your MATLAB software installation, as shown by running the following command at the MATLAB command prompt.:matlabroot2. Copy the attached momento.m file to $MATLAB/toolbox/matlab/codetools/@codegen/@momento.3) Run the following command at the MATLAB command prompt:rehash toolboxcache

Summary: Interface Elements library icon not displayed correctly in SimDriveline library
Description: If SimMechanics is not installed, in addition to SimDriveline, the icon for the Interface Elements library is not displayed correctly.
Workaround: 1. Close all block diagrams in your MATLAB session by entering the command bdclose all.2. To retain a copy of the current SimDriveline library before replacing it, rename the library file $MATLABROOT/toolbox/physmod/drive/drive/driveline.mdl to $MATLABROOT/toolbox/physmod/drive/drive/driveline.mdl.bak.3. Download the attached file driveline.mdl, and place it in the directory $MATLABROOT/toolbox/physmod/drive/drive/.4. Close and restart MATLAB.

Summary: Editing a MATLAB-File with an illegal assignment statement can crash MATLAB.
Description: Certain illegal left-hand sides of assignments in MATLAB programs cause a segmentation violation when the editor is invoked with M-Lint, causing a MATLAB crash. The simplest such case is [] = foo;
Workaround: Correcting the syntax error fixes the problem. Or M-Lint can be disabled in the editor.

Summary: PM Synchronous Machine does not honour number of poles setting in SimPowerSystems 4.4
Description: The PM Synchronous Machine always reverts to 2 poles regardless of what user specifies (for a trapezoidal flux distribution).
Workaround: 1. Open the block dialog.2. Set Pole pairs to the desired number, click Apply.3. Change the Mechanical input from Speed w to Torque Tm, click Apply.4. Change back the Mechanical input to Speed w, click Apply, save your model.

Summary: Incorrect code generation: Code genenerated for C MEX S-function by the Legacy Code Tool fails to compile
Description: For a function specification that you declare in the Legacy Code Tool, the generated C MEX S-function code fails to compile if all of the following conditions exist: The data type of a function argument is specified as a Simulink.Bus object. This Simulink.Bus object contains a Simulink.BusElement which specifies its data type as a Simulink.NumericType. The Simulink.NumericType IsAlias  property is set to 0 .
Workaround: There are 2 workarounds:Option 1: Specify the Simulink.BusElement data type with the corresponding built-in data type instead of the Simulink.NumericType.Option 2: Make the Simulink.NumericType an alias type. You must make the following modifications:Add the Simulink.NumericType typedef declaration in your legacy header file.Set the numeric type IsAlias property to 1.Set the numeric type HeaderFile property to the name of your legacy header file.

Summary: Simulating a model containing an Embedded MATLAB Function block that invokes syntactically-incorrect external MATLAB-files may cause MATLAB to crash
Description: MATLAB may crash during simulation of models containing Embedded MATLAB Function blocks that call external MATLAB-functions if any of the MATLAB-functions contains a syntax error. In rare circumstances, the syntax error is not handled correctly and MATLAB crashes or prints a misleading message: "Embedded MATLAB Interface Error: Error calling MATLAB function 'sf'" This bug does not occur when you declare external MATLAB-functions to be extrinsic in your Embedded MATLAB function.
Workaround: Fix syntax errors before trying to invoke MATLAB-files in Embedded MATLAB functions. Call each of your external MATLAB-files from MATLAB using dummy inputs. When these invocations run without error, your model should simulate correctly.

Summary: The Buffer block does not always back propagate signal dimensions correctly
Description: The Buffer block does not always back propagate signal dimensions optimally and can cause a propagation error. You might encounter this situation when you place a Buffer block in a feedback loop and do not specify the input dimensions to the block.
Workaround: To work around this issue, specify the desired signal dimension to the Buffer block by inserting the Simulink Signal Specification block before the Buffer block in your model.

Summary: Error in spoll with Agilent GPIB adaptor if instrument sets bit 7
Description: If an instrument sets bit 7 of the status byte during an spoll , the following error occurs: ??? Error using ==> gpib.spoll at 104GPIB: AGILENT: A gpib error occurred.
Workaround: To work around the issue, download and install the attached patch:Type matlabroot in the MATLAB Command Window and note the directory returned.Close MATLAB.In Windows Explorer navigate to the <matlabroot>toolbox\instrument\instrumentadaptors\win32 directory where <matlabroot> is the directory returned in step 1.Rename the existing mwagilentgpib.dll to mwagilentgpib.bakDownload the attached mwagilentgpib.dll file to this directory.Restart MATLAB.The attached patch should resolve the issue.

Summary: Sign error in polyconstd projection at the Equator
Description: For points with a latitude of precisely zero, the Y-value of the map coordinates computed by the standard polyconic (polyconstd) projection has the wrong sign. (Only Y is affected; the X-value is correct.)
Workaround: Change the sign of the right-hand side of the expression in line 85 in file MATLABROOT/toolbox/map/mapproj/polyconstd.m to be negative. This line should read:y(indx1) = -M0;

Summary: MATLAB crashes when clear mex command executed
Description: MATLAB might crash when the clear mex  command is executed after the emlc  function is used to generate C code and a compilation report. For example, the executing the following commands cause MATLAB to crash: >> emlc -report foo >> clear mex
Workaround: If you have the Simulink product installed, load Simulink, for example by opening the Simulink Library Browser, before executing the emlc function.

Description: Using the linmodv5 function to linearize a model containing normal mode model references to another model results in the following error: Not enough input arguments. Error in ==> linmodv5>local_pop_context at 316 set_param(models{ct}, prop, old.(prop));

Description: If a signal is defined using Simulink.AliasType , and the signal is saved in the Signal logging  object specified in the Configuration Parameters > Data Import/Export  pane, the saved signal always has data type double . The signal should be saved in the BaseType  specified in the Simulink.AliasType , which may or may not be double . ?

Summary: Wrapping, interpolation, and units issues in function ltln2val
Description: Function ltln2val  exhibits the following problems: 1. Longitude wrapping isn't handled correctly except when using interpolation method 'nearest' . With 'bilinear'  or 'bicubic' , unless input longitudes are "pre-wrapped" to match the longitude value given in the referencing vector, values of NaN are returned instead of correctly interpolated values. 2. Except when using interpolation method 'nearest' , a value of NaN is returned for points within half a cell width of the northern or eastern edge of the data grid, instead of correctly interpolated value. 3. The function only works properly when all inputs are in degrees, but this condition is not documented.
Workaround: You can download and use an updated version of function ltln2val, but it depends on the update to setpostn that is available from Bug Report 173338 so, if you haven't yet done so, make sure to start by following workaround in that bug report.To work around this issue, replace the ltln2val.m function file in your current installation with version in the attachment, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file. matlabroot/toolbox/map/mapdisp/ltln2val.mmatlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function.For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension, ltln2val.m.OLD.Copy the version of ltln2val.m from the attachment into the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: I recieve a buffer underwrite error with the Analog Output subsystem, using the National Instruments DAQCARD-6062E
Description: When generating analog output data with a National Instruments DAQCARD-6062E and this code: ao=analogoutput('nidaq','Dev1'); % Dev1 is a DAQCARD-6062Eaddchannel(ao,0);putdata(ao,zeros(3000,1));start(ao)Error event occurred at 16:43:29 for the object: nidaqmxdev1-AO.  I get this error: NIDAQmx error : Output buffer underwrite. Your application was unable to write samples to the background buffer fast enough for the device to get new samples at the specified sample rate.  To avoid this error, you can do any the following:1. Increase the size of the background buffer by configuring the buffer.2. Increase the number of samples you write each time you invoke a write operation.3. Write samples more often.4. Reduce your sample rate.5. Change the data transfer mechanism from interrupts to DMA.6. Initially write a sufficient number of samples to satisfy the specified data transfer request condition.7. Reduce the number of applications that your computer is executing concurrently.  In addition, if you do not need to ensure that each sample is generated once and only once, you can set the regeneration mode to allow regeneration.Task Name: MWDAT0004  Status Code: -200166
Workaround: To prevent this error, change the BufferingConfig property as shown:ao.BufferingConfig = [BUFSIZE, NUMBUFFERS];Where BUFSIZE times NUMBUFFERS is larger than the size of the data queued to the device with the putdata command. You may have to try several combinations to find the correct one, but start by examining the value of ao.BufferingConfig after the call to putdata. This will show you the default BUFSIZE and NUMBUFFERS. For a first attempt double the BUFSIZE. For example, in the code above in which 3000 samples are queued we see:>> ao.BufferingConfigans = 64 47>> ao.BufferingConfig = [ 128 47]; % double the default buffer sizeThese values prevent the error seen above.?

Summary: dicominfo errors on DICOM files containing improperly formatted names of people
Description: When a DICOM file contains "person name" data with more than five parts (such as "Family Name," "Given Name," etc.), dicominfo  can issue the following error: Index exceeds matrix dimensions
Workaround: Make a backup of the file dicominfo.m located in toolbox/images/medformats. Then find the the lines that look like this, which should be around line 294:        for q = 1:length(componentParts)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      personName(p).(pnParts{q}) = componentParts{q};                                                                                                                                                                                                               end                                                                                                                      Replace the lines with the following code:        % The DICOM standard requires that PN values have five or fewer                                                                  % values separated by "^".  Some vendors produce files with more                                                                 % than these person name parts.                                                                                                  if (numel(componentParts) <= 5)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       % If there are the correct numbers, put them in separate fields.                                                                 for q = 1:length(componentParts)                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      personName(p).(pnParts{q}) = componentParts{q};                                                                                                                                                                                                               end                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           else                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  % If there are more, just return the whole string.                                                                               personName(p).FamilyName = people{p};                                                                                                                                                                                                                         end

Summary: Illegal rate transition not detected between block and conditionally executed subsystem containing Unit Delay or Zero-Order Hold block
Description: The Simulink software fails to detect and flag an illegal rate transition when all of the following conditions are true: A block feeds or is fed by a conditionally executed subsystem in which all blocks have triggered sample time (that is, a triggered subsystem, an iterator subsystem, a triggered and enabled subsystem, or a function call subsystem) The block sample time differs from the subsystem sample time The block is connected to a Unit Delay or Zero-Order Hold block inside the subsystem (for example, a Gain block at the top level of a model is fed by a Unit Delay block inside a triggered subsystem)
Workaround: To work around this bug, avoid directly connecting a Unit Delay block or Zero-Order Hold block inside a triggered subsystem, iterator subsystem, triggered and enabled subsystem, or function call subsystem to a block outside the subsystem. Consider adding nonvirtual blocks before and after the Unit Delay or Zero-Order Hold block in the subsystem.

Summary: Conditions that contain references to non-scalar bus members sometimes cause assertion failures during simulation
Description: Stateflow may generate an assertion failure during simulation under the following conditions: Chart contains data of bus type Bus contains a non-scalar field Chart contains a condition on a transition Condition references one of the elements of the non-scalar bus field in a boolean context  For example, if the bus data name is "x ", the bus has a vector field named "f ", and the condition is [x.f[i]] , then you will see an assertion failure that looks like this: Stack Trace: [0] sf.mexw32:0x1d553dc9(0x1d5b7dbc "actualSource!=NULL", 0x1d5b7c50 "B:\matlab\toolbox\stateflow\src\..", 182, 0) [1] sf.mexw32:0x1d4923b3(0x5ec78468, 0, 0x2278ec38, 0x19cd59a8) [2] sf.mexw32:0x1d492fc6(0x1dce3468, 0xabce5f61, 0x1d3cad68, 0x16785e38) [3] sf.mexw32:0x1d4930e0(0x1d3cad68, 0x16785e38, 0x16785e38, 0x16785e38) [4] sf.mexw32:0x1d48a668(569, 0x16785e38, 0x1d3cad68, 569) [5] sf.mexw32:0x1d480fb4(569, 0, 0, 0) [6] sf.mexw32:0x1d43bdb5(0, 0x00ce2358, 1, 0x00ce23c0) [7] sf.mexw32:0x1d43d467(45, 0, 0x00ce2358, 2) [8] sf.mexw32:0x1d55a4d9(0, 0x00ce2358, 3, 0x00ce23b8)
Workaround: Compare the expression to zero instead of using an explicit boolean expression. For instance, change the condition in the above example as follows:[x.f[i]!=0]

Summary: Real-Time Workshop software generates noncompileable code for Saturation blocks
Description: The Real-Time Workshop software might generate noncompileable code for Saturation blocks under all of the following conditions: The output of the Saturation block is connected to one destination. The output signal of the Saturation block is not a test point. The data type of the input signal to the Saturation block is fixed-point.
Workaround: Do any of the following:Connect the output signal of the Saturation block to multiple destinations.Add a test point to the output signal of the Saturation block.Change the data type of the input signal of the Saturation block to a non-fixed-point data type.

Summary: Linearization I/O ignored for Signal Conversion and Bus Assignment blocks on bus signals
Description: When a linearization I/O is specified on the output port of a Signal Conversion block or a Bus Assignment block that is attached to a bus signal, the I/O is not included in the linearization results.

Summary: Relational blocks give wrong answer when comparing to NaN during simulation.
Description: When the output data type is not Boolean, the relational blocks (Relational Operator, Compare to Constant) give a wrong answer when comparing the output to NaN.
Workaround: Set the output data type to Boolean.

Summary: Code generation for DM643x processor fails with CACHE_16KCACHE undefined error
Description: When you generate code for a DM643x series processor, and you set the L1D or L1P cache size to 0, 4, 8, or 16 Kb, the code generation process fails with a build error similar to the following: "MW_c6xxx_csl.c", line 208: error: identifier "CACHE_16KCACHE" is undefined1 error detected in the compilation of "MW_c6xxx_csl.c". This build error is caused by the software using an incorrect enumerated type identifier, CACHE_<%d>KCACHE , to set the L1 cache size. The correct enumerated type is CACHE_L1_<%d>KCACHE .
Workaround: Download the attached ZIP archive patch_416478.zip and the patch installer patch_installer.m. Start MATLAB and enter the following command at the command prompt to install the patch:patch_installer('patch_416478.zip', 'install')Follow the instructions displayed on the MATLAB command line to install the patch. The patch modifies the following file in your MATLAB installation&toolbox\rtw\targets\ccslink\ccslink\@CCSLinkTgtPkg\@TMS320C643x\generateCacheFunctions.pto use the CACHE_L1_<%d>KCACHE type indentifier to set the cache size.

Summary: Closing the To Video Display block during a simulation can cause MATLAB to hang
Description: Closing a display window opened by the To Video Display block during simulation can cause MATLAB to hang. Note: This problem occurs only if graphics hardware acceleration is turned off. ?
Workaround: For MATLAB R2007b, do the following:1) Rename $MATLABROOT/bin/$ARCH/SLDirectShow.dll to $MATLABROOT/bin/$ARCH/SLDirectShow.old2) Unzip the attached attachment_416297_20080102.zip file into a temporary directory. Copy the extracted SLDirectShow.dll.$ARCH to the $MATLABROOT/bin/$ARCH/ directory, without the $ARCH extention.In the text above, please replace $ARCH with {win32, win64} as appropriate, and $MATLABROOT with the full path of the directory in which you installed MATLAB.

Summary: Can I set options for a hybrid function that runs after ga, gamultiobj, threshacceptbnd, or simulanealbnd? For example, how can I have the hybrid function show iterative display, and use a medium-scale algorithm?
Description: The documentation does not make clear how to set options separately for hybrid functions.

Summary: Multirate blocks may have missed sample hits during variable-step simulation in normal mode.
Description: Blocks that have multiple sample rates, such as the Rate Transistion block or the DSP Unbuffer block, may start missing sample hits for long running simulations when using a variable-step solver. This is due to the small numerical skew that accumulates between the task times of the different sample rates. Because of this skew, we may encounter a case where the block's GCD rate does not have a sample hit even though one of its rates has a hit! If the GCD rate does not have a hit, then Simulink assumes that none of the block rates have a hit and skips executing the block. ?
Workaround: You can avoid this bug by either switching to Accelerator mode or using a fixed-step solver.

Summary: xPC Target host-target serial (RS-232) connection times out
Description: The use of the xPC Target serial (RS-232) connection for host-target communications will fail and return the error message: ??? timeout while receiving: check serial link
Workaround: To work around this issue, do one of the following:Use the TCP/IP connection for host-target communications.Contact MathWorks Support for an alternate solution.

Summary: imwrite puts an incorrect pixel aspect ratio value into GIF files
Description: imwrite  puts an incorrect pixel aspect ratio value into GIF files. This can cause warnings or incorrect image appearance in external applications, such as Photoshop and the GIMP. The image pixel data is correct, though.

Summary: The Uncertain Parameters dialog box does not update when parameters are deleted
Description: When deleting parameters in the Uncertain Parameters dialog box, the parameters are removed from the response optimization project but the dialog box does not update.In the Uncertain Parameters dialog box, When you select all the parameters and remove them using the delete button, the dialog box does not update and continues to display the parameters. When you remove each parameter individually, the dialog box does not update when you delete the last remaining parameter.
Workaround: If you are using a previous version, please read the following:Exit MATLABRename <matlabroot>\toolbox\sloptim\sloptguis\@srogui\@UncertaintyDialog\updateTable.m to <matlabroot>\toolbox\sloptim\sloptguis\@srogui\@UncertaintyDialog\updateTable.original.m.Replace the file <matlabroot>\toolbox\sloptim\sloptguis\@srogui\@UncertaintyDialog\updateTable.m with the attached file.Start MATLAB and type the following command at the MATLAB command prompt:      rehash toolboxcache

Summary: Configuration set changes are not applied
Description: When configuring a user defined system target, you can use the slConfigUISetVal function in callback routines to change configuration set options. Unless you make and apply other changes using the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the changes made using the slConfigUISetVal function are not applied.

Summary: Masking particular built-in blocks makes the block parameter dialog box inaccessible
Description: Suppose that you mask any of the following built-in blocks in your model: Assignment Interpolation using Prelookup Lookup Table Lookup Table (2-D) Lookup Table (n-D) Prelookup Selector  If you attempt to access the original block parameter dialog box, e.g., by selecting Edit > Look Under Mask , Simulink displays an error message similar to the following: Error occurred when trying to create dialogReference to non-existent field 'parameterName'.
Workaround: To work around this issue, use the set_param and get_param functions to interact programmatically with an affected block's original parameters.

Summary: MATLAB may crash during Simulink model compilation if the model intermixes virtual and nonvirtual buses
Description: If a Simulink model intermixes virtual and nonvirtual buses in the following way, MATLAB may exit with a segmentation violation when compiling the model: The model contains a Bus Creator block whose output bus signal is a direct input (no intervening blocks) to a second Bus Creator block. The virtuality of the bus that is input to the second Bus Creator block does not match the virtuality of the bus that is output by the block.
Workaround: Ensure that the virtuality of any bus signal created by one Bus Creator block, and input to a second Bus Creator block, matches the virtuality of the bus signal output by the second Bus Creator block. You can use a Signal Conversion block to change the virtuality of a bus.

Summary: Function pcolorm errors with graticule specified by lat, lon vectors
Description: If function pcolorm  is called with three or more arguments and the first two are vectors, an error results. In this syntax, the first two arguments (LAT  and LON ) contain latitudes and longitudes used to define the graticule onto which an image of the input data grid is colormapped. If LAT  and LON  are matrices, there is no problem. Vectors should be acceptable as well (they are expanded via function meshgrat ), but instead lead to an error in function surfacem .
