Workaround: To work around this issue, replace the pcolorm.m function file in your current installation with the version in the attachment, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file. matlabroot/toolbox/map/mapdisp/pcolorm.mmatlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function.For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension,e.g. pcolorm.m.OLD.Copy the version of pcolorm.m from the attachment into the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: The MATLAB compiler (mcc) version 4.7 (R2007b) hangs, and applications compiled with version 4.7 hang, when invoked on Apple OS X 10.5 (Leopard)
Description: The MATLAB compiler (mcc ) version 4.7 (R2007b) hangs, and applications compiled with version 4.7 hang, when invoked on Apple OS X 10.5 (Leopard).
Workaround: If the DISPLAY environment variable is cleared by entering unsetenv DISPLAY at the shell prompt, the MATLAB compiler (mcc) will not hang. In addition, clearing the DISPLAY environment variable also allows the compiled application to execute without hanging.As a workaround for Intel based Leopard systems, download the attached files. Update these files in both the installed MATLAB area, as well as in the MCR area, for those machines to which you have deployed any applications. Be sure to save existing files before updating. This workaround has been tested; however, the root cause of this problem is unknown and still under investigation.To implement the workaround, do the following:Backup /bin/maci and your /java/jar/mwswing.jar file before proceeding.Determine the base of the installation path. The installation path defaults to /Applications/MATLAB_R2007b.Determine the base of the MCR installation. The MCR installation defaults to /Applications/MATLAB/MATLAB_Component_Runtime/V77.Copy libmwmclmcr.dylib and libmwmcr.dylib into matlabroot|mcrroot/bin/maci where matlabroot|mcrroot is the base of the MATLAB installation or MCR installation.Copy mwswing.jar into matlabroot|mcrroot/java/jar.

Summary: VBA code with Spreadsheet Link? EX API functions returns compilation error.
Description: Customer using Spreadsheet Link? EX writes VBA code that uses Spreadsheet Link? EX functions such as MLGetMatrix  or MLPutMatrix . The customer's VB code runs in the VB Editor and in the spreadsheet, however when the customer uses the Debug -> Compile option in the Visual Basic editor, they get the error: "Compile error in hidden module: Module1". This error only occurs when Spreadsheet Link? EX R2007b is setup with Excel.
Workaround: The error described above will occur with any code when using the Debug -> Compile option as long as the Spreadsheet Link? EX reference is selected in the VB editor. Once the Spreadsheet Link? EX reference is unselected, VB code can be compiled

Summary: imshow does not display the bottom row or left column of an image when a 'tight' border is used.
Description: When you specify a 'tight'  border for imshow  image display, via a parameter/value pair or via the IPT preference 'imshowborder' , the left column and bottom row of the specified image are not displayed.
Workaround: To workaround this issue, replace the version of the private function initSize.m in your current installation with the updated version contained in the attached file.Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file. matlabroot\toolbox\images\imuitools\private\initSize.mmatlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension, initSize.m.OLD.Download the attached file, extract initSize.m, and store it in the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Dragging Simulink blocks is slow on Windows Vista with the Aero window manager
Description: Dragging Simulink blocks is slow on Windows Vista with the Aero window manager. It can take seconds for the outlines of the dragged blocks to catch up with the mouse as it moves about the screen.
Workaround: Do not use the Aero window manager with Simulink. Instead, use another manager, such as the basic Vista window manager.

Summary: MATTEST tscore sign is incorrect for the PERMUTATION option.
Description: When using the PERMUTATION option, MATTEST returns tscores with the wrong sign, that is, + instead of -.
Workaround: Follow these steps as a workaround:1. Quite MATLAB.2. Rename the file $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\microarray\mattest.m to mattest.m.old.(where $MATLAB$=the MATLAB root directory on your machine)3. Copy the attached file to $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\microarray.4. Restart MATLAB5. After restarting MATLAB, issue the following command at the MATLAB prompt:rehash toolboxcache.

Summary: UNIX? platforms: help function incorrectly returns No help comments found in some situations
Description: On UNIX? platforms, when you run help  for a file, sometimes the result is No help comments found , even though help information is available. This occurs when the full path to the file contains the same name as the help file, but using a different case. For example, if you run help myfile  which is located at .../myFile/myfile.m  , where myFile  is on the search path, the result is No help comments found even though there is help for myfile.m .
Workaround: Run the help function for the file, but include the file extension. For example, instead ofhelp myfileusehelp myfile.m

Summary: Incorrect code generation: Real-Time Workshop code generator incorrectly breaks character constants when creating code for Stateflow or Embedded MATLAB Function blocks
Description: The Real-Time Workshop code generator formats a line of code with length greater than 80 characters so that the code spans multiple lines, each line having a maximum of 80 characters. When the character sequence ' ' or " " is at the end or break point of an 80 character line, the code generator replaces the space with a new line character, and the code fails to compile. For example, consider the following code: [78 character length expression] ' ' In this case, the code generator creates the following code, which does not compile: [78 character length expression] '' ?

Summary: Simulink on Mac OS X may crash when you wake a sleeping display or after a fast user switch
Description: Simulink on Mac OS X may crash when you wake a sleeping display or after a fast user switch. An error message may report an error being detected "on GUI thread " or "in QT Code. A simulink_crash_dump file is created in the user's home directory.

Summary: Block I/O buffer of a Merge block might change between local and global when the name of a source block changes
Description: If the name of a source block to a Merge block changes, block I/O buffer allocation might change in the following way. If the alphabetical order of the name of the source block: Precedes the name of the outport signal which serves as input to the Merge block, local buffer allocation occurs. Follows the name of the outport signal which serves as input to the Merge block, global allocation occurs.  The block I/O buffer allocation only occurs when all of the following conditions are met: The Configuration Parameters > Optimization > Enable local block outputs  check box is selected. There are two or more conditionally executed subsystems, for example, the Switch Case Action Subsystem, in the model. Outputs of the conditionally executed subsystems are merged in a different, nonparent subsystem. The conditionally executed subsystems are not in the model root level.

Summary: Error occurs when launching Model Advisor on a renamed model
Description: The Simulink software produces an error under the following conditions: Launch Model Advisor for your model. Save your model under another name. Do not close the renamed model. Launch Model Advisor on the renamed model.  The following is an example of the error: ??? Error using ==> getfullname at 19 Invalid Simulink object name: rtwdemo_counter.  Error in ==> C:\matlab\toolbox\simulink\simulink\modeladvisor\modeladvisor.p>modeladvisor at 33  mdladvObj = Simulink.ModelAdvisor.getModelAdvisor(system,'new');
Workaround: To correct this error, close and reopen the renamed model, then launch Model Advisor.

Summary: Macintosh? OS X 10.5 platforms: startup problems with X11 fail to open MATLAB? desktop
Description: On Apple? Macintosh? OS X 10.5 platforms, X11 sometimes interferes with opening the MATLAB? desktop upon startup. You will not experience the problem if you performed a clean install of OS X 10.5 and MATLAB, and created a new user account. Symptoms might include: X11 starts, but never opens an xterm window and MATLAB becomes unresponsive. X11 reports a problem with an invalid DISPLAY . X11 reports an _X11TransSocketINETConnect  error.  To determine if you are experiencing a startup conflict with X11, run the following commands from xterm or Terminal: /Applications/MATLAB_R2007b/bin/matlabxhost +xlogoecho $DISPLAY When functioning correctly MATLAB starts and opens the desktop xhost +  reports: access control disabled, clients can connect from any host  xlogo  opens an X11 window containing the X11 logo the DISPLAY  environment variable is a system-determined value in a format similar to /tmp/launch-tm3BjQ/:0
Workaround: If you are not able to use Software Update or are still experiencing problems, try one or more of the following workarounds:Perform a clean installation of OS X 10.5 and MATLAB, use a new user account, and regularly check System Preferences > Software Update for OS X and X11 updates.If you receive an error with DISPLAY, or if DISPLAY has a value of :0, try to determine where it is being redefined. If you are not aware of any file where it is being redefined, it could be that some system administrators, third party applications, or open source installers changed DISPLAY in hidden files without your knowledge. Using xterm or Terminal and the ls -a?option, look for hidden shell startup files in your home folder that might have changed DISPLAY, including: .MacOSX/environment.plist, .login, .bash_login, .bash_profile, .bashrc, .cshrc, and .xinitrc. Comment out any lines that are changing DISPLAY, so that the system value is used.Look in the X11 preferences file in your home folder's Library > Preferences folder, for the file org.x.x11.plist, and review the contents of that file for anything that might be related to the issue you are experiencing.If you have tried all of the above and are still experiencing problems, consider upgrading X11 to the most recent stable version available from https://www.xquartz.org. However, The MathWorks cannot guarantee that MATLAB performs properly with the most recent stable version from xquartz.

Summary: The document produced by publishing a MATLAB file to HTML specifies XHTML as the DOCTYPE, but the document is not valid XHTML.
Description: When you publish MATLAB files to HTML, the files that the MATLAB?  software produces declare that they are XHTML in their DOCTYPE, but they are not. The files, for example, contain empty meta tags that lack the trailing slash.
Workaround: Edit the matlabroot/toolbox/matlab/codetools/private/mxdom2simplehtml.xsl file, to changedoctype-public="-//W3C//DTD XHTML 1.0 Strict//EN"/>todoctype-public="-//W3C//DTD HTML 4.01 Transitional//EN"/>The matlabroot directory is the directory where the MATLAB? product is installed. To determine where this directory is on your system, run the matlabroot command.?

Summary: Incorrect code generation: Code generator incorrectly uses local variable for block output
Description: The Real-Time Workshop software might incorrectly use local variables, instead of global variables, for block output buffers in conditionally executed subsystems, resulting in incorrect or uncompilable generated code, under the following conditions: The Configuration Parameters > Optimization > Enable local block outputs  check box is selected. There are two or more conditionally executed subsystems, for example, the Switch Case Action Subsystem, in the model. Outputs of the conditionally executed subsystems are merged in a different, nonparent subsystem. The conditionally executed subsystems are not in the model root level.  Attached is a sample model that demonstrates the problem.
Workaround: Make the merge signals global using a test point or global storage class.

Summary: Mechanical input parameter of DC Machine and Discrete DC Machine blocks does not work correctly
Description: If you change the Mechanical input  parameter of the DC Machine block or of the Discrete DC Machine block to speed w  and then look under the mask, you will see that the mechanical subsystem (Calculating the FCEM signal) remains as a torque TL input model.

Summary: Packaging a deploytool project fails with Java exception AWT-EventQueue-0 error.
Description: In some cases, adding files to deploytool  for packaging causes a Java exception to be thrown.
Workaround: In deploytool, open the Settings dialog and select Packaging. Do the following:Add and remove MATLAB files you want to package, in succession.Close the Settings dialog.Add any remaining files as you normally would.You should now be able to package the project successfully.

Summary: THD calculation in FFT Analysis tool changes depending upon number of cycles analysed
Description: The THD calculation in FFT Analysis tool of Powergui block computes a THD value that incorrectly depends on the Number of cycles  parameter specified in the GUI. For a periodic steady-state signal, the THD value should not be dependent on the number of cycles of analysed signal.
Workaround: Download the attached file and unzip it into a temporary directory.Copy the power_fftscope.p file into the following directory, overwriting the existing file:$MATLABROOT/toolbox/physmod/powersys/powersys/ where $MATLABROOT is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by enteringmatlabroot in the MATLAB Command Window.

Summary: "Parse" and "Parse Diagram" may report false errors about temporal threshold values
Description: When you invoke the "Parse" or "Parse Diagram" options in Stateflow just after loading a model and before updating or simulating it, you may receive false errors that look like this: Temporal threshold count must be a positive number less than 0x8000_0000. These false errors occur when temporal operators use data of "constant" scope to specify the temporal threshold, as in the following example: after(XYZ,tick)every(ABC,E) In this example, ABC  and XYZ  are data of "constant" scope whose initial values are legal and below the threshold of 0x8000_0000 (2^30). These errors do not occur when you invoke parsing as part of model compilation, or after first updating or simulating the model.
Workaround: Update or simulate the model once before using "Parse" or "Parse Diagram".

Summary: impoly is slow when a large number of vertices are specified.
Description: When you call impoly  with a large number of vertices, impoly  takes a long time to define the polygon.
Workaround: To workaround this issue, replace the following files in your current installation with the updated version contained in the attached file.Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following files: matlabroot/toolbox/images/imuitools/private/defaultPointSymbol.mmatlabroot/toolbox/images/imuitools/impoint.mmatlabroot/toolbox/images/imuitools/impoly.mmatlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. For example, rename the existing files, adding "OLD" to the file extension, impoly.m.OLD.Download the attached file, extract the 3 files, and store them in the directories specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: NEWFF documentation refers to T as inputs instead of targets.
Description: The NEWFF documentation refers to input argument T as network inputs.  It should refer to T as network targets.
Workaround: Disregard the reference to T as inputs.  T refers to targets.?

Summary: DIVIDERAND example in version 5.1 of NNT doc fails.
Description: The following call to DIVIDERAND, without optional arguments, fails: p=rands(3,1000);[trainP ,valP,testV,trainInd,valInd,testInd] = dividerand(p);
Workaround: In versions of the toolbox where this has not been fixed, use the following code, supply thedefault values for the optional arguments as follows:p=rands(3,1000);[trainP,valP,testV,trainInd,valInd,testInd] = dividerand(p,0.6,0.2,0.2);

Summary: Model Discretizer fails when applied to Transfer Fcn block with initial states
Description: When you apply the Model Discretizer to a Transfer Fcn block with initial states, the following errors can occur:  When you simulate the discretized model, the model gives a wrong numerical result because the initial state is not calculated correctly. The transform method of the block always changes to 'tustin' (a bilinear Tustin approximation). When you use the transform method 'prewarp' (Tustin approximation with frequency prewarping), the following errors occur: ??? Error using ==> lti.c2d at 53 Prewarping frequency must be a positive scalar smaller than the Nyquist frequency. Error in ==> sldiscutil at 141 md = c2d(m,SampleTime,method,WcRad); Error in ==> getinternaldiscfunction>DiscretizedTransferFcnWithIC at 308 [numd, dend] = sldiscutil('disctransferfcn', {SampleTime, method, cf, Numerator, Denominator}); Error in ==> sldiscmdl>DiscretizeSubsystem at 736 newBlk = feval( getDiscretizeFunction(discfcn), theSelected, method,... Error in ==> sldiscmdl at 535 [hasContinuousBlk, origs, news, xPosn, yPosn] = DiscretizeSubsystem(selected{i},SampleTime,offset,... Error in ==> slmdldisc at 93 [origBlk, newBlk,errorOcurred] = sldiscmdl(sys, strstoffset, method, cf, option);  When you transform to a configurable subsystem, the following errors occur: ??? Error using ==> add_param tt1 parameter 'disc_configurable_lib' already exists. Error in ==> sldiscmdl at 344 add_param(sys,'disc_configurable_lib',libName); Error in ==> slmdldisc at 68 [origBlk, newBlk,errorOcurred] = sldiscmdl(sys, strstoffset, method, ...

Summary: The Plot Browser fails to list the children of an axes under some conditions
Description: The Plot Browser sometimes fails to list the children of an axes The issue tends to occur when there are multiple figures containing many graphics.
Workaround: Closing and reopening the Plot Browser might resolve the problem.

Summary: Bus signal information does not propagate through hidden Rate Transition block during model simulation
Description: When you select the model option Automatically handle rate transition for data transfer , Simulink software automatically inserts hidden Rate Transition blocks in the model as necessary to resolve illegal rate transitions. However, during simulation, if a Rate Transition block is automatically inserted at a bus signal, the bus signal information fails to propagate through the hidden Rate Transition block. As a result, downstream blocks that require bus information, such as the Bus Selector block, might fail.
Workaround: To work around this bug, manually insert a Rate Transition block at a bus signal if the software reports an illegal rate transition at the bus.

Summary: Running an External Mode simulation crashes MATLAB
Description: Running a multi-rate model in single tasking mode under External mode might cause MATLAB to exit. Under these conditions, the external mode server running on the target may send corrupted TCP/IP packets to the MATLAB External Mode client, causing MATLAB to exit.
Workaround: Download the attached patch installer patch_installer.m and the ZIP archive patch_412508.zip. Start MATLAB and enter the following command at the prompt to install the patch:patch_installer('patch_412508.zip', 'install')Follow instructions printed out on the MATLAB command line to complete the installation. The attached patch modifies the following files in your MATLAB installation:<matlabroot>\toolbox\rtw\targets\tic6000\tic6000\src\extMode\ext_svr_c6000.c<matlabroot>\toolbox\rtw\targets\tic6000\tic6000\src\extMode\ext_svr_tcpip_transport_c6000.c<matlabroot>\toolbox\rtw\targets\tic6000\tic6000\src\extMode\ext_tcpip_utils_c6000.c<matlabroot>\toolbox\rtw\targets\tic6000\tic6000\src\extMode\ext_work_c6000.c<matlabroot>\toolbox\rtw\targets\tic6000\tic6000\src\extMode\updown_c6000.c<matlabroot>\toolbox\rtw\targets\tic6000\tic6000demos\c6416dskafxr_extmode.mdl

Summary: Mixed Bus Types: MATLAB can crash when compiling a Simulink model that contains both virtual and non-virtual buses
Description: When all of the following are true, MATLAB may exit with a segmentation violation when compiling a Simulink model. MATLAB will not produce a stack trace on exit. The Simulink model contains both virtual and non-virtual buses. At least one of the buses in the Simulink model is created by a Bus Creator block. If that Bus Creator block is virtual, at least one of its inputs is a nonvirtual bus; or if it is nonvirtual, at least one of its inputs is a virtual bus. The model contains at least one Bus Selector that selects a signal from a Bus Creator that has one of the input configurations mentioned in Step 3.
Workaround: Do not use a Bus Creator block to intermix virtual and non-virtual buses. If the output of a Bus Creator is virtual, all inputs to the block must be virtual buses or non-bus signals. Similarly, if the output of a Bus Creator is non-virtual, all inputs to the block must be non-virtual buses or non-bus signals.

Summary: Selecting the Simulink editor's undo/redo buttons too fast can crash MATLAB
Description: Selecting the undo or redo buttons quickly in succession can cause the undo/redo functions to be called while still handling a previous undo/redo operation. This can result in the undo/redo stack being left in a corrupted state. Many times this results in a MATLAB crash.

Summary: When loading a session of Filter Design and Analysis Tool (FDATool) containing a fixed point filter, the "Product Mode" popup becomes invisible.
Description: When loading a session of Filter Design and Analysis Tool (FDATool) containing a fixed point filter, the "Product Mode" popup becomes invisible.

Summary: Functions distance and azimuth return zero for certain points nearly 180 degrees apart in longitude
Description: This problem arises only in the case of geodesics on the ellipsoid . It does not affect the results for great circles on the sphere, and it does not affect the results for rhumb lines on either the sphere or ellipsoid. There are two distinct, but related, cases: Function distance  uses the Vincenty algorithm to compute distances and azimuths (the "inverse problem") for long geodesics on the ellipsoid. However the applicability of this algorithm breaks down for point pairs in which both points fall sufficiently close to the Equator and  in which the points are located either exactly, or very nearly 180 degrees apart in longitude. In this case distance  returns a value of zero for the range and azimuth (if the azimuth was requested. Likewise, function azimuth  (which is simply a wrapper around distance ) returns a value of zero. These results are clearly invalid (except for the azimuth value in the special case in which the geodesic crosses directly over the North Pole). In a certain case in which the Vincenty algorithm should work, the distance and azimuth values are still set to zero. This happens given a pair in which the points are located either exactly, or very nearly 180 degrees apart in longitude and one  of the points fits the case 1 criterion for being sufficiently close to the Equator.
Workaround: To work around this issue, replace the geodesicinv.m function file in your current installation with the version in the attachment, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file. matlabroot/toolbox/map/private/geodesicinv.mmatlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function.For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension, geodesicinv.m.OLD.Copy the version of geodesicinv.m from the attachment into the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.This file includes a fix such that in case 2, defined above, correct distance and azimuth values will be computed and returned. It also improves the handling of case 1 by returning values of NaN, rather than zero, for both distance and azimuth, in order to more clearly signal that a valid solution cannot be provided. In addition, if any point pairs in a given call to distance or azimuth belong to the set of inputs covered by case 1, then a warning is issued.You can also use the same procedure to download the attached versions of toolbox/map/map/distance.m and toolbox/map/map/azimuth.m, which update the MATLAB-file help in order to document this change in behavior.

Summary: Errors occur when trying to close the Lookup Table Editor
Description: When the Lookup Table Editor is open for a model, change a table value. Then try to open the Lookup Table Editor again for the same model. The first LUT Editor window should appear; however, a new LUT Editor opens.  If you try to close the first LUT Editor window, the second one closes. If you try to close the first LUT Editor window again, errors like the following can occur: ??? Reference to non-existent field 'SwingWidgets'. Error in ==> D:\matlab\toolbox\simulink\blocks\sltbledit.p>doClose at 808 FigFrame = appdata.SwingWidgets.FigFrame; Error in ==> D:\matlab\toolbox\simulink\blocks\sltbledit.p>lutCallbackHandler at 174 feval(char(evd.type), evd.event); ??? Error while evaluating com.mathworks.toolbox.simulink.lookuptable.LUT LutCallback

Summary: Overflow error occurs when using a Subtract block to subtract two unsigned values
Description: Suppose that your model contains a Subtract block that subtracts two unsigned integer or fixed-point values. Simulink might display a run-time overflow error if all of the following conditions apply: The Subtract block's Saturate on integer overflow  parameter (on the Signal Attributes  pane of the block dialog box) is disabled. The model's Detect overflow  parameter (under Data Validity > Signals  on the Configuration Parameters dialog box) specifies error .
Workaround: To work around this issue, consider any of the following approaches:Upcast the Subtract block's input signals to a larger data type. For example, suppose that the data type of a Subtract block's input signals specify ufix16_En3 (i.e., a 16-bit unsigned generalized fixed-point number), and the block's Output data type parameter specifies Inherit: Inherit via internal rule. In this case, you can prevent the overflow error by upcasting both inputs to a data type such as ufix17_En3 (i.e., a 17-bit unsigned generalized fixed-point number).Convert both of the Subtract block's input signals to signed integer or fixed-point data types.Set the model's Detect overflow parameter (under Data Validity > Signals on the Configuration Parameters dialog box) to warning.

Summary: INPOLYGON may return an incorrect result for multiply connected or disjoint polygons.
Description: Multiply connnected polygons have a loop of edges that define an external boundary, and one or more internal loops that bound holes. The distinct loops are defined by a NaN-separated array of coordinates, where the NaN denotes the termination of one loop and beginning of a new loop. Disjoint polygons are defined in a similar manner, in this case the loops may not be contained within other loops. Disjoint polygons are common in the mapping toolbox; for example, to represent a group of islands. The INPOLYGON function sometimes failed to give a correct outcome when a point was tested against such polygons.

Summary: Spurious error message for asynchronous function call subsystem containing Stateflow chart connected to Mux or Bus Creator block
Description: Simulink can incorrectly flag an error for an asynchronous function call subsystem if all of the following conditions are true: The asynchronous function call subsystem contains a Stateflow chart that outputs data to Simulink. At least one data output from the Stateflow chart connects to a Mux or Bus Creator block. At least one input of the Mux or Bus Creator block is not from the Stateflow chart.  Simulink displays the following error message: mux-block-name  may not be used to combine an asynchronous function call with data or a synchronous function call.
Workaround: To work around this bug, insert a nonvirtual block between the Stateflow chart outport and the Mux or Bus Creator block.

Summary: Incorrect output of Fread with repetition factor >1024 and skip = 0 or not set.
Description: If you call the fread  function with the precision  (3rd) input argument specified as a multiple greater than 1024 (for example, fread(fid, 4000, '1025*uint8'))  and with the skip  (4th) argument either set to zero or unspecified,MATLAB reads only 1024 elements of the specified data type from the file. The value returned in the count  (2nd)output is 1024, regardless of what the count  (2nd) input has been set to.Example: fid = fopen('myfile.dat', 'r'); [A count] = fread(fid, 4000, '1023*uint8'); count ans = 4000 numel(A) ans = 4000 ftell(fid) ans = 4000 [A count] = fread(fid, 4000, '1024*uint8'); count ans = 4000 numel(A) ans = 4000 ftell(fid) ans = 8000 [A count] = fread(fid, 4000, '1025*uint8'); count ans = 1024 numel(A) ans = 1024 ftell(fid) ans = 9024 fclose(fid);

Summary: Unexpected simulation results when using the For Iterator Subsystem block with direct connections from Inport to Outport
Description: Simulink can produce unexpected simulation results when you use a For Iterator Subsystem  block. This happens when both of the following conditions are met: An input port of the For Iterator  block is connected directly to an Inport  block of the For Iterator Subsystem. The same Inport  block is also connected directly to an Outport  block of the For Iterator Subsystem.  When both of these conditions are true, Simulink can produce unexpected simulation results.
Workaround: To avoid this problem, you can do the following:Insert a Signal Conversion block on the signal path that runs directly between the Inport and Outport blocks. In the Signal Conversion block, select the Override optimizations and always copy signal parameter.

Summary: Undescriptive error message issued when multiple voltage source blocks are connected in a loop
Description: Connecting voltage source blocks ( DC Voltage Source, AC Voltage Source, and Controlled Voltage Source blocks) in an array of series/parallel combinations produces the following undescriptive error message: Index exceeds matrix dimensions  The expected behavior is to receive an error message similar to the following: Error using ==> psberror at 16 The Voltage Source block 'MyModel/DC Voltage Source' cannot be connected in parallel with another voltage source.

Summary: A low-rate task executes multiple times in the scheduler.
Description: This bug may occur in models that contain three or more rates. When this bug occurs, a low-rate task executes at the more frequent base rate instead, and may block lower priority tasks from executing.
Workaround: Download patch_installer.m and patch_411859.zip from Attachments, below.Install the patch by entering the following command at the MATLAB prompt:  patch_installer('patch_411859.zip', 'install')

Summary: nitfread returns an empty array when reading multispectral imagery
Description: If you call nitfread  on a file containing multispectral imagery, nitfread  returns an empty array as output---even when nitfinfo  returns the correct value in the NumberOfMultiSpectralBands  field for the same file.

Summary: Macintosh? OS X 10.5 platform startup displays "where is OroborOSX?" in Choose Application dialog box
Description: Upon MATLAB? startup on Macintosh? OS X 10.5 platforms, a Choose Application dialog box appears, with the message "where is OroborOSX?". This will not cause any problems in running MATLAB.
Workaround: You can suppress the display of the message at MATLAB startup for future sessions. When the dialog box appears, peruse the list of displayed applications in it, and select X11. The next time you start MATLAB, the dialog box will not appear.

Summary: Continuous Wavelet 1-D GUI buttons not displaying correctly
Description: On some platforms the buttons in the Continuous Wavelet 1-D GUI are not displayed correctly. The New Coefficients Line , Analyze , and Refresh Maxima Lines  buttons overlap each other. The Analyze  button is not enabled as it should be after a signal is loaded.

Summary: Model Info blocks do not work consistently when placed inside Simulink Libraries.?
Description: If you place a Model Info block in a subsystem that is part of a library, and later use this library block in a Simulink model, then changes in the Model Info block in the library are not consistently propagated to the library-linked instance of the block. ?

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Stateflow charts with multiple listeners on implicit events may produce wrong results
Description: As documented, Stateflow charts process multiple listeners on implicit state events (such as enter(A) ) as follows: For multiple listeners on an implicit event: Each time the event is triggered, Stateflow broadcasts the event separately to each listener.For example, in the attached model example1.mdl , two states, B.B1  and B.B11 , listen on the implicit state entry event enter(A.A11) . Therefore, whenever state A.A11  is entered, both B1  and B11  are woken up.  For multiple listeners that share an ancestor-child relationship: Stateflow broadcasts the event only once to the common ancestor of the listeners.In the attached model example2.mdl , the states B1  and B11  share an ancestor relationship (B1  is an ancestor of B11 ). In this case, whenever state A.A11  is entered, only state B1  is woken up.   However, rule #2 above is violated when you create the child before the parent. For example, suppose you create the inner state B.B1.B11  before the outer state B.B1  in example2.mdl . Stateflow might broadcast the event incorrectly to state B.B1.B11  rather than broadcasting the event to state B.B1 . As a result, y1  might not get incremented when state A.A1  is entered.
Workaround: There are no known workarounds for this problem.

Summary: Embedded MATLAB Function blocks with trailing ones in structure output dimensions may cause compile errors
Description: A Simulink model may fail to compile in the following circumstances: The model contains an Embedded MATLAB Function block with output(s) of structure type. The structure has a matrix field whose dimensions contain trailing ones.  This error may occur when you attempt to simulate the model or perform Real-Time Workshop code generation. It produces the following message: Make Error: ..... error: incompatible types in assignment ?
Workaround: Remove trailing ones from the matrix field dimensions of structure outputs.?

Summary: The Rapid Accelerator mode in Simulink Version 7.0 (R2007b) does not support comma separated variable lists in the Load from workspace input dialog.
Description: Models run in Rapid Accelerator mode that are driven from variables listed(in comma seperated list format) in the Load from workspace  input dialog will not simulate in Simulink Version 7.0 (R2007b). Simulating such a model causes an error message to be displayed, and the simulation to halt. ?

Summary: Caps Lock and Shift keys interfere with zooming and other controls
Description: The Caps Lock  and Shift  keys cause most Stateflow key bindings to fail since they match for lower-case letters. This was done by design, in case such keys would one day be useful.

Summary: Command-line errors can occur when attempting to plot time series data in the Test Results Viewer if all the test vectors belong to the same group.
Description: Attempting to plot time series data in the Test Results Viewer when all the test vectors belong to the same group can result in the following error being reported at the command line: ??? Error using ==> feval Subscripted assignment dimension mismatch. Error in ==> hdsviewer.plot.init_listeners>LocalRefreshPlot at 104 draw(this) Warning: Error occurred while evaluating listener callback. > In hdsviewer.plot.hdsplot at 40 In hdsviewer.plotEditor.newplot>localPlot at 190 and a failure to generate the plot.

Summary: MATLAB error when creating a colorbar in a figure containing a colormap with a single entry
Description: If the colorbar  function or GUI button is applied to a figure containing a colormap with one entry (1-by-3), as in the following code: surf(peaks);colormap([0 0 1]);colorbar; the following error is printed to the command window: ??? Bad value for axes property: 'YLim'Values must be increasing and non-NaN Error in ==> colorbar>make_colorbar at 180ch=scribe.colorbar(peeraxes,location,position,pvpairs{:}); Error in ==> colorbar at 145 [x,msg] = make_colorbar(peeraxes,location,position,pvpairs); ?
Workaround: To obtain a colorbar for a monochrome colormap, you may specify a two-element colormap with both entries having the same value.

Summary: Assertion failure in LowerMatrixTypesForBackend.cpp during code generation of models containing Embedded MATLAB functions.
Description: A bug caused by the 'Create reference parameters' optimization causes an assertion during code generation for models containing Embedded MATLAB Function blocks in Simulink or Embedded MATLAB functions in Stateflow. The source file LowerMatrixTypesForBackend.cpp  appears at the top of the stack trace. The bug is triggered by code that contains the pattern x=function (x) , where function   is the name of an Embedded MATLAB function. Note that this pattern may originate from Embedded MATLAB library code.
Workaround: Switch off the 'Create reference parameters' optimization by typing the following command at the MATLAB command prompt: sf('feature', 'Create reference parameters', 0)

Summary: Interactive changes to the color of a textbox annotation do not update its 'Color' property
Description: If you change the color of a textbox annotation using the plot edit mode context menu, the change is not reflected in either the Property Editor or the Color  property of the textbox.
Workaround: Instead of using the context-menu to change the color, you can achieve this interactively by using the Property Inspector. Open the Plotting Tools, select the textbox, and click More Properties to open the Property Inspector to change the Color property. This causes both the Property Editor and the Color property to update.

Summary: Why am I unable to run an application that I created using MATLAB Compiler that includes the IMAQRESET function?
Description: Using MATLAB Compiler, I compiled an M-function which includes the IMAQRESET function. However, the application generates the following error when executed: ??? Undefined variable "iatbrowser" or class "iatbrowser.refreshHardwareList". Error in ==> myFunction at 8 MATLAB:undefinedVarOrClass
Workaround: To work around this bug, download the attached zip file and extract the file in it to:<MATLAB>\toolbox\imaq\imaqwhere <MATLAB> is where you have installed MATLAB.This will replace your current version of IMAQRESET.M.After replacing IMAQRESET.M, restart MATLAB and then recompile your application using MATLAB Compiler.

Summary: Possible incorrect numerical results due to improper target function library (TFL) replacement of addition operation
Description: During code generation for a model containing a Sum block, the addition operation may be improperly replaced with a TFL-specified function if both of the following conditions are true: The inputs to the Sum block have different scaling values than the output of the Sum block. The addition operator entry registered in the current TFL specifies that TFL replacement request processing should not check for an exact match to the call-site slope and bias values.  Under these conditions, the addition operation is replaced and the difference in the slopes and biases between the inputs and output is lost. When the generated code is executed, the numerical results may not match the simulated results for the same model. Note:  This issue can only occur if a custom target function library is used and it registers a replacement for the addition operation. The target function libraries provided with Real-Time Workshop and Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder do not exhibit this issue.

Summary: S functions with zero crossings may cause RTW code generation to fail if a variable step solver is used.
Description: The RTW file created during code generation does not contain the names of the signals registering zero crossings for S functions. This can cause code generation to fail if the S function uses zero crossing detection. This will only occur if a variable step solver is selected because zero crossing detection is only enabled with those solvers.

Summary: Using dicomwrite with 'CreateMode' set to 'Copy' returns warning about invalid characters.
Description: When writing files that were previously read using dicomread  and dicominfo , dicomwrite  can return the following warning: >> X = dicomread('file.dcm');>> info = dicominfo('file.dcm');>> dicomwrite(X, 'newfile.dcm', info, 'CreateMode', 'Copy')Warning: Attribute (0008,0106) has invalid characters Since the original data did not contain any invalid characters, dicomwrite  should not produce any warnings. However, in this case, one of the atrributes has an odd number of characters, and dicomwrite  adds an extra padding byte to the data. dicomwrite  adds the padding byte before validation, which is the wrong order. The new DICOM file contains the correct data, and (in this case) you can ignore the warning.

Summary: Real-Time Workshop incorrectly copies content from one source file to another source file in generated code
Description: The Real-Time Workshop code generator formats a line of code with a length greater than 80 characters so that the code spans multiple lines, each line having a maximum of 80 characters. When there is a very deeply nested set (more than 35 levels) of if , for , or while  statements in a generated source file, the code generator copies part of the source code into another generated source file, and fails to compile the code.
Workaround: To generate code for the affected model, do the following:Download the attached file.In MATLAB, cd to the working directory to which you downloaded the file.Run the file by entering fix410483 at the MATLAB command prompt.You must run the fix410483.p file each time you restart MATLAB.

Summary: Using non-scalar parameters in Embedded MATLAB Function block causes errors during HDL code generation
Description: Embedded MATLAB Function blocks that use non-scalar parameter data generate assertion failures during HDL code generation.
Workaround: To use a vector parameter with an Embedded MATLAB Function block:In the Model Explorer, change the scope of the parameter data to Input.In the Simulink subsystem containing the Embedded MATLAB Function block, create a Constant block and assign its initial value to the parameterConnect the Constant block to the input port you created in step 1.Simulink HDL Coder does not support matrix data types; therefore there is no workaround for matrix parameters. To use the previously described workaround, change your design to use a flattened vector in place of a matrix.

Summary: Switch block might route signals incorrectly if the data type of the control input signal cannot represent the real-world value of zero
Description: The generated code for the Switch block might route its signals incorrectly under all of the following conditions: The Switch block's Criteria for passing first input  parameter specifies u2 ~= 0 . The data type associated with the Switch block's control input (u2) cannot represent the real-world value of zero, as is the case for the data type sfix32_Bp5 .  When simulating the model in Simulink, the control input signal can never be zero; hence, the Switch block's condition is always true and the block always routes its first input signal to its output. But in the code generated by Real-Time Workshop, the value of zero from the u2 ~= 0 expression is cast to the control input signal's data type for comparison, resulting in a non-zero real-world-value; consequently, the control input signal might attain a value that causes the Switch block's condition to be false and passes the third input signal as the block's output.
Workaround: To work around this issue, ensure that the data type of a Switch block's control input signal can represent the real-world value of zero. For example, insert a Data Type Conversion block before the Switch block and cast the control input signal to an appropriate data type. The data type that you specify should be able to represent the real-world value of zero without losing precision, range, or signedness.

Summary: Using slash characters ("/") in the names of Stateflow inputs, outputs, and events may prevent you from loading a model after resaving in it Stateflow 7.0 (R2007b)
Description: When you add inputs, outputs, and events in Stateflow charts, Simulink creates corresponding ports of the same name on the Stateflow block. If the names of these objects contain a forward slash "/" character, the full path name of the port looks ambiguous to Simulink. This ambiguity may cause errors in the way Simulink handles the model. As a result, after saving and closing the model, the model may no longer load. As an example, consider a model "M" containing a chart "C", which has an input named "A/B". The full path name for the input port corresponding to this data is "M/C/A/B", which can be misinterpreted as a model "M" containing subsystem "C" containing subsystem "A" containing port "B".
Workaround: If your model is open:Simulate your model to flag incorrectly named data as parse errors.Fix the errors by renaming the data to eliminate the slash characters.If your model is not open, there is no workaround at this time.

Summary: Inport block of virtual subsystem incorrectly inherits constant (inf) sample time when Inline Parameters is Off
Description: An inport block in a virtual subsystem can get a constant (inf) sample time when it should not if the following conditions hold: The inport specifies a sample time of inherited (-1) The virtual subsystem is driven by an atomic subsystem that has a sample time specified in its dialog Inline parameters (on the Optimization tab of Configuration Parameters) is off for the model  ?
Workaround: To work around this issue either:Set the desired sample time on the inport block in the virtual subsystem, orTurn off inline parameters

Summary: Adding temporary data in an Embedded MATLAB function in Stateflow may cause sporadic crashes.
Description: If you use the Model Explorer to create data with "temporary" scope inside an Embedded MATLAB function in Stateflow, you may experience sporadic crashes if the variable has been eliminated in the generated code. Temporary variables may be eliminated if they are constants or if they are unused.
Workaround: Do not declare temporary data inside an Embedded MATLAB function or make sure the temporary variable is used and not constant.

Summary: The Ports and Data Manager does not display a dialog view.
Description: The Ports and Data Manager dialog box should contain two distinct sections: a list view in the left pane and a dialog view in the right pane. When you use the Ports and Data Manager with Embedded MATLAB Function blocks and Stateflow Truth Table blocks, the dialog view may not appear.
Workaround: Open the Ports and Data Manager.Put your mouse cursor on the rightmost edge of the dialog box.When the shape of the cursor changes, click and drag the the edge to the left. The dialog view should appear.

Summary: A compiled application that calls MMREADER fails to run in the MCR
Description: The use of mmreader  in a MATLAB Compiler deployed application does not work correctly when run in the MATLAB Compiler Runtime (MCR), and generates the following error message: ??? Invalid MEX-file 'C:\Program Files\MATLAB\MATLAB Component Runtime\v77\toolbox\matlab\audiovideo\@mmreader\private\mmreadermex.mexw32': The specified module could not be found.
Workaround: To work around this issue, copy the attached files into your MATLAB Compiler Runtime (MCR) directory as follows:For 32-bit Windows platforms: Download the mmreadermcr32bit.zip file (attached below), unzip it, and place it in MCR_ROOT/bin/win32. For 64-bit Windows platforms: Download the mmreadermcr64bit.zip file (attached below), unzip it, and place it in MCR_ROOT/bin/win64.Where MCR_ROOT is the install directory for the MATLAB Compiler Runtime (MCR).

Summary: Build information function packNGo fails to include referenced model code in zip file
Description: If you use the packNGo  function for a model that contains model blocks referring to other models, packNGo  fails to include the referenced models' source code in the zip file.
Workaround: To work around this bug, perform the following steps:Download the attached zip and file to the same arbitrary directory.Open MATLAB and cd to the directory containing the two files.Issue the MATLAB command>> installPatch('install')This command installs the following patch files at the listed locations in your MATLAB installation tree, and copies the original installed files into a zip file named OriginalFiles.zip:matlab/toolbox/rtw/rtw/@RTW/@BuildInfo/BuildInfo.pmatlab/toolbox/rtw/rtw/@RTW/@BuildInfo/addLinkObjects.pmatlab/toolbox/rtw/rtw/@RTW/@BuildInfo/getFullFileList.pmatlab/toolbox/rtw/rtw/@RTW/@BuildInfo/getHierarchicalChildren.pmatlab/toolbox/rtw/rtw/@RTW/@BuildInfo/packNGo.pmatlab/toolbox/rtw/rtw/@RTW/@BuildInfo/schema.pmatlab/toolbox/rtw/rtw/@RTW/@BuildInfo/updateFilePathsAndExtensions.pmatlab/toolbox/rtw/rtw/@RTW/@BuildInfo/updateFileSeparator.pmatlab/toolbox/rtw/rtw/rtw_c.mmatlab/toolbox/rtw/rtw/rtw_c.pmatlab/toolbox/rtw/rtw/tlc_c.mmatlab/toolbox/rtw/rtw/tlc_c.pmatlab/toolbox/rtw/rtw/private/rtwinfomatman.pmatlab/toolbox/rtw/rtw/@RTW/genMakefileAndBuild.mmatlab/toolbox/rtw/rtw/@RTW/genMakefileAndBuild.pRestart MATLAB.

Summary: Dragging blocks from one window to another on Mac OS X 10.5 ("Leopard") may crash Simulink
Description: Dragging a block from one window to another on Mac OS X 10.5 ("Leopard") can crash Simulink. The crash occurs when the mouse moves over any non-X11 window, including the Mac OS X desktop, during the drag. When the crash occurs, Apple's X11.app also crashes.
Workaround: To fix this issue in an R2007a, R2007a Student Version, or R2007b installation:Log into your Macintosh, using an account with Administrator privileges.Download the appropriate patch for your version from the patches attached to this report.Locate the download.If your web browser did not unzip it for you, double-click in the Finder to unzip the file.Double-click the disk image to mount it.At the top level of the mounted disk image is an application called "Install Patch." Double-click to launch it.You are prompted to select your MATLAB installation. Select the root folder of the install. The patch script attempts to locate this for you and preselects it in the folder chooser if it is successful.Follow the prompts to complete installation of the patch. You can unmount the disk image when you are done.This patch renames the following files (adding a suffix of ".old") in your MATLAB installation, if they are present:matlabroot/bin/mac/libmwuix.dylibmatlabroot/bin/mac/libmwuix.dylib.csfmatlabroot/bin/maci/libmwuix.dylibmatlabroot/bin/maci/libmwuix.dylib.csfIt then replaces them with updated versions.

Summary: iptaddcallback incorrectly dispatches callback functions defined as strings
Description: The iptaddcallback  function incorrectly evaluates callback functions that are defined as strings, if the callback depends on evaluation of variables. When MATLAB evaluates the following code block: a = 2;h_fig = figure('WindowButtonMotionFcn','disp(a)');imshow('pout.tif');iptaddcallback(h_fig,'WindowButtonMotionFcn',@(varargin) disp('fire'));  Moving the mouse pointer over the figure results in an error: ??? Error using ==> evalUndefined function or variable 'a'. Error in ==> iptaddcallback>callbackProcessor at 142 eval(fun); ??? Error while evaluating figure WindowButtonMotionFcn ?
Workaround: To workaround this issue, replace the version of the iptaddcallback function in yourcurrent installation with the updated version contained in the attached file.Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file. matlabroot\toolbox\shared\imageslib/iptaddcallback.mmatlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function.For example, you could rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension, iptaddcallback.m.old.Download the attached file, extract iptaddcallback.m, and store it in the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Specifying a fuzzy system using a fis file in the Fuzzy Logic Controller or the Fuzzy Logic Controller with Ruleviewer block may cause the block to initialize incorrectly
Description: When you specify a fuzzy system using a fis file in 'FIS file or structure' in the Fuzzy Logic Controller Block Parameters dialog box or 'FIS matrix' in the Fuzzy Logic Controller with Ruleviewer Block Parameters dialog box, the block may not initialize correctly and cause a wrong simulation result.
Workaround: Download the attachment and unzip its content to a temporary directory. Replace MATLABROOT\toolbox\fuzzy\fuzzy\fuzblockmask.m.

Summary: MATLAB crashes when the Target Language Compiler executes a FEVAL command
Description: MATLAB crashes when the Target Language Compiler executes a FEVAL statement that returns a NULL. For example, executing the following command crashes MATLAB: %assign lastError = FEVAL("lasterror")

Summary: Solaris 10 8/07: Opening a file in the MATLAB? Editor may cause MATLAB software to become unresponsive
Description: On Solaris?  10 8/07, if you attempt to open the MATLAB?  Editor, it may cause MATLAB to become unresponsive. In particular, this problem has been reported by customers who are working remotely through applications such as X-Win32, Xming, or Sun Ray using Xsun patch 119059-31.
Workaround: If possible, install the most recent Xsun patch (119059-32 or later).If the Xsun patch does not resolve the issue, then for MATLAB? Release 2007a and Release 2008b, install the attached patch by following these steps :Download the attached zip file and extract RobotUtils.class.Create the following subdirectories in the matlabroot/java directory, where matlabroot is the directory where MATLAB software is installed. (You can determine where MATLAB is installed by running the matlabroot function.)/patch/com/mathworks/mwswingPut RobotUtils.class in matlabroot/java/patch/com/mathworks/mwswing.Restart MATLAB.In the MATLAB Command Window, execute the following command to verify that the patch is properly installed. If the patch is installed, the answer is 0.com.mathworks.mwswing.RobotUtils.isSupportedRobotPlatformAfter applying the patch, you can no longer click and drag the splitter bar (above the scrollbar) to divide the Editor window into top and bottom views of your preferred sizes. Instead when you click the splitter bar, the screen is divided evenly, into two views of equal size. This is the only feature lost when you install the patch.

Summary: Specifying the -v option with emlc produces a spurious error.
Description: When you invoke emlc  with the -v  (verbose) option, emlc  correctly shows compilation steps during the build process, but generates a spurious error after completing the build. This error does not affect the status of the build.

Summary: SAMPLEALIGN exhausts memory when the distance function involves calculations that use all the columns of the input matrices. For example, when using the cross-correlation between two signals as the distance metric.
Description: In large datasets, such as in LCMS spectra, and when the 'BAND' and 'WIDTH' constraints are not carefully tuned, SAMPLEALIGN  might exhaust the available memory if the distance function involves calculations that use all the columns of the input matrices. For example, when using the cross-correlation between two signals as the distance metric.
Workaround: 1. In the MATLAB Command Window typewhich samplealignThis displays the location of the file samplealign.m.2. Rename samplealign.m to samplealign.old.3. Download the updated file attached to this bug report to the directory identified with the which command.4. Restart MATLAB and in the command window type:rehash toolboxcache?

Summary: imdivide, immultiply. and imabsdiff error on uint8, int16, or single empty matrices on certain machines.
Description: imdivide , immultiply . and imabsdiff  error on uint8 , int16 , or single  empty matrices on machines with Intel Architecture processors. For example: imdivide(uint8([]),uint8([]))??? Error using ==> imdivmexUnknown error occurred in IPPL. Error in ==> imdivide at 57 Z = imdivmex(X, Y); ?
Workaround: Use double empty matrices before calling immultiply, imdivide, or imabsdiff.For example:imdivide([],[])ans = []

Summary: M-Lint can produce inappropriate warnings on certain calls to fprintf, error, and warning
Description: To give correct warnings, M-Lint must correctly identify which of the arguments is the format argument. If you use expressions in the arguments to fprintf , error , or warning , M-Lint may incorrectly evaluate those arguments and produce an inappropriate message. For example, M-Lint displays the following message, Format string apparently does not agree with the argument count  when it evaluates the following code: x='1'; y='2'; fprintf(['%s', '%s'],x,y) ?
Workaround: You can suppress the erroneous message for a specific line of code using either one of the following methods:Place the %#ok pragma at the end of the code that elicits the behavior. For example:     x='1';      y='2';      fprintf(['%s' '%s'],x,y) %#okPlace the pointer to the left of the code with the red underlined text, right-click, and then select Ignore this "Format string apparently does not..." from the context menu. The Editor places the %#ok<CTPCT> pragma at the end of the code eliciting the behavior, as shown in the following example:    x='1';     y='2';     fprintf(['%s', '%s'],x,y) %#ok<CTPCT>Alternatively, you can suppress the message for all MATLAB-files by setting the M-Lint Preferences, as follows:Select File > Preferences > M-Lint.Under Select messages to enable, scroll to the category of messages entitled Unexpected or Suspicious Usage, and then clear the check box next to Format string apparently does not agree with the argument count.Click OK.Be aware that if you use this method, the message will be suppressed, even in cases where it is appropriate.

Summary: External mode: Connect to Target may throw error when Inline parameters off
Description: When the Simulink optimization option Inline parameters  is off for a model, an external mode Connect to Target  downloads all block parameter values to the target. However, the external mode connect may throw errors in the following cases: For ERT, GRT, GRT malloc, RSim, Real-Time Windows, and Tornado targets: If a downloaded parameter's data type does not have a registered host-to-target conversion function, the external mode connect throws an error and fails when accessing the external mode server on the target. For xPC targets: If a downloaded parameter's data type can not be converted to an mxArray , the external mode connect throws a memory allocation error but makes a valid connection.  An example of a parameter that triggers this behavior is the Initial conditions  (X0 ) parameter of a Rate Transition block that uses BusObject  as the data type of its input signal. The X0  parameter's data type therefore is BusObject , which does not have a host-to-target conversion function and cannot be converted to an mxArray .
Workaround: To work around this bug, select the Inline parameters option before connecting to the target.

Summary: Segmentation fault may occur when simulating or generating code for a Stateflow chart that references data store memory
Description: Simulating or generating code for a model may cause a segmentation fault if the following are true: The model contains a Stateflow chart that references a data store. You select Data store name must resolve to Simulink signal object . There is no signal object of the specified name.
Workaround: Define the missing Simulink signal object.

Summary: Printing figure made by plotyy function from the File menu fails and damages the figure
Description: When printing a figure created with plotyy  from the Print Preview Dialog, MATLAB 7.5 generates an error after any property in the Print Preview dialog is modified. The error results from deletion of an internal reference within the figure. This corrupts the figure, which must be recreated if it is to be used again. The error message displayed by MATLAB is: ??? Error using ==> restore at 50Error using ==> restore at 50Error using ==> setInvalid handle object.   Error in ==> print at 274pj = restore( pj, h );   Error in ==> printdlg at 84eval(pstr)   Error in ==> ppreview>onPrint at 924printdlg(fig);

Summary: Why isn't my National Instruments device PXI-6527 recognized by the Data Acquisition Toolbox software?
Description: I have installed a National Instruments PXI-6527 in my PC but the Data Acquisition Toolbox software does not see its Digital I/O subsystem.
Workaround: This is caused by a missing entry in the initialization file that describes the device's capability.To work around this issue, do the following:Quit MATLAB.Find the following file:  $MATLAB\toolbox\daq\daq\private\mwnidaqmx.ini where $MATLAB is the MATLAB root installation directory that you can obtain by issuing the following command in MATLAB: matlabrootMake a copy of this file and rename it to mwnidaqmx.ini.old.Open the file mwnidaqmx.ini in a text editor, and go to the bottom and add the following lines:???? [PXI-6527]hasAnalogInput?? = falsehasAnalogOutput? = falsehasDigitalIO???? = truedigitalPortSize? = 8,8,8,8,8,8digitalPortCap?? = 2,2,2,1,1,1Save the file mwnidaqmx.ini and restart MATLAB.

Summary: Compiling a standalone executable using DEPLOYTOOL, with 'Additional Include Directories' specified, causes an error.
Description: If any of the settings in the "C/C++ Compiler" panel (except generate debug flag) are set, DEPLOYTOOL fails to compile the project.
Workaround: Setting various compiler flags will generate an incorrect MBUILD command. Do the following to workaround this problem:After each failed compilation:1) Copy the MBUILD command as seen in the verbose output of the compilation process and paste it in the command window.2) Correct the values passed with the -I, -L, -l, -U, and -D flags.3) Execute the modified mbuild command.

Summary: Network outputs returned by TRAIN do not match outputs from SIM.
Description: On some occasions when users called TRAIN with output arguments that included the network output,the network output would be incorrect. One such case was when a static network was trained ondynamical data.
Workaround: The workaround is to not use TRAIN to calculate network outputs. First call TRAIN, then call SIM.The bug has been fixed, but it is no longer recommended that users call TRAINwith the network output as an output argument.

Summary: In trainlm there is an else clause that can never be executed.
Description: In trainlm  there is an if-elseif-elseif-else clause where both else-if conditions are identical, so that the second will never execute. 280 if isequal(net.performFcn,'msereg'),281 gXt = 2*gXt*net.performParam.ratio/numElementsA + 2*(1-net.performParam.ratio)*X/length(X);282 elseif isequal(net.performFcn,'mse'),283 gXt = 2*gXt/numElementsA;284 elseif isequal(net.performFcn,'mse'),285 gXt = 2*gXt;286 else287 flag_test = 0;288 end  This is an error in some optional testing code used by the developer and has no effect on use of trainlm . ?
Workaround: No need for a workaround. Code is test code that is never executed.?

Summary: UNIX: dbquit or exiting debug mode can terminate MATLAB unexpectedly if code being debugged is running from a callback
Description: On UNIX platforms, if code running in a callback stops at a breakpoint, and you run dbquit  or otherwise exit debugging, MATLAB can terminate unexpectedly. Here is a simple example: >> uicontrol('callback', 'ls'); >> dbstop ls;% Press the button on the uicontrol18 if iscellstr(varargin)K>> dbquit% MATLAB can terminate unexpectedly at this point You might also experience this problem if you select Yes  in a dialog that asks if you want to end debug mode.
Workaround: Using dbcont or dbstep to move through the rest of the code being debugged.

Summary: Lookup Table Editor rounds values specified in block dialog box of lookup table
Description: Suppose your model contains a lookup table block whose breakpoint vectors or table data specify values that have six or greater digits. Note that when you open the Lookup Table Editor to view or edit this data, the editor displays such values rounded to five digits. But if you then select Update Block Data  from either the editor's File  menu or toolbar, the editor replaces those values in your lookup table block using the less precise rounded values. For example, the editor replaces 1234567  with 1.2346e+006 ; similarly, it replaces 0.1234567  with 0.12346 .

Summary: Updating a Simulink diagram can cause some signal labels to disappear
Description: Updating a Simulink diagram can cause some signal labels to disappear if the diagram's background color is white. This is because the label text is incorrectly colored white.
Workaround: Change the background color of the diagram to a color other than white before editing signal labels.

Summary: Model Reference Normal Mode produces wrong results when SimDriveline block parameters used as model arguments
Description: SimDriveline?simulates incorrectly?when you use SimDriveline block parameters as model arguments in the Model Reference Normal Mode. (Simulation?does work correctly in Model Reference Accelerator Mode.)For example, if a parameter such as Inertia  in the SimDriveline Inertia block is specified as a model argument for the referenced SimDriveline model, an incorrect value of the Inertia  parameter is passed to the Inertia block. There is no warning or error indicating that this incorrect value was passed.

Summary: Macintosh? OS X 10.5 platforms: CGContext error message on startup from xterm or Terminal
Description: On Macintosh? OS X 10.5 platforms, MATLAB? displays the following CGContext error message when started from an xterm or Terminal window: MATLAB[297] : CGContextGetBaseCTM: invalid contextMATLAB[297] : CGContextConvertSizeToDeviceSpace: invalid context MATLAB[297] : CGContextConvertSizeToUserSpace: invalid context This error does not impact MATLAB, and you can ignore it.

Summary: Some Simulink windows exhibit visual problems on Mac OS X 10.5 ("Leopard")
Description: Some Simulink windows exhibit visual problems on Mac OS X 10.5 ("Leopard"). These issues include: incorrect colors used on tab controls incorrect colors used on toolbar icons incorrect colors used on menu icons inconsistent background colors in menus incorrect colors used on title bar icons incorrect and/or blinking background colors around dialog buttons  Windows affected by these problems include block parameter dialog boxes and the Model Explorer.

Summary: Bioinformatics Toolbox function RNAPLOT returns an error when drawing diagrams and dot diagrams if version 2.2 of the SimBiology software is installed.
Description: Bioinformatics Toolbox function RNAPLOT returns an error when drawing diagrams and dot diagrams if version 2.2 of the SimBiology software is installed. Previous versions of the SimBiology software (or the lack of it) do not affect the RNAPLOT function.
Workaround: 1. Quit MATLAB.2. Rename the file $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfo\rnaplot.m to $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfo\rnaplot.m.oldwhere $MATLAB$ is the directory where MATLAB is installed on your machine.3. Download the attached file to the directory $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfo\4. Restart MATLAB.5. At the MATLAB prompt, run the following command:rehash toolboxcache

Summary: Editing the name of a model workspace variable in the Model Explorer can crash Simulink on an Intel-based Macintosh
Description: When this occurs, the CrashReporter log in ~/Library/Logs/CrashReporter for MATLAB shows a stack trace containing similar to the following: Thread 0 Crashed: 0   libQtGui.4.dylib         0x3d72374b QWidget::inputContext() + 11 1   libQtGui.4.dylib         0x3d7237d0 QWidgetPrivate::focusInputContext() + 22 2   libQtGui.4.dylib         0x3d7238bd QWidget::setFocus(Qt::FocusReason) + 199 3   libQtGui.4.dylib         0x3d6fe951 QApplication::notify(QObject*, QEvent*) + 1613 4   libmwglee.dylib          0x4c842e0e GuiApplication::notify(QObject*, QEvent*) + 116 (GuiApplication.cpp:1333) 5   libQtGui.4.dylib         0x3d733234 QApplicationPrivate::globalEventProcessor(OpaqueEventHandlerCallRef*, OpaqueEventRef*, void*) + 7866 ...

Summary: ERT S-function wrapper creation fails with Microsoft Visual C++ if model uses ERT-based custom target and default ERT template makefiles
Description: A model build using the Microsoft Visual C++ compiler may fail if the following conditions are true: ERT S-function wrapper creation is selected for the model (that is, option Create Simulink (S-Function) block  is selected on the Interface  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box). The model uses an ERT-based custom target. The model uses the default ERT template makefiles for Microsoft Visual C++.  The build error does not  occur if the the model uses the default ERT target (ert.tlc ) or a custom template makefile.
Workaround: To work around this bug, perform the following steps:Note: In the following instructions, matlabroot represents your MATLAB installation root, as returned by the MATLAB command matlabroot.In your MATLAB installation tree, rename the following files, for example, by adding .old to the end of each file name.matlabroot/rtw/c/ert/ert_vc.tmfmatlabroot/rtw/c/ert/ert_vcx64.tmf?Download the attached files and copy them into the same directory as the files you renamed.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: DNDSML returns unfeasible synonymous and nonsynonymous substitution rate estimates on certain edge cases.
Description: DNDSML  returns unfeasible rate estimates between homologous sequences in the following edge cases: Sequences are equal. Sequences do not contain any synonymous substitutions. Sequences do not contain any nonsynonymous substitutions. Sequences do not contain any transversion substitutions. Sequences do not contain any transition substitutions. Any combination of the preceding cases.  These edge cases are not detected a priori, and the numeric algorithm that maximizes the likelihood fails to estimate omega and/or kappa. See formula (6) in Yang, Z. and Nielsen, R. "Estimating synonymous and nonsynonymous substitution rates under realistic evolutionary models." Mol. Biol. Evol. 17(1):32-43, 2000.
Workaround: 1. In the MATLAB Command Window typewhich dndsThis displays the location of the files dnds.m and dndsml.m.2. Rename dnds.m to dnds.old and dndsml.m to dndsml.old.3. Download the updated files attached to this bug report to the directory identified with the which command.4. Restart MATLAB and in the command window type:rehash toolboxcache?

Summary: ccsboardinfo returns incorrect processor information when boards have multiple processors
Description: Function ccsboardinfo  returns incorrect board and processor information when you try to access boards that contain more than one processor. These errors cause Embedded IDE Link CC to connect to the wrong processor when you use ccsdsp . The assignment errors also prevent you from generating code for the correct processor from your Simulink models because the Target Preferences block does not identify the processors correctly. For example, when you use a board with four processors, ccsboardinfo  returns the following listing: ccsboardinfoBoard Board                    Proc Processor      ProcessorNum   Name                     Num  Name           Type ---  ------------------------ ---- -------------  ----------- 0    C64xx XDS510USB Emulator  0   TMS320C6400_2  TMS320C6400  0    C64xx XDS510USB Emulator  1   TMS320C6400_3  TMS320C6400  0    C64xx XDS510USB Emulator  2   TMS320C6400_4  TMS320C6400  0    C64xx XDS510USB Emulator  3   TMS320C6400_1  TMS320C6400 ccsboardinfo  returns the processor numbers improperly. Processor TMS320C6400_1 is reported as Proc Num 3. The correct Proc Num is 0. Processor TMS320C6400_2 is reported as Proc Num 0. The correct Proc Num is 1. Processor TMS320C6400_3 is reported as Proc Num 1. The correct Proc Num is 2. Processor TMS320C6400_4 is reported as Proc Num 2. The correct Proc Num is 3.
Workaround: To fix this problem, download attachment_407507_20080307.zip from the Attachments.Open the attachment and extract the following patch files to your MATLAB installation directory:patch_installer.mpatch_407507.zipFollow these steps to update your MATLAB installation with the patch.1. Start MATLAB.2. In MATLAB, change the Current folder to the location of the patch files.3. Enter the following command at the MATLAB prompt:patch_installer('patch_407507.zip','install')This command installs the patch and updates your installation.4. Close MATLAB and restart.

Summary: Incorrect cut sites when using REBASECUTS and RESTRICT functions with certrain enzymes
Description: The cut sites reported by the REBASECUTS and RESTRICT functions are incorrect?. The affected enzymes are:Ajnl, AlfI, AloI, BaeI, BcgI, BfuCI, BplI, BsaXI, Bsp24I, Bsp143I, BssKI, BstMBI, BstSCI, CjeI, CjePI, DpnII, EcoHI, EcoRII, FalI, FatI, HaeIV, Hin4I, Kzo9I, MaeIII, MboI, NdeII, NmuCI,PpiI, Psp6I, PspGI,?PsrI, Sau3AI, SelI, Sse9I, StyD4I, TasI, Tsp45I, Tsp509I, TspEI, TstI, UnbI, VpaK11AI. ?
Workaround: Download the attached? files.Quit MATLABRename the file $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfo\restrict.m to $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfo\restrict.m.old.Rename the file $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfo\rebasecuts.m to $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfo\rebasecuts.m.old.Where $MATLAB is the directory where MATLAB is installed on your machine.Copy the downloaded files to the directory $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfoRestart MATLABAt the MATLAB prompt, run the following command:rehash toolboxcache.

Summary: Zooming on a plot created using plotyy and linked using linkaxes causes an error
Description: Attempting to zoom in on an axes which has been created using the plotyy  function and which has its two axes synchronized using the linkaxes  function fails. For example, the code AX = plotyy(1:10,rand(1,10),1:10,rand(1,10));linkaxes(AX);zoom on; generates the following error: ??? Error using ==> horzcatCAT arguments dimensions are not consistent.Error in ==> uitools.uimode.modeControl>localModeWindowButtonDownFcn at 127This.modeWindowButtonDownFcn(hFig,evd,hThis,newValue);??? Error while evaluating figure WindowButtonDownFcn
Workaround: Using the linkaxes function to synchronize y-axes plotted by plotyy is no longer necessary. Removing the call to linkaxes prevents the error. Alternatively,?you can work around the issue using the following steps:1.?Exit MATLAB2. Rename the file?$MATLAB/toolbox/matlab/graphics/resetplotview.m to $MATLAB/toolbox/matlab/graphics/resetplotview.m.old, where $MATLAB is the root directory of your MATLAB installation, as returned by running the?commandmatlabrootfrom the MATLAB command prompt.3. Download the attached file, resetplotview.m to the above directory.4. Start MATLAB5. Run the commandrehash toolboxcache?

Summary: Error occurs when opening the Model Parameter Configuration dialog box
Description: When you open the Model Parameter Configuration dialog box, the following error might occur if the MATLAB Workspace is empty: ?? Attempt to reference field of non-structure array.
Workaround: Create a variable in the MATLAB Workspace before opening the Model Parameter Configuration dialog box.

Summary: Enabling conditional input execution optimization when a Switch Block's execution branch is contained within an algebraic loop causes a segmentation violation to occur.
Description: A segmentation violation will occur in Simulink duing simulation if a Switch Block's execution branch is included in an Algebraic loop, and Conditional input execution optimization  is selected.
Workaround: To workaround this problem, deselect the Conditional input branch execution checkbox located in the Optimization pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog:From the Simulink toolbar, select SimulationClick on Configuration ParametersFrom the list on the left, select the Optimization paneLocate the Conditional input branch execution checkbox, and clear the checkmarkClick OK to accept the change and close the dialog?

Summary: Digital Filter block First denominator coefficient = 1, remove a0 term in the structure check box is not persistent
Description: This bug occurs when you perform the following steps: Open the Digital Filter block and select Input port(s) as the coefficient source Clear the First denominator coefficient = 1, remove a0 term in the structure  check box. Click OK . Open the Digital Filter block dialog box again. The First denominator coefficient = 1, remove a0 term in the structure  check box is selected.
Workaround: To work around this issue:Select the Digital Filter block by clicking on it once.Type set_param(gcb,'denIgnore','off') at the MATLAB command line to clear the check box.Type set_param(gcb,'denIgnore','on') at the MATLAB command line to select the check box.

Summary: When a Truth Table block is linked from a library to the main model, the Truth Table Editor is not always locked for the link truth table.
Description: After simulating a model, the Truth Table Editor can become unlocked for a Truth Table block that is linked from a library to the the main model. As a result, that link truth table can be edited. However, it should not be editable.
Workaround: Do not edit a link truth table before disabling the library link, even if the Truth Table Editor allows the changes.

Summary: There is an error in the sample time used in the demo "IEEE 802.11a WLAN Physical Layer".
Description: There is an error in the sample time used in the demo "IEEE 802.11a WLAN Physical Layer". The pilot bits and data bits are generated at a rate as if they were no pilot samples.
Workaround: step one:use the attached file comm80211a_settings.m to replace the following one:matlab/toolbox/commblks/commblksdemos/commwlan80211a_settings.mstep two:open the mask of (double click on)commwlan80211a/Pilots/PN Sequence Generatorand change Sample timefrom params.OFDMSymbolPeriod to params.OFDMPilotPeriod

Summary: Using memmapfile to access the same file from multiple MATLAB sessions simultaneously leads to a "process cannot access the file" error.
