Description: On Microsoft Windows platforms, using the memmapfile class to access the same file from multiple MATLAB sessions simultaneously can cause an error. This error displays the message: ??? Error using ==> hMapFileCannot map file "filename ": The process cannot access the file because it is being used by another process.  The memmapfile object for at least one of the MATLAB sessions must have its Writable property set to true for this to occur.

Summary: Passing function name as a string to the output or plot function produces error
Description: When I provide an output or plot function as a string to the optimization solver, I get an error.
Workaround: You can avoid this error by using a function handle instead of a string for an output or plot function. Here is an example that uses the function 'optimplotfval' as a plot function:?options = optimset('PlotFcn', @optimplotfval);fminunc(@sin,1,options);

Summary: Compiler warnings are generated when using Microsoft MSDEV 8.0 (.NET 2005) to build xPC Target models
Description: When you compile xPC Target models using the Microsoft MSDEV 8.0 compiler, the compiler generates warnings for: Ignoring the /Op  flag. In earlier versions of this compiler, the /Op  flag directed the compiler to perform some floating point code optimizations. In the new compiler, the compiler performs these optimizations all the time and the compiler now warns about the presence of the flag. Use of several standard C library functions such sprintf() , which can overrun a buffer.
Workaround: The MathWorks has concluded that you can safely ignore these warnings.

Summary: Incorrect code generation: Generated code fails to compile for a reusable subsystem
Description: The generated code fails to compile when a reusable subsystem has an input signal that is an array of structures. You encounter an error message such as the following: Error codereuse_struct.c: 40?? operands of = have illegal types `myStruct' and `pointer to const myStruct' Error codereuse_struct.c: 41?? operands of = have illegal types `myStruct' and `pointer to const myStruct' Error codereuse_struct.c: 48?? illegal expression Error codereuse_struct.c: 48?? syntax error; found `codereuse_struct_U' expecting `)' Error codereuse_struct.c: 48?? insufficient number of arguments to `codereuse_struct_Subsystem' Error codereuse_struct.c: 48?? syntax error; found `codereuse_struct_U' expecting `;' Warning codereuse_struct.c: 49?? Statement has no effect Error codereuse_struct.c: 49?? syntax error; found `)' expecting `;' Error codereuse_struct.c: 49?? illegal statement termination Error codereuse_struct.c: 49?? skipping `)' Warning codereuse_struct.c: 75?? Statement has no effect
Workaround: If you are using Real-Time Workshop versions R2007a or R2007a+, please read the following:Replace the file matlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/mw/commonpass.tlc as follows:Determine your MATLAB root directory by entering the matlabroot command at the MATLAB Command Line.Quit MATLAB.Rename matlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/mw/commonpass.tlc to matlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/mw/commonpass.tlc.old.Download the attached file attachment_406964_20080508.zip and extract commonpass_g406964_for_7a.tlc from it, placing it in the directory matlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/mw and renaming it to matlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/mw/commonpass.tlcRestart MATLAB.If you are using Real-Time Workshop versions R2007b, please read the following:Replace the file matlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/mw/commonpass.tlc as follows:Determine your MATLAB root directory by entering the matlabroot command at the MATLAB Command Line.Quit MATLAB.Rename matlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/mw/commonpass.tlc to matlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/mw/commonpass.tlc.old.Download the attached file attachment_406964_20080508.zip and extract commonpass_g406964_for_7b.tlc from it, placing it in the directory matlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/mw and renaming it to matlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/mw/commonpass.tlcRestart MATLAB.There are currently no known workarounds for other versions of Real-Time Workshop.

Summary: Array Editor sometimes truncates strings in mixed char and numeric cell arrays
Description: The Array Editor sometimes truncates the display of strings contained in cell arrays. This happens when the cell arrays contain both string and numeric values. For example, when you run a = {'1234567890123456789012345678901234567890', 2}; openvar a the Array Editor shows '''123456789012345678901234567890123456789' 2 Note that the final 0  is missing from the display of the string. This is a display issue only; MATLAB? maintains the full value in the workspace.
Workaround: To see the full value for the variable, type the variable name in the Command Window. For the above example, typea(1)and MATLAB displaysans =  '1234567890123456789012345678901234567890'?

Summary: NAII 76CS1 synchro/resolver board ignores changes to the excitation frequency
Description: You cannot use the NAII 76CS1 synchro/resolver blocks to change the NAII 76CS1 synchro/resolver board excitation frequency to a value different?from the hardware default 360 Hz.? The board continues to?operate at the default frequency.
Workaround: As a workaround, if you need to use a different excitation frequency,?contact MathWorks Technical Support about the possibility of the creation of a modified driver for the NAII 76CS1 synchro/resolver board.

Summary: Published LaTeX equation is a duplicate of another equation, rather than the specified equation
Description: Sometimes, when you include a LaTeX equation in a MATLAB-File and then publish that MATLAB-File, that equation is not published. Instead, another equation in the MATLAB-file is duplicated. For example, suppose a MATLAB-File contains the following code:    %% LaTeX Equations:   %   % $$ A = B - C $$   %   % $$ B = C \times A $$   %   % $$ C = A - B $$  If you publish the MATLAB-File, by selecting File  > Publish to HTML , the Web page contains the text in the example that follows. Notice that the third equation is a duplicate of the first equation, instead of the specified equation.    LaTeX Equations:    A = B - C   B = C X A   A = B - C
Workaround: In matlabroot, replace \toolbox\matlab\codetools\private\hashEquation.m with the attached file. (The matlabroot is the directory where MATLAB is installed, which you can determine by running the matlabroot function.)?

Summary: Sparse, non-complex matrices, stored in V4 format are incorrectly reported as complex
Description: For a sparse, non-complex matrix, stored in a V4 format MAT-file, when you run whos -file in the MATLAB? language, or matGetVariableInfo in the C language, the result incorrectly identifies the matrix as complex. When you load the matrix using load  in MATLAB or matGetVariable  in C, the result is correct.

Summary: TMTOOL does not find USB and TCPIP instruments while scanning.
Description: ? TMTOOL does not find USB or TCP/IP instruments
Workaround: You must enable TMTOOL to discover USB and TCP/IP objects before you perform a scan. To enable TMTOOL to discover USB and TCP/IP objects:Download the attached file.perform the following steps in MATLAB after downloading the attached file:cd(matlabroot);zip('backup.zip','toolbox/instrument/instrument/instrhwinfo.m'); % This stores a backup copy of the original fileunzip('instrhwinfo.zip');Optionally, to restore to the original configuration, type:cd(matlabroot);unzip('backup.zip');

Summary: For?the North Atlantic Industries NAII 76CS1 board,? you can only set the excitation voltage?to the nearest volt
Description: For the North Atlantic Industries NAII 76CS1 board, you can only set the excitation voltage to the nearest volt. Attempts to set the excitation voltage to a level that is not an integer number of volts causes the level to be rounded down to the integer part of the voltage.
Workaround: As a workaround, if you need to set the excitation voltage to non-integral one, contact MathWorks Technical Support about the possibility of the creation of a modified driver for the NAII 76CS1 board.

Summary: Stateflow charts that execute at initialization cannot access data store memory in entry actions or default transitions
Description: When you configure a chart to execute at initialization, you cannot access data store memory or input data in state entry actions or default transitions. Attempting these actions causes a segmentation violation during Real-Time Workshop code generation.The reason for the restriction is that charts execute certain state entry actions and default transitions during initialization.
Workaround: Turn off the "Execute at initialization" option for your chart or remove access to data store memory and input data in state entry actions and default transitions.

Summary: Inserting impixelregionpanel into a figure disables the 'HitTest' property of the other children of the figure.
Description: When you add impixelregionpanel  to a figure, the 'HitTest'  property of all the children of the figure is set to 'off' , causing any 'ButtonDownFcn'  callbacks defined in the figure to behave unexpectedly. Any children you add to the figure after adding impixelregionpanel  will also have their 'HitTest'  property set to 'off' .
Workaround: To workaround this issue, replace the version of the impixelregionpanel function in yourcurrent installation with the updated version contained in the attached file.Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file. matlabroot\toolbox\images\imuitools\impixelregionpanel.mmatlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function.For example, you could rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension, impixelregionpanel.m.old.Download the attached file, extract impixelregionpanel.m, and store it in the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: The sim command in rapid accelerator mode delivers limited speed up
Description: Running a model in Rapid Acclerator mode using the sim  command may not improve the simulation speed
Workaround: Running your model in Rapid Accelerator mode should result in significanlty shorter run-times than using Normal mode.If not, first review the "Comparing Performance" chapter in the Simulink documentation to determine if your model is a good candidate for improvement with the Rapid Acceleration mode.?If you decide that it is, this bug could be causing the improvement to be less than expected. If so, use the attached file to replace the file: <matlabroot>\rtw\c\raccel\raccel_sup.c

Summary: The BCH and RS Encoder?blocks error out when the product of the input frame size and the message size exceeds 2^31-1.? The BCH and RS Decoder blocks error out when the product of the input frame size and the codeword size exceeds 2^31-1.?
Description: The BCH and RS Encoder and Decoder blocks can operate with message lengths up to 65533 symbols long and codeword lengths up to 65535 symbols long.? The encoders accept frame-based signals with lengths that are integer multiples of the message size.? The decoders accept frame-based signals with lengths that are integer multiples of the codeword size.? The encoder blocks will error out if the product of the input frame size and the message size exceeds 2^31-1 (the maximum value for an int32).? The decoder blocks will error out if the product of the input frame size and the codeword size exceeds 2^31-1.? The error will read similarly to the text below:"Error in port widths or dimensions.? Invalid dimension has been specified for output port 1 of '<modelname>/BCH Encoder'."
Workaround: You can replace the mex files that implement the encoder and decoder blocks with the attached files, appropriate for your platform.? Specifically, perform the following steps:Navigate to $matlabroot/toolbox/commblks/commmex on your computer.Replace scombchrsencoder.mexXXX, scombchrsencoder.mexXXX.csf, scomberlekamp.mexXXX, and scomberlekamp.mexXXX.csf with the files of the same name in the attachment of this bug report.? The string 'XXX' corresponds to your platform, as follows: ?a64 - 64-bit Linuxglx - 32-bit Linuxmaci - Intel Macs64 - 64-bit Solarisw32 - 32-bit Windowsw64 - 64-bit WindowsAt the MATLAB command prompt, type 'bdclose all' to close all open Simulink models. ?At the MATLAB command prompt, type 'clear mex' to remove any existing mex-functions from memory.

Summary: Cut and undo in the Stateflow Editor can result in data loss and syntax highlighting issues
Description: If you cut a state/transition or group of them and then undo the operation, syntax highlighting is gone. Also, any events associated with a state that was part of the cut/undo operation is lost.
Workaround: 1) The lost event information must be manually restored.2) The syntax highlighting for everything else can be restored by clicking on the states and transition labels, forcing them to refresh.

Summary: Real-Time Workshop can generate code that does not compile correctly if the model?includes function-calls?that are involved in self loops and have wide inputs.
Description: The Real-Time Workshop software generates code that cannot be compiled if all of the following conditons are met in a function-call subsystem: Subsystem has inputs that come from within its own context, or from other function-call contexts that are called from within it (function-call contexts are expained in the Workaround section). Subsystem has wide inputs (vector/matrix, not bus inputs) that have non-constant sample time. Subsystem is set to generate a reusable function.  See the attached model for an example?of this bug.
Workaround: It is not?good modeling practice to use function-call subsystems with inputs that are context-dependent.? Such inputs can change during execution of the funciton-call, and cause the behavior of the function-call to be dependent on how it is connected externally to other blocks. See 'sl_subsys_fcncallerr12.mdl', that ships with Simulink, for a more detailed explanation of this issue. The recommended workaround for this bug is to modify the function-call subsystem to elliminate context-dependent inputs.?You can do this by performing the following steps:Select the Warn if function-call inputs are context dependent checkbox on the subsystem dialog.Ensure that the model level setting for the Context Dependent Inputs option in the Diagnostics pane (Diagnostics > Connectivity > Function-calls) of the block-diagram is NOT set to Disable all.Compile the block-diagram and identify all warnings that are referring to the function-call subsystem in question.Use the Latch inputs by copying inside signal option on all function-call input port blocks that are indicated in the warnings.This should fix the Real-Time Workshop compile error.For example, in the attached model, you can set the Latch inputs by copying inside signal option on the input port block 'In2' of the subsystem to workaround the bug.

Summary: unable to write or read binary uint32 data from TMTOOL using interface objects
Description: When you are using an interface object (eg. serial, GPIB, VXI, GPIB-VXI, TCPIP(VXI-11), USB, TCPIP, or UDP) and you selct "binary" for the data type and "unsigned integer 32 bits (uint32)" for data format, the operation fails and displays the following error: "An error occurred while communicating with the instrument"
Workaround: To workaround this issue you can either use the Instrument Control Toolbox FREAD and FWRITE commands to read and write "uint32" data or you can download the attached patch.? To apply the patch, follow these steps:Download the attached ZIP-file.Shutdown MATLAB.Extract the ZIP-file into the directory <MATLABROOT>\java.?  <MATLABROOT> is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by executing the command matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This should create the following directory structure <MATLABROOT>\java\patch\com\mathworks\toolbox\instrument\instrcomm. There should be two files named CommunicationPanel$1.class and CommunicationPanel.class in this directory. If there is already a patch directory with files in it, your ZIP-file extraction program will normally leave those files intact and just add these new files to the folder. We recommend that you back up files already existing in the patch directory prior to extracting the new patch.Restart MATLAB.This patch should work with Instrument Control Toolbox 2.4 (R2006a) and later but may not work with earlier versions.

Summary: DEPLOYTOOL C/C++ Compiler settings not being saved correctly
Description: Settings used in the C/C++ Compiler screen in the Settings panel of Deploytool  are not saved correctly in all cases.?
Workaround: Rather then entering text directly into the text boxes, use the "..." button to the right of each box.? This will?save changes consistently.

Summary: ACTXCONTROLSELECT generates an error when you create an ActiveX control
Description: When you use the ACTXCONTROLSELECT command to invoke the interactive tool to create an ActiveX control and assign callbacks to certain events of the control, you might get the following error when you close the tool window: ??? Index exceeds matrix dimensions.Error in ==> actxcontrolselect>actxcontrolselect_OutputFcn at 129 handler =deblank(fliplr(deblank(handles.EventCallback{i,2})));Error in ==> gui_mainfcn at 265 feval(gui_State.gui_OutputFcn, gui_hFigure, [], gui_Handles);Error in ==> actxcontrolselect at 35 gui_mainfcn(gui_State, varargin{:});
Workaround: Download the attached actxcontrolselect.m file and replace the one comes with the R2007b installation with it to this location:MATLABROOT\toolbox\matlab\winfun\actxcontrolselect.mwhere, MATLABROOT is the directory you installed MATLAB In. You can find this out by issuing the MATLAB command MATLABROOT.

Summary: On Linux 64-bit platform, MATLAB errors out if the customer changes the FPU control word in MEX
Description: On Linux 64-bit platform, after the customer modified FPU control word in a MEX function, MATLAB errored out.
Workaround: Use the following workaround to cleanup FPU flags and control word before returning to MATLAB:#define Initialize_FPU() __asm__ ("fninit")#define Clear_FPU_Exceptions() __asm__ ("fnclex")#define Store_FPU_Control_Word(cw) __asm__ ("fnstcw %0" : "=m" (*&cw))#define Load_FPU_Control_Word(cw) __asm__ ("fldcw %0" : : "m" (*&cw))void mexFunction( int nlhs, mxArray *plhs[], int nrhs, const mxArray*prhs[] ){ unsigned short lcw;Initialize_FPU(); Store_FPU_Control_Word(lcw);... MEX function body ...Clear_FPU_Exceptions(); Load_FPU_Control_Word(lcw);}

Summary: MPlay (Floating mode) disconnects from Simulink model when user selects a 'no signal' area
Description: If you connect MPlay to a Simulink Model, enable Floating mode, run the simulation, and then click on any part of the model that is not a signal path, MPlay disconnects from the Simulink model and the control MPlay toolbar is disabled.
Workaround: Replace the file selectChangeEventHandler.m (located in the matlab/toolbox/shared/spcuilib/@scopeextensions/@DataConnectSimulink/ directory) with the file provided in the Attachments section, and re-launch MPlay. The MPlay toolbar will remain active if user clicks on a 'no signal' area of the Simulink model. This will allow the user to re-establish floating mode. User can then select a signal in the Simulink model, and view it in MPlay.

Summary: Unit Delay block with a state identifier specified does not accept a bus signal
Description: If a Unit Delay block has a state identifier specified, and the input to the block is a virtual bus, Simulink will attempt to implicitly convert the bus to a vector. If the bus cannot be converted to a vector, Simulink will report an error stating that the conversion is impossible. If the bus can be converted to a vector, the output of the Unit Delay block will also be a vector. Any downstream blocks that expect a bus signal (like Bus Selector or Bus Assignment) will report an error stating that a bus signal is expected at the input port of the block.

Summary: Memory leak when using COM Automation Server PutWorkspaceData function in MATLAB
Description: We detected a memory leak in the MATLAB COM Automation Server PutWorkspaceData function. This can affect the system performance when large ammounts of data are passed to the MATLAB from client applications via this API.

Summary: Local Resistance block throws an error at start of simulation
Description: At the start of simulation, Local Resistance blocks in your model may produce the following error:? Reference to non-existent field 'x_kp_d'.
Workaround: Download the attached file local_resistance.p, then copy it into the following directory, overwriting the existing file:$MATLABROOT/toolbox/physmod/simscape/sh/library/local_resistances/where $MATLABROOT is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by enteringmatlabrootin the MATLAB Command Window.

Summary: Zooming out past a maximum in 3-D makes it not possible to programatically zoom back in
Description: If the 'CameraViewAngle'  property of a 3-D axes?is?greater than 75 (degrees),?further zooming in or out via the scroll wheel or by calling the zoom  function is no longer possible. Using the figure's Zoom tool, however,?does work. This can happen as a result of setting the property directly. The issue does not occur for 2-D axes.
Workaround: To work around this issue, either click or drag the mouse to zoom with the Zoom tool turned on, or set the 'CameraViewAngle'? property of the axes to a value less than 75.

Summary: Real-Time Workshop software fails to generate code for models with Stateflow charts
Description: The Real-Time Workshop software might fail to generate code for models with Stateflow charts that execute outputs at initialization. When this occurs, MATLAB displays the following error message at the command line: Error building Real-Time Workshop target for block diagram 'model_name '. MATLAB error message:Error using ==> tlc_newError: File: file_location/file_name Unable to find DeclareInStartFcnScope within the LocalBlockOutput scope.
Workaround: Clear the Execute (enter) Chart At Initialization check box in the Stateflow chart properties dialog.

Summary: Crash upon double-clicking a Tire block in a library
Description: MATLAB might?crash if you try to open a Tire block inside a library.
Workaround: Copy the attached file to the following location, overwriting your installed version of the file:/matlab/toolbox/physmod/pm_util/pm_sli/pmsl_getblocklibraryentry.p

Summary: Why does a long function name mask other, shorter, function names if it is first in the parameter list of an mcc command?
Description: I am compiling a shared library using MATLAB Compiler. When I use the following command: mcc -vN -W lib:libML -T link:lib a ab  both functions are added to the generated library. However, if I reorder the command as follows: mcc -vN -W lib:libML -T link:lib ab a  only ab  is exported in the library. It appears that a long symbol name can mask a shorter one if both of the following conditions are met: 1. The longer function name is first in the parameter list 2. The shorter function name is a substring of the longer function name and contains the same initial characters as the longer function name
Workaround: To workaround this problem, do either of the following:1. Change the order in which the functions are mentioned in the mcc command so that the shorter function name appears before the longer function name2. Change the name of the shorter function name so it is not contained within the initial characters of the longer function name.

Summary: Memory leak in COM object exposing a global variable
Description: A memory leak appears when a MATLAB Builder NE COM object repeatedly changes and then accesses an MCR global variable. MATLAB-code, such as this, will exhibit this error when compiled: function [meanOut] = GetMean() global data; meanOut = mean(data(:));
Workaround: Rewrite the MATLAB-code, if possible, so that it does not make use of the global variable.For example, instead of using:function [meanOut] = GetMean() global data; meanOut = mean(data(:));Use this instead:function [meanOut] = GetMean(data) meanOut = mean(data(:));

Summary: Compile error for model containing S-function block that uses bus for input or output
Description: If you generate an S-function block from a model or subsystem in which a bus is used for the S-function input or output, the block can be incorporated in another model and used successfully for simulations. However, building the model results in a compile-time error similar to the following:  Error ../Subsystem_sf.c: ..\Subsystem_sfcn_rtw\Subsystem_sf.c: 165 unknown field `genericFcn' of `struct _ssMdlInfo'  The error occurs because the generated code for the model calls S-function simulation data structure (SimStruct) macros, which are not supported for standalone code generation.
Workaround: To work around this bug in R2007b, perform the following steps:Note: In the following instructions, matlabroot represents your MATLAB installation root, as returned by the MATLAB command matlabroot.1.??In your MATLAB installation tree, rename the following file, for example, by adding .old to the end of the file name.   matlabroot/simulink/include/simstruc.h2.??Download the attached file simstruc.h and copy it to it to the same directory as the file you renamed.3.??Remove any previously generated code directories related to the generated S-function.4.??Regenerate the S-function.

Summary: read method runs out of memory when reading large n-port Touchstone? files
Description: When you use the read  method to read data from a large n-port Touchstone file, you may encounter the following error: ??? Out of memory. Type HELP MEMORY for your options.
Workaround: You can work around this issue by replacing the read.m file in your current installation as follows:Get the name of the MATLAB installation directory, matlabroot, as returned by the matlabroot function.Exit MATLAB.Rename the following file:matlabroot\toolbox\rf\rf\@rfdata\@data\read.mto: matlabroot\toolbox\rf\rf\@rfdata\@data\read.m.oldThis creates a backup copy of the file.Download the attached file, read.m, to the directory specified in the previous step.Start MATLAB.Run the rehash toolboxcache command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: The Database Toolbox R2007b 'fastinsert'? function runs more slowly than in previous releases.
Description: The Database Toolbox R2007b 'fastinsert'? function runs more slowly than in previous releases.
Workaround: The attached file, $MATLAB/toolbox/database/database/@database/fastinsert.m, addresses this performance problem.

Summary: Code generation fails with messages including "Unable to find CanonicalOutputArgDef within the origInterface scope" and "Arguments to ISFIELD must be records"
Description: Code generation can fail for a model if all of the following conditions are true: The model contains a nonvirtual subsystem, from which output is passed to a Merge block. Additionally, the nonvirtual subsystem contains a reusable subsystem. The reusable subsystem outputs a signal that passes through at least one outport in the containing nonvirtual subsystem. The nonvirtual subsystem outport through which the signal passes specifies an Initial output  value other than [] . In other words, an initial condition is defined between the actual source of the signal and the Merge block.  During code generation, Real-Time Workshop displays errors similar to the following: Error: File: C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2007b\rtw\c\tlc\mw\opaquelib.tlc Line: 362 Column: 36Unable to find CanonicalOutputArgDef within the Interface scopeError: File: C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2007b\rtw\c\tlc\mw\opaquelib.tlc Line: 362 Column: 58The argument for an [] operation must be a repeated scope symbol, a vector, or a matrixError: File: C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2007b\rtw\c\tlc\lib\sysarglib.tlc Line: 59 Column: 21Arguments to ISFIELD must be recordsError: File: C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2007b\rtw\c\tlc\lib\sysarglib.tlc Line: 59 Column: 3Arguments to %addtorecord must be records### Real-Time Workshop build procedure for model: 'model' aborted due to an error.Error using ==> tlc_newError: File: C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2007b\rtw\c\tlc\lib\sysarglib.tlc Line: 67 Column: 21Arguments to ISFIELD must be records
Workaround: To work around this bug, you can perform either of the following actions:Remove the Initial output value from the nonvirtual subsystem outport and place it on the reusable subsystem outport.Remove the Initial output values from all outports and place the initial value on the Merge block.

Summary: Stateflow Search and Replace dialog uses default text color
Description: Text in the Stateflow Search and Replace dialog is incorrectly colored if the default text color is not black. For example, the problem occurs if you issue the following command: set(0, 'DefaultTextColor', Non-BlackColor );
Workaround: Locate the statement in your code that sets default text color, and either remove it or set the color back to black. In particular, verify that the default text color is not modified in your startup.m file. The following command sets the default text color to black:set(0, 'DefaultTextColor', [0 0 0]); % Color black is [0 0 0]

Summary: The Bioinformatics Toolbox function graphmaxflow runs very slow when the min-cut solution (third output argument) is required
Description: The Bioinformatics Toolbox function?graphmaxflow  runs very slow when the min-cut solution (third output argument) is required . This problem is also manifested in the maxflow method for the biograph object.

Summary: Changing 'XDir' or 'YDir' of an axes that contains an imrect ROI results in an error.
Description: If you add an imrect  rectangular ROI to an axes and then modify the 'XDir'  or 'YDir'  properties of the axes, the following error occurs: h_fig = figure;h_ax = axes;h_rect = imrect(gca,[.2 .2 .5 .5]);set(h_ax,'YDir','reverse'); ??? Error using ==> imuitools/private/wingedRect>setupCursorManagement/setupCornerPointerBehavior Too many input arguments.
Workaround: To workaround this issue, replace the version of the wingedRect.m private function in yourcurrent installation with the updated version contained in the attached file.Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file. matlabroot\toolbox\images\imuitools\private\wingedRect.mmatlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function.For example, you could rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension, wingedRect.m.old.Download the attached file, extract wingedRect.m, and store it in the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: PackNGo zip file generated for rapid simulation model omits rsim.h
Description: If the packNGo  function is called during post code generation build processing for a model that is configured for the rapid simulation (RSim) target, the zip file generated by packNGo  omits the required file rsim.h .
Workaround: To work around this bug, in the post code generation command, prior to calling packNGo on the build information object, use a set_param call such as the following to add the rtw/c/rsim target directory to the build information object:set_param(model,'PostCodeGenCommand','addIncludePaths(buildInfo,fullfile(matlabroot,''rtw'',''c'',''rsim''),''Standard''); packNGo(buildInfo);')where model is the name of your model in single-quotes.

Summary: Linearizing a model with a signal viewer at the output of a SimPowerSys block causes the?MATLAB? environment to crash
Description: Linearizing a Simulink? model with?the following two conditions causes the MATLAB? environment to crash:     The model includes a subsystem containing at least one SimPowerSystems block.     The output of a SimPowerSystems block is marked as a linearization point and has a signal viewer connected.
Workaround: Remove the signal viewer and run linearization again.

Summary: Target Preferences block cannot be opened in model
Description: While you work with a model you created for a TI C6000 processor in R2007a or an earlier release, you get an error when you double-click on the Target Preferences block to open the block dialog box: Error evaluating 'OpenFcn' callback of Target Preferences block (mask) ....Reference to non-existent field 'peripherals'.  The missing field prevents the block dialog box from opening and you cannot change the block parameters. ?
Workaround: To correct this problem, replace the file TgtPref.m in your MATLAB installation with the file TgtPref.m attached to this report.To update the file in your MATLAB installation, follow these steps:Close all open instances of MATLAB.Find the file TgtPref.m in the following directory: <matlab_root>\toolbox\shared\idelinks\pjtgenerator\tgtprefwhere matlab_root is the root directory for your MATLAB software.Back up your existing version of the file TgtPref.m by making a copy in another location or by changing the name of the file.Download the TgtPref.m file attached to this report.Replace the original TgtPref.m file with the downloaded file.?

Summary: ForegroundColor property ignored on pushbutton and togglebutton uicontrol objects on Microsoft? Windows? XP platforms
Description: When using the Windows XP theme for the Control Panel Display Properties, the ForegroundColor  property of pushbutton  and togglebutton  uicontrol  objects is ignored. For example uicontrol('style','pushbutton','string','test','ForegroundColor','r') should produce a button with a red label, but produces a black label instead.
Workaround: There are two workarounds.The first option is to install the following patch:Save the attached file into matlabroot\java.Start MATLAB and in the Command Window, type cd (fullfile(matlabroot, 'java')); unzip attachment_404151_20080215.zip; unzip patch_404151.jar.Restart MATLAB. You should now be able to set foreground colors properly on uicontrols.The second option is to use the Windows Classic theme. To do so:From the Windows? desktop, select Start > Settings > Control Panel > Display.Select the Appearance tab.Under Windows and buttons, select Windows Classic style. Click Apply.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Models that have xPC (Scope) blocks set to type target do not work with Simulink external mode if target PC is booted with target scope disabled
Description: An xPC Target model cannot work in Simulink external mode if the following conditions exist: You boot the target PC with the target scope disabled. You have a model that contains Scopes set to type target.
Workaround: As a workaround, perform one of the following:Enable target scopes, recreate the target boot disk or kernel, and reboot the target PCRemove the Scope block set to type target from the model

Summary: Links to MATLAB Central from Web deployment documentation for java_web_vararg_demo and java_web_figures_demo are not correct
Description: References to search MATLAB Central for "java_web_vararg_demo" and "java_web_figures_demo"?from the MATLAB Builder for Java documentation are inaccurate. When this is attempted, no search results are found.

Summary: Non-virtual buses passing through latched function-call subsystem inputs may produce incorrect results in both simulation and generated code
Description: Function-call subsystems in Simulink models may generate incorrect answers or produce incorrect code if both the following conditions are met: One of the inport blocks in the function call subsystem has 'Latch input by copying inside signal'?set to ON. This inport is connected to a non-virtual bus signal (a bus signal that emanates? from the root inport of a model block, or from a Bus Creator in which the?'Output as nonvirtual bus' option is ON).  Please download the attached model (and companion MATLAB-file) to see a reproduction case for?this bug. Load the model (which should also?run the MATLAB-file as part of the PreLoadFcn)?and simulate it. The results?displayed on?the two scopes should be identical, but are clearly different.
Workaround: Turning off?'Latch input by?copying inside signal'?should eliminate?this issue. You can obtain functionality that is equivalent to the latch by manually inserting a Signal Conversion block after the corresponding Inport block inside the subsystem. The Signal Conversion block should be set to the 'Nonvirtual bus' option. Alternatively, you may convert the non-virtual bus to a virtual bus before feeding it to the function-call subsystem by using the Signal Conversion block and choosing the 'Virtual bus' option.

Summary: Setting the AssignedSignals parameter for a Bus Assignment block can fail in some cases
Description: The command:???set_param(gcb, 'AssignedSignals', 'input1') where gcb  points to a Bus Assignment block, can error out if the following conditions are satisfied:    The bus signal entering the Bus Assignment block needs to be defined by a bus object.    (For example, the bus signal originates from a root Inport block.)     The needed bus object is not present in the base workspace.  The error message will be similar to the following:?????? Error using ==> set_param???--> Simulink.DataType object 'BusObject' is not in scope from 'model/In1'.
Workaround: Create a Bus Object with the correct name in the base workspace before executing set_param.

Summary: Example code from COM documentation does not work in MATLAB v7.1 (R14FCS?)? through? v7.5 (R2007b)
Description: The following example in the topic Saving COM Objects Created with actxserver ?does not work in MATLAB versions 7.1 (R14FCS) through 7.5 (R2007b). e = actxserver ('Excel.Application');addproperty(e, 'NewProperty');set(e, 'NewProperty', 500);get(e, 'NewProperty')ans =?? 500save('excelserver.mat')clearget(e, 'NewProperty')??? Undefined function or variable 'e'.load('excelserver.mat')get(e, 'NewProperty')ans =?? 500

Summary: hdrread incorrectly scales high dynamic range data in .hdr files by 256
Description: Radiance .hdr  files contain high dynamic range image data. The hdrread  function imports images from these files into floating-point arrays, but the values are incorrectly scaled. Image data read by hdrread  is too large by a factor of 256.
Workaround: The high dynamic range data has the correct dynamic range.  The values are simply scaled incorrectly.To obtain the correct HDR values, divide the output of hdrread by 256:X = hdrread('office.hdr') ./ 256;

Summary: Disabling library links containing Stateflow blocks when simulating a model will cause a segmentation violation to occur
Description: Disabling library links containing Stateflow blocks when simulating a model will cause a segmentation violation to occur

Summary: Converting a subsystem to a Model block may fail with an incorrect error message
Description: Converting a subsystem to a referenced model using either the subsystem context menu item Convert to Model Block  or the command line API Simulink.SubSystem.convertToModelBlock  may fail and post an incorrect error message if the model has the following settings: SolverType  is set to Fixed-step  Periodic sample time constraint  is set to Ensure sample time independent   The incorrect error message states: Parameter 'SolverMode' is ignored when 'SampleTimeConstraint' is 'Ensure sample time independent'.
Workaround: For R2007a:Back up the following files in your MATLAB installation:MATLABROOT\toolbox\simulink\simulink\private\slss2mdl_util.pMATLABROOT\toolbox\simulink\simulink\@Simulink\@SubSystem\convertToModelReference.mDownload the attached files and copy them into the locations from the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Issue the following command at the MATLAB command prompt:rehash toolboxcacheFor Other Releases:Set Configuration Parameters > Solver > Periodic sample time constraint to Unconstrained.Modify the newly created model if necessary.

Summary: Java Components may not dispose successfully when used in a Swing application
Description: The call to dispose()  on the Builder for Java generated class never returns if you create the component from the Swing event dispatch thread and close it via setDefaultCloseOperation(DISPOSE_ON_CLOSE) . However, if you dispose via the event dispatch thread (through a button click, or through the windowClosing  event), or if you create the component from a separate thread, the dispose succeeds.
Workaround: Instantiate the component object on a thread other than the Swing event dispatch thread.

Summary: Linear algebra computations slow on Solaris machines with UltraSparc III, IIIi, and IV processors
Description: On Solaris machines, MATLAB? uses the Sun Performance Library for all its computations with vectors or matrices. There is a version of the library missing in MATLAB?. The missing library is an accelerated version for Sun UltraSparc III, IIIi, and IV processors. It runs calculations approximately twice as fast as the old library. As a result, MATLAB? is not running as fast as it should on these processors.
Workaround: This can be resolved by installing the accelerated version of Sun Performance Library" in the correct path in the MATLAB? source tree. Download and install Sun" Studio 11 from Sun Developer Network website:http://developers.sun.com/downloads/Copy the librarysunstudioroot/opt/SUNWspro/prod/lib/cpu/sparcv9+vis2/libsunperf_isa.so.6into the following location (you may have to create the directory if it does not already exist):matlabroot/sys/os/sol64/prod/lib/cpu/sparcv9+vis2/sparcv9where sunstudioroot and matlabroot are the root directories of Sun" Studio 11 and MATLAB? respectively. You can also instruct MATLAB? to use the newly installed library by setting the BLAS_VERSION environment variable prior to starting MATLAB?:setenv BLAS_VERSION sunstudioroot/opt/SUNWspro/prod/lib/v9a/libsunperf.so.6

Summary: Element name mismatch diagnostic does not report a warning or error if the mismatched bus element is a nested bus
Description: The Element name mismatch  diagnostic of the Configuration Parameters > Diagnostics > Connectivity pane can detect an inconsistency between the name of a bus signal element and the name specified by the corresponding bus object element. However, the diagnostic?fails to?report a warning or error if a name mismatch occurs and the bus signal element is a nested bus.

Summary: Error mentioning "singularity / Inf / NaN in the solution" when running model in Accelerator mode
Description: An error might occur when running a model in Accelerator mode. The error mentions "singularity / Inf / NaN in the solution".

Summary: Models with NaNs in transition labelPosition property cannot be loaded after resaving in Stateflow 7.0 (R2007b)
Description: In rare cases, you cannot load Stateflow models after saving them in R2007b if the models contain transitions that were saved with NaN s in the labelPosition  property. If you encounter this bug, please email The MathWorks at support@mathworks.com. To proactively detect models that contain this corruption, follow these steps: Download the the attached file look_for_label_position_nans.m   to a writable directory on your machine. Start MATLAB and add this directory to your MATLAB path. In MATLAB, navigate to the directory containing your Simulink model files and run the following function: >> look_for_label_position_nans  The function alerts you to models that contain this corruption by displaying messages in your command window that look like this: Found corrupted labelPosition property set to NaN in xyz.mdl.Found corrupted labelPosition property set to -1.#IO in abc.mdl.
Workaround: To fix corrupt models, follow these steps:Open the model (.mdl file) as a text file in the MATLAB Editor and search for lines containing the pattern labelPosition [-1.#IO or labelPosition [NaN NaN, as in this example: labelPosition ?[-1.#IO -1.#IO 8.182 15.58]Replace the entry -1.#IO or NaN with 0.Navigate to the following directory: $matlabroot/toolbox/stateflow/stateflow/privateIn this path, $matlabroot is the MATLAB installation directory and can be located by executing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt.Rename sfinit.m to sfinit.m.old.Save the attached file (sfinit.m) to this location.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: The regular expression quantifiers {0,0} and {0} behave like *
Description: The regular expression quantifiers {0,0} and {0} mean "match at least zero and not more than zero" and "match exactly zero" respectively.? There is a bug in which these behave the same as {0,} and *, which both mean "match zero or more."
Workaround: Matching exactly zero of a subexpression in a regular expression means to ignore it.? Ignoring a subexpression is the same as commenting it out.? To workaround this bug, use the regular expression comment operator instead.Change: >> regexp('first second', 'first (do not match this part){0,0}second', 'match')ans = ??? 'first second'To:>> regexp('first second', 'first (?#do not match this part)second', 'match')ans = ??? 'first second'Note that the parenthesized group captures an empty token, whereas the comment does not:>> regexp('first second', '(first) (do not match this part){0,0}(second)', 'tokens', 'once')ans = ??? 'first'???? ''??? 'second'>> regexp('first second', '(first) (?#do not match this part)(second)', 'tokens', 'once')ans = ??? 'first'??? 'second'To work around that discrepancy, enclose the comment in another set of parentheses:>> regexp('first second', '(first) ((?#do not match this part))(second)', 'tokens', 'once')ans = ??? 'first'???? ''??? 'second'

Summary: Relational Operator block generates erroneous VHDL code if inputs have different?fixed-point data types
Description: When the two inputs of the Relational Operater have different? fixed-point data types,?incorrect VHDL code is generated and a syntax error occurs when the code is compiled. Verilog code generated for the same block is correct, however.
Workaround: Connect one of the inputs to a?Data Type Conversion block before the Relational Operator block. Configure the??Data Type Conversion block such that?both Relational Operator inputs have the same fixed point data type.

Summary: Missing turbine characteristics graphic in the power_windgen demo
Description: The demo model power_windgen.mdl  is missing a FIG-file psbwindgen_turb.fig . When you double-click the block Double-click to display Turbine characteristics , it generates an error: Error evaluating 'OpenFcn' callback of SubSystem block (mask) 'power_windgen/Subsystem'. Error using ==> open File 'psbwindgen_turb.fig' not found.

Summary: Simulink crashes when saving a model in a "private" directory
Description: If you try to save a model in the "private" sub-directory of a directory on the MATLAB path, an error occurs which may result in any or all of the following:* MATLAB terminates* A segmentation violation is reported in the MATLAB Command Window* The model cannot be closed.In all cases, the model file is saved correctly, though you must move it to a different directory before you can use it.
Workaround: Do not attempt to save Simulink models or libraries in "private" directories.? Simulink does not allow you to load MDL files in these directories, so there is no reason to save them there.

Summary: Why do I receive an error after previewing, closing, reopening and previewing again in IMAQTOOL?
Description: If I start IMAQTOOL, select a format and preview, then close and reopen IMAQTOOL and attempt to start preview again, I receive the following error: ??? Invalid handle object. Error in ==> C:\Applications\MATLAB2007b\toolbox\imaq\imaq\@iatbrowser\@PreviewPanelController\initialize.p>initialize/handleStartPrevClicked at 109 ???????? this.handleStartPrevClickedCallback(obj, event); Warning: Error occurred while evaluating listener callback.
Workaround: To work around this, do one of the following:1.? Minimize IMAQTOOL instead of closing it.2.? Call IMAQRESET before reopening IMAQTOOL.

Summary: Segmentation fault occurs in MATLAB?when using the xpctarget.xpc class getsignalid method to call?a signal with?a long?name
Description: In the following situations, a segmentation fault can occur in MATLAB when calling a signal with a long name (more than 80 characters). These situations result from calls to the xpctarget.xpc  class getsignalid method. Using the xpctarget.xpc  class getsignalid method to call a signal with a long name Selecting a signal with a long name in xPC Target Explorer Accessing a signal with a long name in Simulink external mode
Workaround: To work around this issue:Stop MATLAB.Unzip the attached file xpcapi7a.zip for Version 3.2 (R2007a) or xpcapi7b.zip for Version 3.3 (R2007b)Copy the resutling file, xpcapi.dll, to the following directories: matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\apimatlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\xpc\private where matlabroot represents your MATLAB installation root, as returned by the MATLAB command matlabroot.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Parameterized links to configurable subsystems may result in incorrect parameter values
Description: Simulink may not assign correct values to a parameter of a parameterized link to a configurable subsystem. This typically occurs with nested parameterized links to configurable systems, i.e., when a parameterized link to a configurable subsystem itself contains a parameterized link to a configurable subsystem.

Summary: xmlwrite is not writing <!DOCUMENT> element in the xml file even when a document type is specified.
Description: The output of xmlwrite  does not contain a <!DOCUMENT> element, even when you specify a document type. This example writes to file autoxml.xml : docType = com.mathworks.xml.XMLUtils.createDocumentType(... 'sect1', '-//OASIS//DTD DocBook XML V4.2//EN', ... 'd:\applications\matlab\r2007a\sys\namespace\docbook\v4\dtd\docbookx.dtd'); domNode = com.mathworks.xml.XMLUtils.createDocument('','sect1',docType); domNode.getDocumentElement.setAttribute('lang','en'); domNode.getDocumentElement.appendChild(... domNode.createProcessingInstruction('dbhtml','filename="test.html"')); xmlwrite('autoxml.xml',domNode); The output of xmlwrite  does not contain a <!DOCUMENT> element: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?> <sect1 lang="en"><?dbhtml filename="test.html"?></sect1> ?
Workaround: None known.

Summary: Embedded MATLAB Functions longer than 128 KB do not load correctly.
Description: If your Embedded MATLAB Function block in Simulink or Embedded MATLAB function in Stateflow is longer than 128 KB, the model file will not load correctly. The script is correctly saved in the model file. You can recover the script by opening the model file in a text editor.
Workaround: Use MATLAB-files on the path in R2007b to keep your Embedded MATLAB function in a separate MATLAB-file.

Summary: MATLAB shuts down when a model containing a Schedule Timeout block is simulated with certain event logging options turned on
Description: Simulating a model that contains a Schedule Timeout block with any of the following event logging options turned on will cause MATLAB to shut down, resulting in loss of data. Display events in event calendar Log events when executed Log events when scheduled

Summary: Customer Storage Class Designer: On non-English machines, Struct name for Flat Structure custom storage class does not change
Description: This bug occurs only   on computers that are configured to use a language other than English. On any such computer, if the Type  of a custom storage class is set to Flat Structure  in the Custom Storage Class Designer, you cannot change the value of Struct name  on the Structure Attributes  tab. If you try to select a different value using the Struct name  pulldown, Struct name  retains its existing value. No warning or error occurs.
Workaround: Use the following workaround to enable the value selected from the Struct name pulldown to be changed on a non-English machine:Stop MATLAB.In the source directory: ?????matlabroot/toolbox/simulink/simulink/@Simulink/@CSCTypeAttributes_FlatStructure/Rename the file?instanceComboFcn.p to instanceComboFcn.oldIn the source directory: ?????matlabroot/toolbox/simulink/simulink/@Simulink/@CSCDefn/Rename the file?getCSCPropDetails.p to getCSCPropDetails.oldOpen the attached .zip file.Copy or extract instanceComboFcn.p to:  ?????matlabroot/toolbox/simulink/simulink/@Simulink/@CSCTypeAttributes_FlatStructure/Copy or extract getCSCPropDetails.p to:  ?????matlabroot/toolbox/simulink/simulink/@Simulink/@CSCDefn/Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Sum block might generate a data type propagation error when its input signals specify data type aliases
Description: A Sum block might generate erroneous data type propagation errors under all of the following conditions: The Sum block's Output data type  parameter specifies Inherit: Inherit via internal rule . The Sum block's input signals use Simulink.AliasType  objects to specify data type aliases. For example, suppose that the data type of an input signal is set to myfloat , which is a Simulink.AliasType  object whose BaseType  property specifies single .  In this case, the Sum block might fail to recognize the equivalence between the Simulink.AliasType  object (e.g., myfloat ) and the base data type (e.g., single ) that the block chooses to output. Consequently, Simulink displays a data type propagation error.
Workaround: You can work around this issue using either of the following methods:Specify the Sum block's Output data type parameter as something other than Inherit: Inherit via internal rule. For example, specify the parameter as Inherit: Same as first input; or you can explicitly specify it as the name of your data type alias, e.g., myfloat.Connect a Signal Specification block to the Sum block's outport. Specify the Signal Specification block's Data type parameter as the name of your data type alias, e.g., myfloat.

Summary: db2pow and pow2db described in the documentation but not shipped
Description: Signal Processing Toolbox documentation describes the db2pow  and pow2db  functions, but the code for these functions was not shipped.
Workaround: To use these functions, copy the two Attachments files into the directory:matlab/toolbox/signal/signalEnter>>rehash toolboxcacheat the Matlab command prompt to refresh the toolbox cache.

Summary: In Embedded MATLAB, logical fields of buses and their corresponding structures generate uncompilable code.
Description: Passing Simulink buses with logical fields as outputs from an Embedded MATLAB Function block, Truth Table block, or Embedded MATLAB function inside a Stateflow chart may lead to the generation of uncompilable code under the following circumstances: The relevant block or Stateflow chart has a bus output B  with logical fields The first assignment to B  is not  a direct assignment from an existing input bus signal A  (not  B=A ).  When this bug occurs, the C compiler produces an error message about multiple definitions of the same structure type.

Summary: tf function errors when input filter has parallel structure with a filter stage equal to zero
Description: The tf  function errors if the input filter contains two or more filter stages in parallel and one of those filter stages is equal to zero.

Summary: Linux platforms: using the Help browser sometimes results in segmentation violation errors
Description: On Linux platforms, using the Help browser sometimes results in segmentation violation errors that appear in the Command Window, and include stack trace information similar to the following: Stack Trace: [0] libzip.so:ZIP_Read~(0x08a87ae0, 0, 245, 0) + 35 bytes After the problem occurs, MATLAB might not be stable, so exit and start MATLAB again.

Summary: fread and fwrite functions do not recognize the formats: 'unsigned char', 'signed char', 'unsigned short', 'unsigned int', and 'unsigned long'.
Description: fread  and fwrite  functions do not recognize the formats: 'unsigned char', 'signed char', 'unsigned short', 'unsigned int',  and 'unsigned long'.  An error is returned: ??? Error using ==> fwriteInvalid precision.
Workaround: Use the 'uchar' or other equivalent formats.

Summary: The MODEL_acc_data.c file produced for an accelerated simulation may fail to compile, resulting in a failure to simulate
Description: The MODEL_acc_data.c file produced for an accelerated simulation may fail to compile, resulting in a failure to simulate.? The error will refer to an invalid number of initializers; for example it may look similar to the following:MODEL_acc_data.c(975) : error C2078: too many initializers?U:\ARCHIVE\R2007B\PERFECT\MATLAB\BIN\MEX.PL: Error: Compile of MODEL_acc_data.c' failed.

Summary: SimEvents blocks fail to recognize [1x1] attribute values as scalar
Description: If you set an attribute using a [1x1] signal and use that attribute in a block that requires an attribute to be a scalar, then you might see an erroneous message similar to the following: Error occurred in 'mymodel/Attribute Scope'. Inconsistency in attributedimensions as specified or inherited in different blocks in the model. The'mymodel/Attribute Scope' block defines attribute 'Attribute1' as a scalar. All SimEvents blocks that require attributes to be scalars exhibit this behavior.
Workaround: 1. Insert a Signal Specification block (from Simulink Signal Attributes library) in your model.2. Set the Dimensions parameter to 1 in the Signal Specification block.3. Connect the [1x1] signal to the input port of the Signal Specification block.4. Connect the output signal of the Signal Specification block to the block that sets the attribute.

Summary: For Abs block, Simulink HDL Coder supports only default (Inherit: Same as input) setting for Output data type property
Description: Simulink HDL Coder does not generate correct code for the Abs block unless the Output data type  property is set to Inherit: Same as input (the default setting).
Workaround: To work around this problem, set the Output data type property of the Abs block to Inherit: Same as input.?Connect the?output of the Abs block to a Data Type Conversion block, and convert the output signal to the desired data type.

Summary: Designing a compensator for?a model with top-level input ports?produces an error
Description: Designing a compensator in the Control and Estimation Tools Manager?for?certain models with top-level input ports may produce the error below.??This error may occur for?models with root-level input ports directly connected to the import of a subsystem.Your Simulink model could not be analyzed due to the following error:
Workaround: Download the attached file to the following directory:matlabroot\toolbox\slcontrol\slcontrol\@LinearizationObjects\@linearizationutil

Summary: xpctest fails if the xPC Target kernel uses RS-232 for communications between the host PC and target PC
Description: xpctest  fails if the xPC Target kernel uses RS-232 for communications between the host PC and target PC. This issue occurs when you download and unload your model to and from the target PC without running the model. If you download, run, then unload your model, this issue does not occur.
Workaround: As a workaround, use TCP/IP to communicate between the host PC and target PC.

Summary: TMTOOL throws a Java exception while opening a second time
Description: TMTOOL may fail to start when launched a second time.? This may occur if you close the TMTOOL after scanning for hardware.? A Java error, similar to the following is displayed and the TMTOOL window does not open correctly and you cannot close it. java.lang.reflect.InvocationTargetException?? ?at sun.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke0(Native Method)?? ?at sun.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(Unknown Source)?? ?at sun.reflect.DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(Unknown Source)?? ?at java.lang.reflect.Method.invoke(Unknown Source)?? ?at com.mathworks.jmi.AWTUtilities$Invoker$2.runWithOutput(AWTUtilities.java:135)?? ?at com.mathworks.jmi.AWTUtilities$Invoker$1.run(AWTUtilities.java:103)?? ?at java.awt.event.InvocationEvent.dispatch(Unknown Source)?? ?at java.awt.EventQueue.dispatchEvent(Unknown Source)?? ?at java.awt.EventDispatchThread.pumpOneEventForFilters(Unknown Source)?? ?at java.awt.EventDispatchThread.pumpEventsForFilter(Unknown Source)?? ?at java.awt.EventDispatchThread.pumpEventsForHierarchy(Unknown Source)?? ?at java.awt.EventDispatchThread.pumpEvents(Unknown Source)?? ?at java.awt.EventDispatchThread.pumpEvents(Unknown Source)?? ?at java.awt.EventDispatchThread.run(Unknown Source) ?
Workaround: To fix this problem, do the following:Backup your current the jar file by renaming the? instrument.jar? in the <MATLABROOT>/java/jar/toolbox/instrument.jar directory to instrument.old.? <MATLABROOT> is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned when you execute the command matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt.Download the attached file.Copy the downloaded jar file to <MATLABROOT>/java/jar/toolbox/Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Using FWRITE with data format uint32 may result in data loss
Description: When you work with instrument control interfaces or MATLAB serial objects, FWRITE will treat uint32 as int32 resulting in data loss for numbers larger than (2^31)-1 or 2147483647. s = serial('COM1');fopen(s);fwrite(s,2^32 - 1, 'uint32')>> fread(s,1,'uint32')  ans =  2.1475e+009
Workaround: ?Select an appropriate data format other than uint32.

Summary: INSTRHWINFO fails to find VISA TCP/IP instruments
Description: INSTRHWINFO fails to report available instruments when you use the instrhwinfo function with the following arguments:  instrhwinfo('visa','agilent','tcpip'); Another symptom of this issue is that when scanning for TCPIP - VXI11 instruments TMTOOL fails to find them.
Workaround: To resolve the issue rename the file <MATLABROOT>/toolbox/instrument/instrument/instrhwinfo.m to instrhwinfo.bak,?save the attached file as <MATLABROOT>/toolbox/instrument/instrument/instrhwinfo.m and then restart MATLAB.

Summary: xPC Target models that contain blocks for the Measurement Computing CIO-QUAD02, CIO-QUAD04, and PCI-QUAD04 boards might fail during initialization
Description: If the count input to the board is active when you start your model, the xPC Target CIO-QUAD02, CIO-QUAD04, and PCI-QUAD04 blocks for the corresponding Measurement Computing boards will see the changing count as a failure of the board. This issue exists for the xPC Target CIO-QUAD02, CIO-QUAD04, and PCI-QUAD04 blocks contained in the xPC Target Block Library and the Obsolete xPC Target Block Library.
Workaround: As a workaround, prevent the block input from changing until after you start the model. If this is not possible for your situation, contact MathWorks Technical Support.

Summary: Stateflow Snapshot component can not take a snapshot of a linked Stateflow Chart.
Description: When a report is generated, no snapshot is included. The Report Generator message includes the following statement: In component Stateflow Snapshot :   Error using ==> rptgen_sf.appdata_sf.getTypeInfo at 25   Object type "LinkChart" not found
Workaround: 1. Backup these filesmatlab/toolbox/stateflow/stateflow/@rptgen_sf/@csf_hier_loop/loop_setState.p matlab/toolbox/stateflow/stateflow/@rptgen_sf/@csf_obj_loop/loop_setState.p matlab/toolbox/stateflow/stateflow/@rptgen_sf/@csf_chart_loop/loop_setState.p matlab/toolbox/stateflow/stateflow/@rptgen_sf/@csf_machine_loop/loop_setState.p matlab/toolbox/stateflow/stateflow/@rptgen_sf/@csf_state_loop/loop_setState.p2. Extract toolbox.zip file to MATLAB installation directory. This patch works with releases R2006b-R207b. There is no patch available for earlier releases

Summary: Potential wrong answer when editing C-mex S-function in between Accelerator mode simulation runs
Description: When using the following workflow: Run a model simulation in Accelerator or Rapid Accelerator mode. Edit the C file for a C-mex S-function with an output port that has a constant sample time; change the hard-coded value of the constant output. Re-mex the S-function. Re-run the model simulation in Accelerator or Rapid Accelerator mode.  The Accelerator and Rapid Accelerator will fail to re-generate code, and potentially give incorrect simulation results.
Workaround: When changing a C-mex S-function in this way, force the Accelerator or Rapid Accelerator to re-generate code by making an additional change to the model that triggers a re-build. For example, add a block to the model.

Summary: Error occurs when relaunching Model Advisor
Description: The Simulink software produces an error under the following conditions: Launch Model Advisor for your model. Close the Model Advisor window without closing the model. Launch Model Advisor for the model or a subsystem within the model.  The following is an example of the error message: No public field text exists for class handle.
Workaround: You can resolve this issue in two ways:Option 1: Close and reopen the model between Model Advisor sessions.Option 2: Use the following instructions to relpace the matlabroot\toolbox\simulink\simulink\@Simulink\@ModelAdvisor\displayExplorer.p file.Determine your MATLAB root directory by entering the matlabroot command at the MATLAB command prompt.Quit MATLAB.Rename matlabroot\toolbox\simulink\simulink\@Simulink\@ModelAdvisor\displayExplorer.p to matlabroot\toolbox\simulink\simulink\@Simulink\@ModelAdvisor\displayExplorer.p.old.Download the attached file and place it in the directory matlabroot\toolbox\simulink\simulink\@Simulink\@ModelAdvisor.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Log-likelihood for inverse Gaussian distribution in dfittool missing constant term
Description: The log-likelihood for the inverse Gaussian distribution calculated in the Distribution Fitting Tool, dfittool , differs from the log of the probability density function (log PDF) by an additive constant that does not depend on the parameters of the fitted distribution.? While the missing constant makes no difference for the purposes of fitting the distribution or performing likelihood ratio tests, it does affect the use of model comparison measures such as AIC and BIC.
Workaround: To compute the log-likelihood, including the additive constant, for an inverse Gaussian fit made with the Distribution Fitting Tool, either1) Evaluate the inverse Gaussian probability density function using the pdf function, and take its log, or2) Subtract n*.5*log(2*pi) from the log-likelihood displayed in dfittool, where n is the number of observations in your data, or3) Modify line 267 (in the current version, Revision 1.1.4.16.2.1) in the local function invg_nloglf of the MATLAB file MATLABROOT/toolbox/stats/private/addinvg.m to read L = .5.*log(lambda./(2*pi)) - 1.5.*log(x) - lambda.*(x./mu-1).^2 ./ (2.*x);

Summary: Help for a subfunction does not work if its function definition line contains empty parentheses.
Description: MATLAB-functions that have no input arguments may be specified using either no parentheses or empty parenthesis. If you use empty parentheses to specify a subfunction that has no input arguments, MATLAB is not able to locate help text for the subfunction.For example, for the following file, MATLAB does not return help on subfunction someNumber : function x = myfun(y)  % Add some number to y.  x = y + someNumber;  function x = someNumber()  % Generate a random number.  x = rand;>>help myfun>someNumber  myfun>someNumber not found. However, if you remove the empty parentheses from the subfunction definition line, help does work: function x = myfun(y)  % Add some number to y.  x = y + someNumber;  function x = someNumber % Parentheses removed.  % Generate a random number.  x = rand;>>help myfun>someNumber  Generate a random number.
Workaround: Remove the empty parentheses from the subfunction definition.

Summary: LIBSTRUCT may generate incorrect structure layout for sized arrays with?packing greater than 1
Description: For the following structure on Windows: struct c_struct { ???? unsigned long p1; ???? unsigned long p2[2]; ???? unsigned long p3; };? With a packing of 8 (the default),? the structure size is 24 instead of 16 and member access is incorrect. st=libstruct('c_struct');st.structsizeans =? 24 ?
Workaround: Add? #pragma pack(1)?to the included?header file.?????

Summary: Comma-separated list assignment gives wrong answer if too many values assigned
Description: Comma-separated list assignment from a right-hand side of the form variable(indices).field to a left-hand side with fewer list elements than the right-hand side assigns the wrong values to the left-hand side variables.For example, the statement S(1:3).a below generates three values, but only two of these are assigned: S(1).a = 1; S(2).a = 2; S(3).a = 3;  [x, y] = S(1:3).a The expected behavior is for MATLAB to assign the first return value (S(1).a) to x, the second (S(2).a) to y, and to ignore the third (S(3).a). Instead, the results are incorrect: x = 2 and y = 3.
Workaround: In cases where the right-hand side comma-separated list might contain more elements that the left-hand side, avoid the syntax var(indices).field, by breaking the indexing into a separate operation. For example,S1 = S(1:3);[x, y] = S1.a x = 1 y = 2

Summary: xPC Target might not recognize the Condor ARINC 520 board
Description: The xPC Target initialization code for Condor ARINC 520 might fail to find the board. The boards that xPC Target finds will still work correctly.
Workaround: For a workaround, if you have xPC Target Version 3.2 or 3.3 (R2007a or R2007b respectively), contact MathWorks Technical Support.

Summary: DEPLOYTOOL fails to compile when a subset of toolboxes is selected in the "Toolboxes on Path" settings.
Description: When only a subset of toolboxes are selected in the "Toolboxes on Path" settings in DEPLOYTOOL, it fails to compile the project.
Workaround: Select all toolboxes in the "Toolboxes on Path" setting and then compile the project.

Summary: Javadoc is unavailable for searching from mathworks.com
Description: Javadoc will not?be searchable from mathworks.com for  R2007B.
Workaround: The R2007b Javadoc is available from the following sources:1) From the product's Help Navigator. Click Help > Product Help from MATLAB.2) In mcr_root/help/toolbox/javabuilder/MWArrayAPI/ installed?on all platforms.3) As an attachment to the EBR associated with this Geck. To dowload this attachment, perform these steps:a) Save the attachment mwarrayapi.zip to a location on your local computer.b) Open the .zip file.c) From WinZip, select Actions > Select All.d) Select Actions > Extract.e) Choose a directory where you want the contents?extracted from the .zip,?and click Extract.f) ?From the directory where you extracted the contents in step (e), open index.html.

Summary: In Embedded MATLAB, using small negative double values as conditions for if and while statements produces incorrect results during simulation.
Description: In Embedded MATLAB, if  statements and while  loops that use negative double values between -1 and 0 as the condition produce incorrect results during simulation. For example, in the following code, the if  branch never executes: u = -0.06;if u? x = x - 1;else? x = x + 1; % The else branch executes.end
Workaround: Use logical expressions as conditions for if statements and while loops, as in the following example:u = -0.06;if u ~= 0? x = x - 1;else? x = x + 1;end?

Summary: When MATLAB installation directory contains spaces and MEX compiler is Borland, Stateflow generates build errors
Description: When you install MATLAB in a directory that contains spaces ˇŞ such as C:\Program Files\R2007b  ˇŞ and you select Borland C++ Compiler (free command line tools) 5.5  as the MEX compiler, Stateflow generates build errors. The generated makefiles for the Borland compiler cannot handle space characters.
Workaround: Reinstall MATLAB in a directory without spaces or download the attached file code_borland_make_file.m to the directory <matlabroot>/toolbox/stateflow/coder/private.

Summary: Sporadic crash when using Simulink menus
Description: Using the Simulink menus (in particular, the context menu) can crash MATLAB.? To tell if a MATLAB crash is caused by this problem, examine the the stack trace resulting from the crash. It should contain the following line:Stack Trace: ?? [0] libmwsimulink.dll:bool __cdecl get_selected(struct MWObject_tag * *)
Workaround: Download the attached MATLAB file and copy it into matlabroot/toolbox/shared/dastudio, replacing the version already there.

Summary: Closing the library browser w/o closing the block dialogs opened through it, will cause MATLAB to crash
Description: 1) Open the library browser2) Double click on some of the block(s) to open the block dialog3) Close the library browserMATLAB will crash
Workaround: The workaround is to close all the block dialogs opened FROM the library browser before closing the library browser itself.

Summary: Why does HWTREE return invalid probability values when I specify time steps of different sizes in the Financial Derivatives Toolbox 5.1?
Description: HWTREE returns invalid probability values when specifying time steps of different sizes in the Financial Derivatives Toolbox.

Summary: License Manager version number incorrect in product list displayed by R2007b MathWorks Installer for Windows platforms.
Description: When you run the R2007b MathWorks Installer on Windows systems, the Installer displays the License Manager as version 10.8, but the correct version is 11.4. Note: The R2007b Installer actually installs the correct version of the FLEXNET license manager (11.4); it's just the display that's incorrect. ?

Summary: Using textscan on a file opened with only 'write' permission fails to throw the appropriate error.
Description: Trying to use textscan  on a file opened with 'w' permission does not result in an eventual? 'Out of memory' error.
Workaround: Do not use textscan on a file opened for writing.

Summary: Setting c2812 PWM outputs unconditionally blocks 8 pins of the I/O port.
Description: The code generated by the c28x PWM block unconditionally reserves 8 pins of the I/O port, regardless of how many pins the PWM module uses.Module A blocks pins GPIOA0 to GPIOA7 for use by peripherals.Module B blocks pins GPIOB0 to GPIOB7 for use by peripherals.The PWM block may overwrite settings of GPIO blocks that subsequently try to access GPIOA0 to GPIOA7 or GPIOB0 to GPIOB7.
Workaround: The following patch only applies to MATLAB versions R14sp3, R2006a, R2006b, R2007a, and R2007b.Please avoid installing this patch to other MATLAB versions as doing so may damage the MATLAB software.This patch modifies the code generated by the c28x PWM block so that it reserves the PWM pairs selected on the block, and leaves the remaining pins available for other purposes, such as GPIO.To verify that this patch applies to the MATLAB version:    Start MATLAB.    Type ver at the MATLAB prompt.    Compare the MATLAB version with the versions listed above.    If the patch applies to the MATLAB version, install the patch.To install the patch:    Download the attachments, patch_installer.m?and patch_399668.zip. (If needed, righ-click the link for patch_installer.m and select Save As.)    Save the attachments to Current Directory of the MATLAB software, or modify the Current Directory so it points to the location of the attachments.    Enter the following command at the MATLAB prompt:patch_installer('patch_399668.zip', 'install')If you install the patch to the wrong version of the MATLAB software, the following command uninstalls the patch and restores the previous MATLAB software:patch_installer('patch_399668.zip', 'uninstall')

Summary: Function adapt returns network outputs with incorrect values.
Description: ADAPT.M returns network outputs with incorrect values. The outputs have not had the network outputs processing functions applied to them.
Workaround: The workaround is to call PROCESSOUTPUTS after each call to ADAPT:[net,a] = adapt(net,p,t);a = processoutputs(net,a);

Summary: Coding error in polybool.m wastes allocation time and memory
Description: By mistake, in the main polybool  M-function the 'ishole'  field for each element of structure arrays p1  and p2  is initialized to a square logical matrix instead of a logical scalar. The initialization takes extra time and wastes memory. However, unless the memory loss is severe enough to cause additional problems, these fields are later overwritten with the correct scalar values and the final answer returned by polybool is unaffected.
Workaround: To reduce the memory used by polybool, replace the polybool.m function file in your installation with the version in the attachment, as follows:    Exit MATLAB.    Make a backup copy of the following file.    MATLABROOT\matlab\toolbox\map\map\polybool.m    MATLABROOT represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function.    For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension: polybool.m.old        Copy the version of polybool.m from the attachment into the directory specified in the previous step.    Restart MATLAB.    Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Memory Copy block output port dimension is wrong when Copy from option is either Specified source code symbol or Specified address
Description: If you configure the Memory Copy block with the following settings: Copy from --Specified address  or Specified source code symbol  Data length --Any positive integer N Copy to --Output port ,  the output port length of the block is incorrectly set to 1, rather than the correct Data length  N. The code generated from the Memory Copy block correctly copies data of length N. The code generated from downstream blocks, however, assumes the data being passed is of data length=1 because downstream blocks inherit the data length from the Memory Copy block output port length. Therefore, when you generate code from the model, there is an inconsistency when data passes from the Memory Copy block to downstream blocks. This inconsistency causes data to be written beyond the memory boundaries of downstream blocks.
Workaround: To fix this problem, download and unzip the attached file. This contains the following patch files:patch_installer.mpatch_399514.zipFollow these steps to update your MATLAB installation with the patch.Extract the files, patch_installer.m and patch_399514.zip, to your MATLAB installation directory.Start MATLAB.In MATLAB, change your working directory to the installation directory.To install the patch, enter the following command at the MATLAB prompt:patch_installer('patch_399514.zip','install')This command installs the patch and updates your installation.5. Close MATLAB and restart.

Summary: Signal Conversion block might generate a data type propagation error when its output signal specifies a data type alias
Description: A Signal Conversion block might generate erroneous data type propagation errors under all of the following conditions: The output signal from the Signal Conversion block uses a Simulink.AliasType  object to specify a data type alias, via either a Signal Specification block or a Simulink.Signal  object. For example, the data type of the output signal is set to myfloat , which is a Simulink.AliasType  object whose BaseType  property specifies single . The input signal to the Signal Conversion block comes from a subsystem, and that signal specifies the same data type as the BaseType  property of the Simulink.AliasType  object that the block's output signal uses.  In this case, the Signal Conversion block might fail to recognize the equivalence between the Simulink.AliasType  object (e.g., myfloat ) and the data type (e.g., single ) of the signal entering the block. Consequently, Simulink displays a data type propagation error.
