Workaround: You can work around this issue by inserting a Data Type Conversion block before the Signal Conversion block. Specify the Data Type Conversion block's Output data type parameter as the name of your data type alias, e.g., myfloat.

Summary: The xPC Target COM API ScopeGetType method returns an empty string when querying scopes of type file
Description: The ScopeGetType  method works as expected for a scope of type host or target; for scopes of type file, this method returns an empty string.
Workaround: To work around this issue:    Exit any application or project that references xpcapiCOM.dll.    Quit MATLAB.    In a DOS command shell, cd to the directory    matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\api        In a DOS command shell, run the following:    regsvr32 /u xpcapicom.dll    A dialog box is displayed indicating that the DLL was unregistered.    In a DOS command shell, rename the original xpcapiCOM.dll with the command:    ren xpcapiCOM.dll xpcapiCOM.dll.old    Leave the DOS command shell open.    Unzip the attached zip file, xpcapiCOM.dll, to the directory from step 3.    In a DOS command shell, register the new xpcapiCOM.dll with the command:    regsvr32 xpcapicom.dll

Summary: MATLAB can crash or produce a segmentation violation when loading MAT-files containing objects that do not support serialization and issue errors when an attempt is made to load an object.
Description: Due to a bug in MATLAB, the presence of certain kinds of objects in MAT-files cause the load command to crash or issue a segmentation violation.
Workaround: You might be able to load some variables from the MAT-file by passing the names of specific variables to the load command.

Summary: External mode on Windows platforms:?Target?time on model status bar does not update?after 'ExtModeAutoUpdateStatusClock' unexpectedly disabled
Description: For models running in external mode on Windows platforms, the target time on the model window status bar is updated continuously whenever the external mode parameter ExtModeAutoUpdateStatusClock  is set to 'on' . However, this parameter may become disabled during a model save, even when the user does not specifically disable it, and in subsequent uses of the model?on Windows platforms the target time will fail to update. The parameter?can be?disabled during a model save if     The model is saved on a Linux platform     The model is saved on a Windows platform when the model window status bar is disabled
Workaround: To work around this bug:    Make sure that the model window status bar is enabled. If necessary, enable the status bar using the MATLAB command set_param(bd, 'StatusBar', 'on').    When the status bar is enabled, you can reenable target time updating using the MATLAB command set_param(bd, 'ExtModeAutoUpdateStatusClock', 'on').

Summary: External mode simulation: scope may fail to display output waveform if scope window is not open during model execution
Description: Given a model that displays output waveforms on a scope and is configured for external mode simulation, if the scope window is not open when you start model execution, the output waveform may fail to appear in the scope.
Workaround: To work around this bug, check that scope windows are open before initiating an external mode model simulation.

Summary: Selecting properties to show from the Model Explorer's Customize Contents pane can cause a SegV
Description: Selecting properties to show from the Model Explorer's Customize Contents  pane can cause a SegV if the Model Explorer is set to not show properties.
Workaround: When choosing desired properties to show in the Model Explorer, make sure that the View->Show Properties menu item is checked.

Summary: A model that contains Physical Network blocks whose names differ only in case results in a? 'Property name already in use' error.
Description: When you try to run a Simscape model that contains blocks whose names differ only in case,?it issues the following?error message:  Property name already in use
Workaround: Download the attached file, then copy it into the following directory, overwriting the existing file:$MATLABROOT/toolbox/physmod/network_engine/ne_sli/private/where $MATLABROOT is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by enteringmatlabrootin the MATLAB Command Window.

Summary: Warnings or errors returned when using BIOINFORMATICS Toolbox function JCAMPREAD with JCAMP-DX Version 6.0 files
Description: Warnings similiar to the?following?one are returned by JCAMPREAD when attempting to read?JCAMP-DX Version 6.0 files:Warning: Some expected header information is missing from the file: Expected ##ORIGIN, found ##NUM DIM.> In jcampread at 92Warning: Version 6.000000 is not supported. The function may not behave as expected.> In jcampread at 120Sometimes errors similar to the following one are also returned:??? Error using ==> jcampread>ntupleRead at 485NTUPLE Table format (F2++(Y..Y)) is unrecognized. Error in ==> jcampread at 196??????? dataBlocks = ntupleRead(fid);
Workaround: Download the attached? file.Quit MATLABRename the file $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfo\jcampread.m to $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfo\jcampread.m.old.Where $MATLAB is the directory where MATLAB is installed on your machine.Copy the downloaded file to the directory $MATLAB$\toolbox\bioinfo\bioinfoRestart MATLABRun the commandrehash toolboxcacheat the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: The Model Explorer may hang momentarily when navigating to a workspace node with sorting enabled
Description: If list sorting is enabled and you select a workspace node with more than 500 objects, the Model Explorer may hang for about 30 seconds.
Workaround: Remove the dastudio.prf file from prefdir before starting MATLAB.

Summary: DELAYSS command fails for systems with unequal numbers of inputs and outputs
Description: The DELAYSS command results in an error for a system when the number of inputs is not equal to the number of outputs. For example, the following code results in an error: td = 0.5; %delay %% 1st order system with delayed input: 2 inputs, 1 output a21 = -10; b21 = [10 1]; c21 = 1; d21 = [0 0]; delay21 = struct('delay',td, 'a',0*a21, 'b',b21, 'c',0*c21, 'd',0*d21); sys21 = delayss(a21, 0*b21, c21, d21, delay21);

Summary: Bus Selector block might not display incoming signal elements when connected to a Merge block
Description: If a Merge block is between a Bus Selector block and its signal source, and you double click the Bus Selector block to display its parameters, the left pane of the Function Block Parameters?dialog box that appears might not display the correct signal names.??Merge blocks?that you specify with initial output values?are not affected by this bug.? That is, if your model requires the Merge block parameter, Initial?output , be set to a non-empty value, this bug does not occur.
Workaround: To work around this bug do one of the following:Perform an Update Diagram (Ctrl-D)?to?load the bus information and then?check whether ?the?signal names for the Bus Selector block appear correctly in the Function Block Parameters?dialog box .Complete the following procedure:Double click the Merge block to open its Function Block Parameters dialog box.Set Initial?output?to a ?non-empty value and click?OK. For example, change [ ] to 0.Double click ?the Bus Selector block.?Verify that the signal names that appear in the left pane of the dialog box are correct.Reset the Merge block's ?Initial?output?parameter to [ ] and click OK.This procedure loads bus information into the Bus Selector block. The information is persistent for the current?model editing session.?

Summary: HDL code generated for?Upsample block with Sample offset? > 0, and running at highest rate in multirate model, has incorrect latency/delay
Description: HDL code generated for a Signal Processing Blockset ?Upsample block will have an incorrect latency/ delay if both  the following conditions are met: (1) the?Sample output  parameter of the block is set to a value greater than 0; (2) the block is running at the highest rate in a multirate model.
Workaround: To work around this problem, set the Sample output parameter of the Upsample block to 0 and insert an Integer Delay with the required offset??after the Upsample block.

Description: Using imfinfo  on a corrupted JPEG file with a header but no image data causes a MATLAB segmentation fault.

Summary: Bus Selector block: An error can occur on double clicking the Bus Selector block
Description: Double-clicking the Bus Selector block to display its Block Parameters dialog box can cause an error when both of the following are true: The block is connected directly to an input port. The input port's bus object specifies an element with fixed point data type.  The error may look like this: Error occured when trying to create dialogSpecified data type '.....' is not a valid identifier

Summary: Undo/redo behavior for graphical functions can result in data loss
Description: Create a new graphical function Edit the label to say "y = foo(u1,u2)" Select the state and press <C-x> (cut command) Click somewhere in the chart and press <C-v> (paste). You will notice a new state with the label "y = foo(u1,u2)" Press <C-z> (undo). Notice that there are no states visible in the chart. Press <C-y> (redo). Now notice that the state label says "foo" rather than "y = foo(u1,u2)". However, the Model Explorer shows the data "y", "u1" and "u2". At this point, if you click inside the label and click outside, the data are also deleted.  In other words, although the sequence of operations <C-z><C-y> (undo followed by redo) should have no effect, they can result in state local data disappearing.

Summary: Deleting base workspace objects? while a model is loading may crash the Model Explorer
Description: A model whose OpenFcn clears workspace variables can crash the Model Explorer if its Base Workspace node is selected.
Workaround: Do not select nodes under the Base Workspace node of the Model Explorer when loading a model that clears the workspace.

Summary: GUIDE Run button becomes disabled after running and debugging a GUI
Description: When building a GUI in GUIDE, you can run and debug your GUI by setting breakpoints in the GUI MATLAB file and starting the GUI from within GUIDE by pressing the Run  button on the GUIDE toolbar. If your GUI code then exits abnormally, the Run  button can become permanently disabled. This can happen, for example, if you stop running the GUI MATLAB file and exit the GUI after hitting a breakpoint.
Workaround: To debug your GUIDE GUI, run your GUI from the MATLAB Editor after your GUI M-file is loaded in the editor, or execute it from the MATLAB Command Window by typing the name of the GUI MATLAB file. If the GUIDE Run button becomes disabled, you can exit GUIDE and restart it to make the button available again.

Summary: Why does the selection in the manual user configuration combobox sometimes change in the Distributed Testing pane?
Description: When I am adding? file or path dependencies in the Distributed Testing pane or the SystemTest desktop gains focus, I noticed the selection in the manual user configuration combobox sometimes changes. This causes SystemTest to run the test with the wrong user configuration.This is a bug in SystemTest. When adding file or path dependencies in the Distribtued Testing pane or the SystemTest desktop gains focus, the selection in the manual user configuration always changes to the first item in the list of user configurations.
Workaround: As a workaround, set the desired user configuration as a default user configuration in?MATLAB, and then select the default option in the Distributed Testing pane in SystemTest.?You can set a default user configuration in MATLAB?by selecting "Manage Configurations..." under the Distributed menu in the MATLAB desktop.

Summary: The Diamond Systems Athena D/A driver might produce bad output on D/A output channels?other than the first one
Description: If a model uses more than one output D/A channel on a Diamond Systems?Athena processor, only the output from the first D/A channel will be intact. The output from the subsequent?channels might?be corrupt.
Workaround: To work around this issue:    Quit MATLAB.    Download the attached file, daprometheus.c.    Place the new file in the directory:    matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\target\build\xpcblocks        Restart MATLAB.    Rebuild your model.

Summary: S-Function with a terminate method may fail in a model referenced in Normal mode
Description: The terminate method (mdlTerminate ) of an S-Function may work incorrectly when the block is in a model that is referenced in Normal mode. For example, the SimStruct  macro ssGetRootSS(S)  points to the top model during simulation, but points to the S-Function's SimStruct  during mdlTerminate . Some other SimStruct  macros also may not work properly during the call to mdlTerminate , because the macro may be looking at memory that has already been freed and possibly reused. Depending on the current contents of the memory, the effect of this error can range from no effect to a segv .

Summary: Linux platforms:?model build incorrectly uses gcc compiler -ansi flag when target function library is?C99 (ISO)?or GNU99 (GNU)
Description: On Linux platforms, the Real-Time Workshop build process uses the gcc compiler -ansi ?flag to compile the generated code even when the target function library (TFL) is set to C99 (ISO)  or GNU99 (GNU)  on the Interface  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box. If the model contains certain math function blocks, such as a Trigonometric Function block, the created executable will give wrong results. This bug does not affect other UNIX-based platforms or Windows platforms.
Workaround: To work around this bug, perform the following steps.Note: In the following instructions, matlabroot represents your MATLAB installation root, as returned by the MATLAB command matlabroot.In your MATLAB installation tree, rename the following files, for example, by adding .old to the end of each file name.matlabroot/toolbox/rtw/rtw/rtw_c.mmatlabroot/toolbox/rtw/rtw/rtw_c.pmatlabroot/toolbox/rtw/rtw/@RTW/resolveTflName.pDownload the attached files and place them in the same directory as the files you renamed.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Startup?on Windows? platforms can fail; MATLAB? splash screen might appear first
Description: The MATLAB? application sometimes fails to start on Microsoft? Windows platforms. The splash screen usually appears briefly, but then no longer displays. This problem most frequently been reported by customers with a dual-core or multi-core PC running Windows? Vista? or Windows? XP?. This problem has also been reported under one of these conditions: When?the user's java.opts  file specifies?a combined total of more than 256MB of reserved memory (usually through the?-Xmx<MaxHeapSize>  and/or -XX:MaxPermSize=<MaxPermSize>  options) When the platform is Windows? 2000 for the Czech language with certain startup options When registering or using MATLAB as a COM Automation server  When the problem occurs, a java.log.<pid>  file, where? <pid> ? is Process Id number, is created in a location typically defined by the TEMP  environment variable. Some examples of this location are: C:\TEMP  C:\Documents and Settings\username \Local Settings\Temp  C:\Users\ username \AppData\Local\Temp   To determine your system's value for TEMP , type the following at a DOS command prompt:echo %TEMP%   The java.log  reports this error:?Could not reserve?enough space for object heap .This problem occurs because under certain circumstances, MATLAB temporarily reserves too much memory address space during Sun Microsystems? JVM? software initialization and MATLAB desktop initialization. MATLAB tries to reserve the memory space to ensure a large contiguous free block for user data while leaving 256 MB available for JVM software startup.
Workaround: As a workaround, set the environment variable to MATLAB_RESERVE_LO=0 by performing these steps:In the Windows Control Panel, select System > Advanced > Environment Variables.In the User variables for your user name tab, click New.Enter the name MATLAB_RESERVE_LO and set the?value to?0.Click OK to save your changes and close the Control Panel.Log?out from your Windows operating system, then log in. MATLAB should now start correctly.The workaround effectively disables the memory reservation, which causes this problem.The pair of environment variables, MATLAB_RESERVE_LO and MATLAB_RESERVE_HI, explicitly define a memory address range that is temporarily reserved during MATLAB startup (in particular, while the JVM starts up). That can improve the size of the largest contiguous free block of memory by displacing one or more DLLs that might otherwise be loaded within the range.If these environment variables are not set, MATLAB Version 7.5 (R2007b) attempts to automatically calculate the best low and high address reserve range to ensure the largest possible contiguous free block of memory. The explicit value of MATLAB_RESERVE_LO=0, as used in the workaround, causes no reservation to be performed, which might reduce the size of the largest contiguous free block available.In versions of MATLAB prior to 7.5 (R2007b), when these environment variables were not explicitly set, the reservation range defaulted to 0x50000000 to 0x70000000.To see how memory address space is used, run feature dumpmem in the MATLAB Command Window. MATLAB lists modules and allocations occupying specific address ranges.?

Summary: Exclusive OR operator yields incorrect answers with constant inputs.
Description: Exclusive OR operations can yield incorrect answers if the code generation software can constant-fold the inputs. This problem can occur for: 1) Embedded MATLAB 'xor' function 2) Stateflow action language operator '^' when the "Enable C-bit operations" box is checked in the chart properties dialog

Summary: Why is every other frame dropped with my DCAM camera and the?Image Acquisition Toolbox software?
Description: When I use the Image Acquisition Toolbox software with a DCAM camera capable of more than 60 frames per second, it appears that every other frame is dropped. For example, the Basler A602fc is capable of acquiring 100 frames per second in format 7 mode, but when I use this camera with the toolbox, it only acquires 50 frames per second.
Workaround: There is a patch available for R2007b.?To use this patch do the following:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup of the file <matlabroot>/toolbox/imaq/imaqadaptors/win32/mwdcamimaq.dllDownload the attachment and extract the files in it to <matlabroot>/toolbox/imaq/imaqadaptors/win32/Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Code generation error message for unsupported use of Lookup Table (2D) block contains nonfunctional block link
Description: If the Lookup Table (2D) block is configured with the Lookup method parameter set to Interpolation-Extrapolation and code generation is attempted, the Simulink Diagnostics Viewer correctly reports an unsupported use of the lookup method. However, the error message contains a confusing and nonfunctional link to the block: Error: The lookup method: Linear_Extrap is not supported for code generation. Block: '/Lookup Table (2-D)'
Workaround: If you encounter this error message, you can view the highlighted block either by copying the das_dv_hyperlink function call from the nonfunctional block link into the MATLAB command window, or by copying the block path string from the nonfunctional block link into a hilite_system command. For example:>> das_dv_hyperlink('mdl','<Root>/Lookup Table (2-D)')>> hilite_system('<Root>/Lookup Table (2-D)')

Summary: Interactive placement of Region of Interest (ROI) tools does not work properly in figures with non-pixel 'Units'
Description: Interactive placement of ROI tools (impoint , imline , imrect , imellipse , impoly , imfreehand ) or clients of ROI tools (roifill , roipoly , imcrop ) does not behave properly in figures whose 'Units'   property is set to a? non-pixel value.? For example, because GUIDE figures have 'Units'   set to 'characters'  by default, interactive placement of ROI tools does not behave as expected in GUIDE GUIs. This bug results in the following error: ??? Attempted to access start_position(:,1); index out of bounds becausesize(start_position)=[0,0]. Error in ==> basicPolygon>startDrag at 122 inside_polygon = inpolygon(start_x,start_y,... ??? Error using ==> waitforError while evaluating line ButtonDownFcn ??? Attempted to access start_position(:,1); index out of bounds becausesize(start_position)=[0,0]. Error in ==> basicPolygon>startDrag at 122 inside_polygon = inpolygon(start_x,start_y,... ??? Error using ==> waitforError while evaluating line ButtonDownFcn ?
Workaround: To work around this issue, replace the following files in your existing MATLAB installation with the updated versions contained in the attached file.Determine the MATLAB root directory on your machine by typing matlabrootat the MATLAB prompt. This will be referred to as $MATLAB below.Quit MATLAB.Move to the following directory: cd $MATLAB/toolbox/images/imuitools/privateMake a back up copy of the following files.?  mv polygonSymbol.m polygonSymbol.m.oldmv manageInteractivePlacement.m manageInteractivePlacement.m.oldDownload the attached file, extract the two files and store them in the current directory.Restart MATLAB

Summary: If MATLAB is stopped at a breakpoint, and an HG callback is invoked that?throws an error, MATLAB will crash and exit.
Description: If MATLAB is stopped at a breakpoint, and an HG callback is invoked that throws an error, MATLAB will crash and exit. There is no problem if MATLAB is?not at a breakpoint or if the callback does?not throw an error.The following steps cause the seg-v:1. Create the following MATLAB-file:          function foobie        u = uipushtool('CData',rand(16,16,3),'ClickedCallback','error(''BADERROR'',''foo'')')        disp foo;  2. Place a breakpoint at line that has the disp  command, and then run the function.3. While paused at the breakpoint, click the left mouse button.4. Observe that a seg-v occurs.
Workaround: Removing the breakpoint fixes the issue.Another workaround is to let the callback run the first time without a breakpoint. Setting a breakpoint after that will work fine.

Summary: RTW can generate code that does not compile correctly if the model?includes function-calls?that are involved in self loops and have other constant scalar inputs.
Description: RTW will generate code that cannot be compiled if all of the following conditons are met? in a? function-call subsystem:    Subsystem has inputs that come from within its own context, or from other function-call contexts that are called from within it (function-call contexts are expained in the Workaround section).     Subsystem has constant scalar inputs that have non-constant sample time.     Subsystem is set to generate a reusable function.     The 'Inline Parameters' option in the block-diagram is set to?ON.  See the attached model for an example?of this bug.
Workaround: It is not?good modeling practice to use function-call subsystems with inputs that are context-dependent.? Such inputs can change during execution of the funciton-call, and cause the behavior of the function-call to be dependent on how it is connected externally to other blocks. See 'sl_subsys_fncallerr12.mdl', that ships with Simulink, for a more detailed explanation of this issue. The recommended workaround for this bug is to modify the function-call subsystem to elliminate context-dependent inputs.?You can do this by performing the following steps:    Select the 'Warn if function-call inputs are context dependent' checkbox on the subsystem dialog.    Ensure that the model level setting for the 'Context Dependent Inputs' option in the Diagnostics pane (Connectivity section, Function-calls subsection) of the block-diagram is NOT set to 'Disable all'.    Compile the block-diagram and identify all warnings that are referring to the function-call subsystem in question.    Use the 'Latch inputs by copying inside signal' option on all function-call input port blocks that are indicated in the warnings.This should fix the compile error in RTW.For example, in the attached model, you can set the 'Latch inputs by copying inside signal' option on the input port block 'In2' of the subsystem to workaround the bug.Another possible short-term workaround for this issue is to ensure that the constant scalar feeding the function-call subsystem has constant sample time.?For example, in the attached model, you can open the dialog of the Constant block and set its sample time value to 'inf'. This should also fix the bug.

Summary: Running without the Java Virtual Machine, the figure menu item, Copy Figure, will generate a segmentation violation.
Description: When running MATLAB without the Java Virtual Machine (JVM) by using:? matlab -nojvmthe figure menu item, Copy Figure, will generate a segmentation violation.

Summary: Cannot edit inputs for models and test plans
Description: You cannot edit inputs at the command-line for existing test plans or models. Additionally, you cannot edit inputs for test plans after matching data from the command-line or Model Browser.This bug only occurs in R2007b.
Workaround: You can create mbcmodel.modelinput objects and use them to create test plans (using mbcmodel.project.createTestplan) or models (using mbcmodel.CreateModel).Install the attached files to fix the bugs that prevent you editing inputs for existing test plans or models.1. Download the files listed in the Attachments section and save them to these locations:$MATLAB/toolbox/mbc/mbc/@mbcmodel/@model/schema.p$MATLAB/toobox/mbc/mbctools/@mdevtestplan/setInputs.m$MATLAB/toolbox/mbc/mbc/@mbcmodel/@testplan/TSschema.p2. Rename TSschema.p to schema.p3. Restart MATLAB

Summary: Error when running MATLAB-code generated from a figure window containing annotations that are not normalized
Description: If a figure window contains any annotation objects with Units  property set to anything other than 'Normalized' ,?executing MATLAB code generated from that figure window?(created using File->Generate MATLAB file ), the following error occurs:??? Error using ==> annotation at 116X and Y values must be between 0 and 1  The default setting for annotation Units  is 'Normalized'  if you or a script has not changed any of them, the issue will not arise.
Workaround: To work around this issue, be sure that the Units property of all annotations in the figure window is set to 'Normalized' prior to generating MATLAB-code.Once the code is generated, you can subsequently manually modify?it to set the Units property of annotation objects to different values.

Summary: Segmentation violation might occur when bus signals feed root-level Outport blocks
Description: Suppose the top level of your model contains an Outport block to which all of the following conditions apply: The Outport block receives a bus signal as its input. The Outport block's Icon display  parameter specifies either Signal name  or Port number and signal name .  In this case, simulating the model or updating the diagram causes a segmentation violation.
Workaround: To work around this issue, specify Port number as the value of the Icon display parameter for affected Outport blocks in the top level of your model.

Summary: Simulink Find dialog box "Contains Word" option fails when search string contains special characters
Description: The Simulink Find dialog box Contains Word  option fails when the search string contains characters considered special in regular expression searches, such as an asterisk (*) or a period (.). For example, suppose that, using the Contains Word  option,?you search for all blocks in a model whose name begins with Foo.  and the model contains a block named Foo.bar . Searching does not find the block.
Workaround: Use the Find dialog box Regular Expression option to find objects whose properties you are searching for include special characters.

Summary: Colorbar labels and ticks can change color when the figure changes color and the Plotting Tools are opened
Description: If a colorbar is created with a custom label and tick color, as with the following code: surf(peaks)colorbar('XColor',[.9 .9 .9],'YColor',[.9 .9 .9]); If the Color  property of the figure is changed or the figure is redrawn using the refresh  function, the colorbar colors will change. This can also happen if the plotting tools are opened for such a figure. ?

Summary: boxplot errors if plotted?on axes contained in a GUI uipanel
Description: The boxplot  function errors when it tries to draw into axes that are in a panel container created by uipanel . This happens if you create the current axes with a uipanel as their parent, or if you explicitly pass in a handle to such axes.
Workaround: In the boxplot MATLAB-file, change line 391 fromset(get(ax,'parent'),'CurrentAxes',ax);toset(ancestor(ax,'figure'),'CurrentAxes',ax);

Summary: Incorrect code generation: generated code for From Workspace blocks in multiple subsystems is incorrect
Description: The Real-Time Workshop code generator creates one function and reuses it for multiple subsystems with different data values, resulting in incorrect code, when all of the following are true: Two non-virtual and masked?subsystems have the same contents and contain From Workspace blocks The parameter values specified in the two subsystem masks are different The From Workspace blocks in the subsystems use the parameters specified in the masks The subsystems have the Real-Time Workshop system code  parameter set to Reusable function  or Auto .
Workaround: Set the Real-Time Workshop system code parameter for the non-virtual and masked subsystems to Inline or Function.

Summary: Running the power_hvdc12pulse demo in the Rapid Accelerator mode produces an error
Description: If you try to simulate the demo power_hvdc12pulse.mdl  in the Rapid Accelerator mode, you get an error message similar to: Error executing 'build_rapid_accel_target' in MATLAB : Error using ==> tlc_new Error: File: C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2007b\toolbox\physmod\powersys\powersys\tlc_c\discreteNLastSamples.tlc Line: 175 Column: 45 DataTypeIdx is no longer a field within the DataInputPort scope. Execute "helpview ([docroot '/toolbox/rtw/helptargets.map'], 'rtwfileformatchanges')" at the MATLAB command prompt to see how to access that information.

Summary: Inability to swith data type mode to Bus Object for Stateflow data using Data Type Assistant
Description: When you use the Data Type Assistant to specify the data type mode for Stateflow data as Bus Object , the data type mode reverts to the previously set value and the following error message is generated. ??? Error using ==> sfError using ==> parse_data_type_string at 12SL/SF internal error: Failed to parse data type string "Bus: ".Attempted to access remainder(2); index out of bounds because numel(remainder)=1.
Workaround: Use one of the following approaches.(A) Enter or select the data type as Bus: <bus object name> in the data dialog before opening the Data Type Assistant.(B) Use the following installation instructions to apply the patch provided in the Attachments section below to your MATLAB installation to prevent this issue from occurring.Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. This folder is referred to as in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l /<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems*:Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):cdunzip <.zip file>Replace the following files with the corresponding extracted files. matlab/toolbox/simulink/simulink/@Simulink/@DataTypePrmWidget/parseDataTypeString.pmatlab/toolbox/simulink/simulink/@Simulink/@DataTypePrmWidget/callbackDataTypeWidget.pmatlab/toolbox/stateflow/stateflow/private/dataddg.mDelete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start ˇ°WinZipˇ± in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting ˇ°Run as administratorˇ±.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your folder.

Summary: MATLAB crashes during Real-Time Workshop code generation
Description: Generating code using Real-Time Workshop might cause MATLAB to crash when the Configuration Parameters > Real-Time Workshop >Comments > Simulink block comments  check box is selected. The following messages are displayed in the MATLAB Command Window: [0] rtwcg.dll:bool __cdecl RTWCG::AppendBlockComment(class RTWCG::RTWCGNodeTag const *,struct TxtBuf_struct *)[1] rtwcg.dll:void __cdecl RTWCG::EmitStartComment(class RTWCG::RTWCGNodeTag const *,struct TxtBuf_struct *)
Workaround: In the Configuration Parameters > Real-Time Workshop > Comments pane, clear the Simulink block comments check box.

Summary: Why does hdfread not distinguish between identically named data sets in different Vgroups?
Description: Vgroups are like folders or directories on a computer. With folders, you can have files with identical names, and with Vgroups, you can have different data sets with the same name. For example, you can have Vgroups named "/a" and "/b", each with a data set called "c". The full path name of each data set would be "/a/c" and "/b/c". In R2006a, R2006b, and R2007a, attempts to read such data sets that are within top-level Vgroups will only give correct results for the first "c" dataset in the file. For example,  data1 = hdfread ( hdffile, '/a/c' ); % OKdata2 = hdfread ( hdffile, '/b/c' ); % data2 will be wrong In this example, result data2  will be the same as a data1 . NOTE: This issue only effects top-level Vgroups with identically named data sets. If the Vgroups are nested two or more deep,? hdfread  reads the data correctly.
Workaround: Installation instructions?????? Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.?????? Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.?????? Exit MATLAB.?????? Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.?????? Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file. To see the contents of the .zip file: ????????????? ???????????????????? Windows Systems: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.???????????????????? Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/<.zip file>????????????? ?????? ?????? Extract the files from the .zip file. ????????????? ???????????????????? Windows Systems:?????????????????????????? ????????????????????????????????? Open the .zip file.????????????????????????????????? Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.?????????????????????????? ???????????????????? ???????????????????? Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): ?????????????????????????? cd <MATLAB>unzip <.zip file>???????????????????? ????????????? ?????? ?????? Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.

Summary: Stateflow unexpectedly generates errors about using vectors in transition guard conditions
Description: Stateflow versions prior to 8a may generate a spurious parse error when you use vector expressions in transition guard conditions. The error message is:Illegal use of vector in transition guard For example, consider the following transition condition:[foo(u+v)] If u  and v  are vectors and foo  is a function that takes a vector input and returns a scalar, this transition condition would generate the error.
Workaround: Assign the condition expression to an intermediate variable and then use that variable in the transition condition, as in this example:?------------------->o---------------->? {t = foo(u+v);}?????????? [t]?

Summary: CAN Message Unpacking (CANdb) block?outputs incorrect values for signed signals
Description: The CAN Message Unpacking (CANdb) block outputs incorrect values for signed signals if the?length of the signal is?smaller than the word size of the processor (for example,?32-bit for xPC Target).?? This problem affects the following products: xPC Target Target for TI C2000 Target for Infineon C166 Target for Freescale MPC5xx  ?
Workaround: To work around this issue:Save a copy of the file matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\common\can\blocks\tlc_c\EightBytePackingUtils.tlc, for example EightBytePackingUtils.tlc.old.Download the attached file. Unzip, extract, and save it to matlab\toolbox\rtw\targets\common\can\blocks\tlc_c\EightBytePackingUtils.tlcRestart MATLAB.

Summary: MATLAB crashes: calling LibIndentSourceFile causes MATLAB to crash
Description: If you customize file processing to generate custom source code and use the LibIndentSourceFile  function with the pathname or filename of a file that does not exist, MATLAB crashes during the build process.

Summary: Some optimization options are reset when?a?model is closed after the Configuration Parameters Real-Time Workshop System target file option is set to xpctarget.tlc or xpctargetert.tlc
Description: In the model Configuration Parameters dialog box, if you set the following: Optimization pane, select     Block reduction      Signal storage reuse      Inline invariant Signals      Eliminate superfluous temporary variables (Expression folding)   Real-Time Workshop pane, set     System target file  option to xpctarget.tlc  or xpctargetert.tlc   Closing and reopening the model causes the Optimization pane options to be reset.
Workaround: To work around the issue:    Unzip the attached file, optimizationFix.zip, to matlabroot, where matlabroot represents your MATLAB installation root, as returned by the MATLAB command matlabroot.    Restart MATLAB.Note, with this workaround the Optimization pane options are still visually cleared when setting the System target file option to xpctarget.tlc or xpctargetert.tlc. However, any changes to the Optimization pane options will persist in the model for the target application.

Summary: Help browser does not display files on 32-bit Windows platforms for certain installation paths
Description: If the directory in which MATLAB is installed (or any directory above that) contains a?plus sign (+), the Help browser does not display the?documentation.?This only?occurs on 32-bit Windows platforms.
Workaround: Reinstall MATLAB in a directory in which the full pathname does not contain a plus sign (+).

Summary: It is possible to edit the default chart in the Stateflow library
Description: Using the Simulink Library Browser, navigate to the Stateflow libary and then edit the Stateflow chart (i.e., double-click on it) instead of adding it to a model. It will look like you have created a new model and are editing a Stateflow chart in that model. However, you are modifying the underlying Stateflow library.
Workaround: Avoid accidentally editing the Stateflow library. If you do, then simply delete everything in that chart and save it again.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: Stateflow does not regenerate code after you update boxes with parallel decomposition
Description: Stateflow performs incremental code generation. However, when you make changes to boxes with parallel decomposition, Stateflow does not regenerate code either for simulation or Real-Time Workshop targets. For example, consider the following hierarchy: State A contains Box B which in turn contains State C. State A has parallel decomposition. In this model, changes to state C do not appear in generated code.
Workaround: Delete boxes with parallel decomposition, but preserve their contents. Follow these steps to identify and safely delete boxes that cause this error:    Load your model; modelName, making sure to include all Stateflow charts, as follows:                    Load your model by executing this command:>>open_system(modelName)                If your model includes referenced models that contain Stateflow charts, execute these commands as well:         >>mdls = find_mdlrefs(modelName);>>open_system(mdls)                If your model uses libraries that contain Stateflow charts, load the libraries by updating the diagram or by executing this additional command:         >>find_system(modelName,'Masktype','Stateflow','LookUnderMasks','on','FollowLinks','on');;                    Run the attached MATLAB file find_bad_boxes, which returns a vector of handles to the offending box objects.          >>badBoxes = find_bad_boxes        For each box in the the vector, follow these steps:             Open and select the box.                 >>badBoxes(i).view                Manually delete the box, but preserve its contents. The reason for not performing the deletions automatically in a loop is because some of the boxes may have been subcharted and grouped.

Summary: Spurious digit '1' appears in figure window when using LaTeX interpreter
Description: Execute the following code and maximize the figure window. surf(peaks);zlabel('testing','interpreter','latex'); Notice the digit '1' that hovers over the plot and rotates with it.

Summary: Function viewshed issues repeated warnings given an observation point outside the elevation grid
Description: When using the ?viewshed  function, observer's location?should be within the latitude-longitude limits of the elevation grid.?? If the observer is located outside the grid, there is insufficient information to calculate a viewshed, and the warningWarning: Point outside of the grid. > In setpostn at 57 ?? In imbedm at 47 ?? In viewshed at 143? is issued.? The problem is that this warning is repeated many times.(In addition to warning, viewshed  sets all elements of the vismap output to zero (that is, not visible); this behavior is both reasonable and intentional, but has not been documented in the past.)
Workaround: In R2007a (Mapping Toolbox Version 2.5), you can turn off the warning with the commandwarning('off','map:setpostn:pointOutsideLimits')

Summary: Generated code for model containing empty function call subsystem might not compile
Description: The code generated for a model containing a function call subsystem might not compile if all of the following are true: The function call subsystem does not contain executable blocks (for example, contains only Inport, Outport, Ground, or Terminator blocks) The function call initiator is a noninlined S-Function  ?

Summary: There is a bug in TFUTBYPRICE and TFUTBYYIELD
Description: Specifically, the following line in tfutbyprice.m  and tfutbyyield.m  should not have a minus sign after the power symbol:CashFut = (CashPrice - discCFAmnts) .* (1+SpotatMatFut/2).^-(2*(MatFut(:, 1) - SettleFut)/365) ;
Workaround: ?The attached tfutbyprice.m and tfutbyyield.m provide a workaround for this issue.

Summary: Linearizing models using the 'v5' argument of the linmod command can crash MATLAB
Description: Linearizing models that contain Signal Processing Blockset?blocks using the 'v5' argument of the linmod  command might cause MATLAB to crash.

Summary: Generating code for Bitwise Operator blocks might crash MATLAB
Description: If you use Real-Time Workshop to build a model containing a Bitwise Operator block whose input signal does not match one of the CPU's native types, MATLAB crashes. In this case, MATLAB either fails to leave a crash dump or produces an empty crash dump file. Likely setups include the following: Models where uncommon types are used in bitwise operations (e.g., uint11  or int17_En5 ). Models for Texas Instruments or Analog Devices SHARC targets with 16-16-16-32 or all-32 configurations, where non-16 or non-32 bit signals are fed into a Bitwise Operator block.
Workaround: To work around this issue, cast the input signals of a Bitwise Operator block to one of the CPU's native types. For example, suppose that char, short, int, and long are all 32 bits on your CPU. Insert a Data Type Conversion block before affected Bitwise Operator blocks in your model and convert the signals to a uint32 type.Download the attached zip file and extract the script detect_g396284.m. Use this script to locate in a model the blocks that are likely suspects for triggering this issue.

Summary: With the Simulink integration, the Viewer sometimes hangs with 100% CPU consumption after a click in the RTE-view because of unsupported multilines links.
Description: With the Simulink integration, PolySpace Viewer is parsing the generated code and search for HTML links in order to allow a "back to model" feature. Because of some unsupported multilines links, the Viewer will hang and take 100% of the CPU after having clicked the source file containing one of this link in the source code view. ?

Summary: The tstool Import Wizard incorrectly computes uniform time vectors ?for some date string formats
Description: Using the Specify time vector  panel in step 2 of the?tstool Import Wizard to define a uniform time vector based on date strings can cause the resulting time vector to have a sample time of 1 second even if the time interval was specified to be larger. This will occur if the time interval specified is greater than 1 second, and the specified format is one of: dd-mmm-yyyy , HH:MM:SS , HH:MM:SS , HH:MM:SS PM , mmm.dd,yyyy , or HH:MM:SS . That is, the tstool Import Wizard incorrectly computes uniform time vectors based on date strings for some formats.
Workaround: Go to the command line and type:datestr(datenum(STARTDATE_STRING)+(NUMBER_OF_SAMPLES-1)*INTERVAL*tsunitconv('days','seconds'))where STARTDATE_STRING is the string representation of the start date, NUMBER_OF_SAMPLES is the number of samples, and INTERVAL is the correct interval in seconds. Then copy and paste the resulting string into the End time edit box in the Uniform Time Dialog.

Summary: A parent model that references a model that contains a top level Stateflow chart may fail to update or simulate
Description: When a model contains a Model block, the model may fail to update or simulate if: The referenced model is configured for accelerated mode. The referenced model contains a Stateflow block at the root level of the model. At least one signal in the Stateflow chart is marked for logging.  When the parent model is updated or simulated, error messages similar to the following may appear in the MATLAB command window:   Error: File: C:\MATLAB\rtw\c\tlc\lib\utillib.tlc Line: 2214 Column: 55 The specified index (9) was out of the range 0 - 8  Error: File: C:\MATLAB\rtw\c\tlc\lib\utillib.tlc Line: 2214 Column: 69 Ambiguous reference to Subsystem - must use array index to refer to one of multiple scopes  Error: File: C:\MATLAB\rtw\c\tlc\lib\utillib.tlc Line: 2214 Column: 69 The left-hand side of a . operator must be a valid scope identifier  Error: File: C:\MATLAB\rtw\c\tlc\lib\utillib.tlc Line: 2203 Column: 15 The == and != operators can only be used to compare values of the same type  ### Real-Time Workshop build procedure for model: 'referenced_model ' aborted due to an error.

Summary: Fixed-point data with embedded.fi values can't be viewed in Stateflow debugger.
Description: You cannot view fixed-point data with embedded.fi  values in the Browse Data tab of the Stateflow debugger. If you watch an embedded.fi  data value, Stateflow generates an error and terminates the debug session. The error message looks like this:??? Error using ==> sprintf Function is not defined for 'embedded.fi' inputs.

Summary: Printing: the color option in the print dialog box is ignored
Description: You attempt to print a file in color by following these steps, but the file still prints in black and white:?? 1. Select File  > Print .?? 2. Click the Appearance  tab.?? 3. Under Color Appearance , select Color .?? 4. Click Print .
Workaround: Select the syntax highlighting option in the Page Setup dialog box and then print the file in color, as follows:    Select File > Page Setup.    Select the Layout tab.    Under Syntax Highlighting, select Colored text, and then click OK.    Select File > Print.    Click Print.

Summary: Matlab may stop responding if you lock and then unlock the Windows desktop
Description: If you lock and then unlock the Windows desktop, Matlab may stop responding when running in a long loop. This happens only on Windows platforms if there is already a figure or you are creating one, and there is no drawnow  in the body of the loop. Attempts to break out of the loop by pressing CTRL C do not work. You have to wait until the loop completes. Otherwise you might have to end the MATLAB process through the Windows Task Manager.
Workaround: To ensure that Matlab does not stop responding, add drawnow in the body of the loop.

Summary: The MCR Installer does not finish on Windows 64-bit VISTA.
Description: Installer times out with Error 1316: A network error occurred while attempting to read from this file.
Workaround: Uninstall the product and reinstall, per usual procedure.If reinstallation fails, an updated MCR Installer may be required. Please contact Technical Support for a modified installer.

Summary: Locale-specific characters fail to render correctly when output from MATLAB-code running in a Java application
Description: This bug affects MATLAB applications deployed using MATLAB Builder for Java on Windows. Some characters specific to the locale settings on Windows may not appear correctly in MATLAB-code output. These characters are typically Unicode characters outside the ASCII range 0-127.

Summary: The SUBS function may error when subsituting into a 3D symbolic array
Description: The?subs  function may error when substituting into an n -dimensional array for n  > 2.? For example: syms x y z  m = sym(zeros(3,3,3));  m(1,1,1) = x*y*z;mn = subs(m,{x,y,z},{1,2,3}) ??? Error using ==> maple at 129Error, (in index/FillInitVals) array defined with 1 indices, used with 3 indices Error in ==> sym.subs at 224???????? NEWf = sym(maple('evalm',strrep(NEWf,'MATRIX','array')));
Workaround: Perform the subsitution, one variable at a time. For example:subs(subs(subs(m,x,1),y,2),z,3)

Summary: An error occurs when using printpreview in deployed applications.
Description: On Windows, using either the printpreview  command or the Print Preview  menu option in a deployed graphics application and then pressing the Print  button results in PrintImage  returning the following error: PrintImage Error (X) Unknown argument: Helvetica
Workaround: Specify a non-empty header string in the Print Preview dialog box.

Summary: The sim command behavior for default parameter values is incorrectly described
Description: The documentation for the sim  command in R2007b and earlier incorrectly states: You can supply a null ([ ]) matrix for any right-side argument except the first (the model name). The sim  command uses default values for unspecified arguments and arguments specified as null matrices. The default values are the values specified by the model. You can set optional simulation parameters, using the sim  command's options argument. Parameters set in this way override parameters specified by the model.  This description is incorrect. A correct description, plus additional information, appears in the Workaround.
Workaround: The correct description of sim command behavior is as follows:The sim command causes the specified Simulink model to be executed with the data passed to the sim command. This data may include parameter values specified in an options structure. The values in the structure override the values shown for block diagram parameters in the Configuration Parameters dialog box, and the structure may set additional parameters that are not otherwise available (such as DstWorkSpace). The parameters in an options structure are are useful for setting conditions for a specific simulation run.Use the simset command to create an options structure for use by the sim command. The simset command inputs name-value pairs and sets each named parameter to the value indicated. You do not need to specify values for all block diagram parameters that simset accepts. In most cases, an unspecified options parameter defaults to the block diagram value that is current when sim executes the model, with some exceptions. See the simset command documentation for details.The documentation for the simset command in R2007b and earlier does not explicitly state that the block diagram parameter values in an options structure created by simset affect simulation only when the sim command executes with that options structure as an argument. The values shown for block diagram parameters in the Configuration Parameters dialog box remain unchanged.The documentation for the simset command in R2007b and earlier does not list the default values that apply when a call to simset does not specify a value for every block diagram parameter that simset accepts. For most unspecified options parameters, Simulink uses the block diagram value that is current when sim executes the model, but some parameters (such as MaxDataPoints) take on a different value. Additionally, not all parameters accepted by simset have an equivalent block diagram parameter.For each parameter that simset accepts, the attached table (see attached files) shows the equivalent block diagram parameter (if any) and the default value that applies if simset is called with no value specified for that simset parameter.

Summary: The xpcbench function fails when used with the Microsoft Visual Studio .NET 2003 (v7.1) C/C++ compiler
Description: The xpcbench  function fails and returns a TCP/IP Connect Error  message when used with the Microsoft Visual Studio .NET 2003 (v7.1) C/C++ compiler.
Workaround: To work around this issue, if possible use another xPC Target supported compiler:Microsoft Visual Studio (v6.0) C/C++ compilerMicrosoft Visual Studio 8 (v8.0) C/C++ compilerOpen Watcom (v1.3) C/C++ compiler

Summary: The Stateflow Snapshot component of Simulink Report Generator does not correctly report on the Stateflow object description property.
Description: When you set the Caption  option of the Stateflow Snapshot component of Simulink Report Generator to "Description " and then generate a report, the description does not appear in the report.
Workaround: 1. Copy the attached file to the following folder:$MATLAB/toolbox/stateflow/stateflow/@rptgen_sf/@csl_obj_snap/Where $MATLAB is the path returned when you enter the matlabroot command at the MATLAB command prompt.2. Enter the following command at the MATLAB command prompt:rehash toolboxcache;3. Restart the MATLAB session.This patch works with releases R2006b-R2007b. There is no patch available for earlier releases."?

Summary: MATLAB may crash during Simulink model compilation if the model contains a block with a mask callback that deletes other blocks
Description: If the following conditions are true, MATLAB may exit with a segmentation violation when compiling a Simulink model: The model contains a block that has been masked. The mask initialization callback deletes one or more blocks in the model. The deleted blocks are one or more Bus Creators, Bus Selectors, or blocks connected to Bus Creators or Bus Selectors.  A stack trace similar to the following will be reported: Stack Trace: [0] libmwsimulink.dll:void __cdecl RecurseForNonVirtualInfo(class slBlock *,struct SLbus_tag *,bool *)(0x2c99db80, 1, 0x00ceaadb, 0x009dd860) + 45 bytes [1] libmwsimulink.dll:bool __cdecl RefreshCachedBusDueToConversionAtBusCreatorUpstream(class slBlock *)(0x2c99db80, 0x2c9f7040, 0x2c99d6a0, 0x00ceac1c) + 30 bytes [2] libmwsimulink.dll:void __cdecl GetAllBusSelectorAndRefreshCacheAfterExpand(class slModel *,struct slGraph_tag const *,class sl_vector<class slBlock *,class slAllocator<class slBlock *> > *)(0x0fa420a0, 0x2ca10820, 0x00ceac1c, 0x0dc8c660) + 118 bytes [3] libmwsimulink.dll:void __cdecl GetAllBusSelectorAndRefreshCacheAfterExpand(class slModel *,struct slGraph_tag const *,class sl_vector<class slBlock *,class slAllocator<class slBlock *> > *)(0x0fa420a0, 0x2ca10620, 0x00ceac1c, 0x0dc8c180) + 431 bytes
Workaround: Consider preprocessing the model to delete all blocks that need to be deleted before starting model compilation. If the mask initialization code of a block deletes blocks in a model, the callback can be triggered by manually moving the block. If the list of masked blocks is known, an MATLAB script can be written that slightly alters the position of the block by executing:    oldPosition = get_param(block_handle, 'Position');    newPosition = oldPosition+1;    set_param(block_handle, 'Position', newPosition);Changing the position of the block will trigger the mask initialization callback. These workarounds must be applied before model simulation. Please note that the model will contain unsaved changes after block position alteration. If required, please resave the model before starting simulation.

Summary: Rate Limiter block with unsigned inputs produces incorrect results
Description: Suppose your model contains a Rate Limiter block whose input signal has an unsigned data type. If you execute the build process, Real-Time Workshop generates code that returns wrong answers. Similarly, if you simulate the model using an acceleration mode, Simulink produces wrong answers. However, the results that you obtain from Normal mode simulation are correct.
Workaround: To work around this issue, specify only signed data types for a Rate Limiter block's input signal. For example, to alter the signedness of a Rate Limiter block's input signal, add a Data Type Conversion block that specifies a signed data type with precision doubled. You can insert another Data Type Conversion block after the Rate Limiter block to change its output signal back to an unsigned data type.

Summary: Specifying the Transfer Fcn First Order block's pole as one causes a failed assertion
Description: Suppose your model contains a Transfer Fcn First Order block. If you specify its Pole (in Z plane)  parameter as one, Simulink generates a failed assertion with a stack trace that begins as follows: Assertion failed: Forced Assertion at line 714 of file ".\memmgr\mem32aligned.cpp".Corrupted memory block foundStack Trace:  [0] mcr.dll:_mnSignalHandler(0xffffffff, 0, 0, 0x8484d2cc) + 385 bytes  [1] mcr.dll:void __cdecl ThrowAssertion(void)(0x00f25068, 0x18540000, 0x18540024, 0x65737341) + 167 bytes  [2] mcr.dll:_MATLABAssertFcn(0x7abec038 ": Forced Assertion", 0x7abec700 ".\memmgr\mem32aligned.cpp", 714, 0x7abecaac "Corrupted memory block found") + 124 bytes  [3] libut.dll:_mw_malloc32(2, 0x00ceaa8c "$??", 0x7ab9b6e4, 2) + 289 bytes  [4] libut.dll:_utMalloc(2, 0x174eb480, 0x00ceab24 "\??", 0x6f9af1a8) + 90 bytes  [5] libut.dll:_utStrdup(0x18540028, 0x17622220, 130, 0x159e5820) + 36 bytes

Summary: Build process gmake errors when an incompatible sh.exe is on the Microsoft Windows path
Description: Build process errors can occur if you have an incompatible application called sh.exe  on your Microsoft Windows path. The error message produced by gmake.exe  during the build process is similar to the following example: R2007b\bin\win32\gmake.exe: *** [exceptions.s] Error 2 If gmake.exe  can find a sh.exe  on the Windows path, it is used instead of the Windows cmd.exe  to execute applications as part of the build process. The use of an incompatible sh.exe  causes errors.
Workaround: To resolve the gmake errors, you can use the which command to find all sh.exe on the Microsoft Windows path, then rename each one (e.g., to "sh.exe_rename"). This ensures that gmake.exe will not use them and will use cmd.exe instead. To find instances of sh.exe, run this command in MATLAB:!which sh.exeYou may?need to run the which command multiple times to find all the sh.exe. This command will only work if you have which.exe installed and on the path. It is likely that which.exe has been installed with sh.exe.?As an alternative, if you do not have which.exe installed, you can run the following MATLAB-code in MATLAB to print out all the directories where a sh.exe can be found on the Microsoft Windows path:[stat, res] = system('path');if stat == 0,?[tok, remainder] = strtok(res, '=');?remainder = remainder(2:end);?while ~isempty(remainder),??? [tok, remainder] = strtok(remainder, ';');??? tok = strrep(tok, sprintf('\n'), '');??? d = dir([tok '\sh.exe']);??? if ~isempty(d),???? ?disp(['Found sh.exe: ' tok]);??? end,??? remainder = remainder(2:end);?end,end,

Summary: Accelerator or Rapid Accelerator mode:?possible?incorrect results if single-rate model without continuous sample time?uses variable-step solver
Description: A model running in Accelerator mode or Rapid Accelerator mode may produce incorrect results if all of the following conditions are true: The model uses a variable-step solver. The model does not use a continous sample time. The model is single rate. The Max step size  specified on the Solver  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box is smaller than the discrete sample time.
Workaround: If your model does not contain a continuous block, you can work around this bug by using a fixed-step solver.

Summary: sw2dtool images disappear if decomposition level changed
Description: In the Stationary Wavelet Transform Denoising 2-D Tool (sw2dtool ), if you change the wavelet decomposition level, the original, denoised, and histogram images disappear from the display.?
Workaround: Copy the file in the Attachments below into this directory:matlab/toolbox/wavelet/wavelet

Summary: Unexpected errors when certain Mapping Toolbox functions run out of memory
Description: If MATLAB runs out of memory when you are trying to do one of the following operations:    Write a shapefile with shapewrite      Use makedbfspec  to prepare for a call to shapewrite      Use geoshow  or mapshow  with a symbolspec   it is possible that MATLAB could report an error other the standard 'MATLAB:nomem'  error, which could obscure the cause of the problem.
Workaround: To avoid generating unexpected error messages, replace the attributes2properties.m and makedbfspec.m function files in your installation with the versions in the attachment, as follows:    Exit MATLAB.    Make a backup copy of the following files.    MATLABROOT\toolbox\map\map\private\attributes2properties.m    MATLABROOT\toolbox\map\mapformats\makedbfspec.m    MATLABROOT represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function.    For example, rename the existing files, adding "old" to the file extension: attributes2properties.m.old and makedbfspec.m.old        Copy the versions of attributes2properties.m and makedbfspec.m from the attachment into the directories specified in the previous step.    Restart MATLAB.    Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: MATLAB console mode displays warning when suspended and garbage when MATLAB is resumed.
Description: When MATLAB is running in console mode (-nojvm , -nodesktop ) and is suspended via CTRL-Z , the following warning may be displayed: Warning: Error reading character from command line. Unknown error 512 The terminal also displays garbage on resume whenever special keys are used, such as page up, arrow down, etc.
Workaround: If a command prompt is needed, do not suspend MATLAB. Instead:1. Start a new terminal.2. Either?use the MATLAB system function?or the MATLAB bang(!) operator.

Summary: UEI PD2-AO-96/16 block ignores any request to set an initial or final reset value
Description: The UEI PD2-AO-96/16 block ignores any request to set an initial or final reset value.
Workaround: As a workaround for xPC Target 3.1 (R2006b) or 3.2 (R2007a), download and incorporate the attached file, as follows:    Quit MATLAB.    Download the attached file, AO96_A1.zip, and unzip it to matlabroot, where matlabroot represents your MATLAB installation root, as returned by the MATLAB command matlabroot.    Restart MATLAB.    Rebuild your models.

Summary: The Model Explorer's Search control loses focus when used with keyboard
Description: Using arrow keys to select an option from the drop-down list of options displayed by the Search  control on the Model Explorer's tool bar causes the keyboard focus to move to the next control on the tool bar.

Summary: Using Glob within Perl errors out.
Description: MATLAB V7.5 uses a fully-qualified path name to call Perl.? Calling Glob from?within Perl requires that the perlglob.exe file reside on the system path.? If it is not on the system path, it will error out.
Workaround: Add the directory %your_matlab_directory%\sys\perl\%your_windows_arch%\bin to your system path.

Summary: Code generation fails to provide useful error message when Async Interrupt S-function block used with Generate reusable code
Description: The Async Interrupt S-function block, used to drive function-call subsystems or Task Sync blocks, does not support code reuse. If you try to use an asynchronous S-function to drive function calls in a reusable subsystem, the software displays an informative error message. However, if the asynchronous S-function is located at root level of your model, and if you select the Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder model option Generate reusable code , building the model fails with "undeclared identifier " compilation errors instead of providing a useful error message during code generation.

Summary: Model Explorer displays an error when searching for blocks that support fixed-point data types
Description: Suppose that your model contains either of the following blocks: Time-Based Linearization Trigger-Based Linearization  If you use Model Explorer to search your model for fixed-point-capable blocks, it fails to complete the task and displays the message "Incomplete search."
Workaround: To work around this issue, temporarily remove the Time-Based Linearization or Trigger-Based Linearization blocks from your model and then repeat the search.

Summary: Using Virtual Reality Toolbox Viewer with Simulink External Mode Causes Warnings
Description: The Virtual Reality Toolbox viewer does not support the Start , Pause , and Stop  buttons in Simulink external mode. Pressing these buttons in Simulink external mode generates warnings in the MATLAB Command Window.

Summary: Function model_SetEventsForThisBaseStep not removed from model.h when generating VxWorks example main program
Description: When Real-Time Workshop generates code for an ERT-based multitasking model that is configured to generate a VxWorks example main program, the generated code correctly omits entry-point function model _SetEventsForThisBaseStep  from model .c/.cpp , but incorrectly generates a declaration for the function in model .h .

Summary: Compilation of generated shared utility function that uses non 8/16/32/64-bit data types fails with undefined identifier errors
Description: If a generated shared utility function in a shared utility directory uses data types with nonstandard numbers of bits, for example, non 8/16/32/64-bit data types, the Real-Time Workshop compilation of the generated shared utility function fails with errors of the form   line n : error: identifier "name " is undefined where n   is a line number and name   is an identifier name.
Workaround: To work around this bug, disable the use of a shared location for utility function generation, for example, by selecting Auto for the Utility function generation parameter on the Real-Time Workshop > Interface pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box.

Summary: Lookup Table Editor does not save changes when closing
Description: If you have unsaved changes in the Lookup Table Editor and close it without first saving the changes, it prompts you to save the changes but appears to ignore a Yes  answer when the block dialog box is open. This problem is most likely to happen if you click Edit  in the dialog of a Lookup Table block, edit the table, and close the Lookup Table Editor without saving.
Workaround: After making changes to data in the Lookup Table Editor, select File > Update Block Data. Performing this step guarantees that changes are saved. However, this workaround does not work in R2007a. If you use R2007a, please explicitly save any changes before closing the Lookup Table Editor.

Summary: Closing a referenced model after searching in the Model Explorer may cause MATLAB to crash
Description: If shortcuts to a referenced model appear in the Model Explorer's search results pane and you close the referenced model, MATLAB may crash.
Workaround: Click on the 'Contents' tab in the Model Explorer before closing a referenced model after a search.

Summary: MATLAB Compiler and builder products fail to compile MATLAB code that use the same variable name for input and output variables
Description: If any MATLAB file that is passed to the mcc  command has the same?name for both input and output variables, the resulting compilation fails with errors. These errors also occur when?using DEPLOYTOOL when the MATLAB file with the same variable name for input and output is added to either the "Exported function" category or a user-defined class for the builder targets.
Workaround: To workaround this problem, change either of the names of the input or the output variables, ensuring they are?no longer the same.??

Summary: Incorrect range checking for Simulink data objects whose data types specify slope and bias scaling
Description: Simulink data objects, such as Simulink.Parameter  and Simulink.Signal  objects, perform range checking of their values. For instance, Simulink alerts you when the Value  field of a parameter object lies outside a range that corresponds to its specified Minimum  and Maximum  values and data type. However, for data types that specify slope and bias scaling, Simulink Fixed Point incorrectly calculates the representable range and reports an error.

Summary: Linearizing a Bus Selector block might generate an error
Description: Suppose your model contains a Bus Selector block whose input bus contains many signals. If you linearize the model by invoking a command such as [A, B, C, D] = linmod('modelname ')  Simulink might hang or display the following error message: ??? Error using ==> fevalThe model contains too many elements for linearization.Please reduce the model size. This issue occurs because the Bus Selector block Jacobian incorrectly allocates memory for a full "D" matrix instead of using a sparse matrix during linearization. Consequently, the linearization code exceeds its 32-bit limitation.

Summary: uigetfile throws a java exception in the MATLAB command window
Description: The uigetfile  function occassionally throws a java exception in the MATLAB command window. >> [a,b] = uigetfile('/home/ssimon/test.m') ??? Java exception occurred: java.lang.IndexOutOfBoundsException: Invalid index at  javax.swing.DefaultRowSorter.convertRowIndexToModel(Unknown  Source) at  sun.swing.FilePane$SortableListModel.getElementAt(Unknown  Source) at  javax.swing.plaf.basic.BasicListUI.updateLayoutState(Unknown  Source) at  javax.swing.plaf.basic.BasicListUI.maybeUpdateLayoutState(Unknown  Source) ... The exception occured due to some thread unsafe operations in Java.

Summary: Blocks with external?X0 ports may override initial state specified in the configuration set
Description: The externally provided initial state can override the initial value provided in the configuration set when an external X0 port is used.
Workaround: Create a variable in the workspace and use the variable name for both the external X0 and in the configuration set. ?Alternatively, do not simultaneously use external X0 and an initial state in the configuration set.

Summary: NullPointerException displayed when opening certain files in the Import Wizard
Description: When opening certain files in the Import Wizard, the following exception is displayed to the MATLAB Command Window:Exception in thread "AWT-EventQueue-0" java.lang.NullPointerException ?? at com.mathworks.mde.dataimport.DataSourceSelectionPanel.setPreviewTextContents(DataSourceSelectionPanel.java:298) ?? at com.mathworks.mde.dataimport.DataSourceSelectionPanel.refreshPreviewBorder(DataSourceSelectionPanel.java:530) ?? at com.mathworks.mde.dataimport.DataSourceSelectionPanel.setFileToImport(DataSourceSelectionPanel.java:506) ?? at com.mathworks.mde.dataimport.DataSourceSelectionPanel.calculateNextAvailable(DataSourceSelectionPanel.java:540) ?? at com.mathworks.mde.dataimport.DataSourceSelectionPanel.handleNextEnabled(DataSourceSelectionPanel.java:486) ?? at com.mathworks.mde.dataimport.DataSourceSelectionPanel.access$400(DataSourceSelectionPanel.java:23) ?? at com.mathworks.mde.dataimport.DataSourceSelectionPanel$BrowserListener.run(DataSourceSelectionPanel.java:251) ?? at java.awt.event.InvocationEvent.dispatch(Unknown Source) ?? at java.awt.EventQueue.dispatchEvent(Unknown Source) ?? at java.awt.EventDispatchThread.pumpOneEventForHierarchy(Unknown Source) ?? at java.awt.EventDispatchThread.pumpEventsForHierarchy(Unknown Source) ?? at java.awt.EventDispatchThread.pumpEvents(Unknown Source) ?? at java.awt.EventDispatchThread.pumpEvents(Unknown Source) ?? at java.awt.EventDispatchThread.run(Unknown Source) After this, data import can proceed normally

Summary: I am not able to save a Simulink?model as an earlier version if 'UpdateHistory' is?set to 'UpdateHistoryWhenSave'
Description: In Simulink 6.6 (R2007a), a simulink version 6.6 (R2007a) library cannot be saved as a Simulink 6.3 (R14SP3) library when using SaveAs  on the File  menu unless the model parameter UpdateHistory  is set to UpdateHistoryNever.
Workaround: To save a library to an earlier release, use?the MATLAB? save_system command instead of? SaveAs on the Simulink File menu.

Summary: Incorrect substitution performed by the SUBS command with matrix arguments
Description: The?subs  function may perform an incorrect substitution when substituting matrix inputs into a scalar expression. For example: syms a b c d subs(a+b+c+d, [a b;c d], [1 2;3 4])
Workaround: Perform the substitution as vectors instead of matrices. For example:syms a b c d subs(a+b+c+d, [a b c d], [1 2 3 4])

Summary: Sol64: Build procedure can error out during call to make command
Description: On 64-bit Solaris SPARC platforms, the Real-Time Workshop build procedure can generate a fatal error (such as Unexpected end of line seen ) if a make utility other than gmake  appears earlier on the MATLAB search path than the MATLAB-installed gmake . The error is caused by incompatible syntax between the generated makefile, which is intended for use with gmake , and the make utility that is called.
Workaround: To work around this bug, modify the MAKECMD macro in the following template makefiles, as well as in any custom template makefiles?that you use:Note: In the following list, matlabroot represents your MATLAB installation root, as returned by the MATLAB command matlabroot.    matlabroot/rtw/c/accel/accel_unix.tmf    matlabroot/rtw/c/ert/ert_unix.tmf    matlabroot/rtw/c/grt/grt_unix.tmf    matlabroot/rtw/c/grt_malloc/grt_malloc_unix.tmf    matlabroot/rtw/c/modelref/modelrefsim_unix.tmf    matlabroot/rtw/c/raccel/raccel_unix.tmf    matlabroot/rtw/c/rsim/rsim_unix.tmf    matlabroot/rtw/c/rtwsfcn/rtwsfcn_unix.tmfThe MAKECMD line in the template makefiles should be changed to the following:MAKECMD         = |>MATLAB_ROOT<|/bin/sol64/gmake

Summary: Accelerating model with linked library block that contains S-Function Builder block might generate an error
Description: Suppose your model includes a linked library block that contains an S-Function Builder block. Further, the S-Function Builder block references external code, such as libraries, object files, and source files. If you simulate the model in an accelerated mode, for example, by selecting Accelerator  from the Simulation  menu, Simulink displays a link error like the following: ### Linking ...C:/PROGRA~1/MATLAB/R2007b/bin\mex.bat -win32 OPTIMFLAGS="-DNDEBUG" -f  C:/WINNT/Profiles/user/APPLIC~1/MATHWO~1/MATLAB/R2007b/mexopts.bat  -outdir .. model_acc.obj model_acc_data.obj model_wrapper.obj  C:/PROGRA~1/MATLAB/R2007b/rtw/c/lib/win32\rtwlib_rtwsfcn_lcc.lib  C:/PROGRA~1/MATLAB/R2007b\extern\lib\win32\lcc\libut.lib  C:/PROGRA~1/MATLAB/R2007b\extern\lib\win32\lcc\libmwmathutil.lib Writing library for model_acc.mexw32model_wrapper.obj .text: undefined reference to '_func'  C:\PROGRA~1\MATLAB\R2007B\BIN\MEX.PL: Error: Link of '..\model_acc.mexw32' failed.gmake: *** [../model_acc.mexw32] Error 2### Real-Time Workshop build procedure for model: 'model' aborted due to an error.
Workaround: To work around this issue, disable the library block's link before simulating the model in an accelerated mode.

Summary: Generated ERT S-function code?does not compile if nonvirtual bus input signal has non-Auto storage class.
Description: Real-Time Workshop generates uncompilable ERT S-function code under the following conditions: The source model has a nonvirtual bus input signal with a storage class other than Auto . An ERT-based target is selected for the model on the Real-Time Workshop  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box. Create Simulink (S-Function) block  is selected on the Interface  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box.  The same failure occurs if you use subsystem-based S-function code generation, under the following conditions: The selected subsystem has a nonvirtual bus input signal with a storage class other than Auto . Use Embedded Coder  is selected in the dialog box that appears when you select Real-Time Workshop > Generate S-Function  from the Tools menu or the subsystem right-click menu.
Workaround: To work around this bug in R2007b, perform the following steps.Note: In the following instructions, matlabroot represents your MATLAB installation root folder, as returned by the MATLAB command matlabroot.In your MATLAB installation tree, rename or make a backup copy of the following file:matlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/mw/ertsfcnbody.tlcDownload the attached .zip archive file and extract the file ertsfcnbody.tlc from the archive.Place the extracted file in the same folder as the file that you renamed or copied.Restart MATLAB.For earlier releases, please contact Technical Support.
