Summary: dpss with invalid inputs causes segmentation violation in MATLAB
Description: If you enter invalid inputs to the dpss  function, such as dsps(0,nan) , a segmentation violation occurs and MATLAB stops running.? The input arguments are not being validated.

Summary: Name conflict with libmmd.dll
Description: When deploying a MATLAB Compiler-generated application to a Windows computer that has the Intel Fortran Compiler installed, the following error is received: lapack.dll The specified procedure cannot be found.  This error happens during during the execution of the deployed application, and is caused by the loading of an improper version of libmmd.dll.  This error is an operating system message that usually appears in a message box during initialization of the application.
Workaround: This problem is caused by a conflict with libmmd.dll, which is part of the Intel Fortran Compiler. The MathWorks also redistributes this DLL with MATLAB as a third party math library. The problem arises because Intel's Fortran Compiler product installs this library into the system32 directory on Windows machines. MathWorks installs this library in the product directory <matlab>/bin/win32. Since Windows always searches system32 first, it will always pick the library in system32.To workaround this problem, rename or otherwise remove libmmd.dll form the Windows system32 directory before running an application built with the MATLAB Compiler. Before running an application built with The Intel Fortran Compiler, restore the original file in system32, or place this file into a separate directory (not system32) and add this directory to the SYSTEM path.

Summary: MATLAB exceeds Java Heap Space when parsing MZXML files with the MZXMLREAD function.
Description: MATLAB exceeds Java Heap Space when parsing MZXML files larger than 100 Mb with the MZXMLREAD  function.
Workaround: 1. In the MATLAB Command Window type:which mzxmlreadThis displays the location of the file mzxmlread.m.2. Rename mzxmlread.m to mzxmlread.old.3. Download the updated file attached to this bug report to the directory identified with the which command.4. Restart MATLAB and in the command window type:rehash toolboxcache

Summary: Inconsistent dynamic states do not result in a warning or error for models with dependent dynamic states and user-specified initial states
Description: When dependent dynamic states are present in a Simscape or SimHydraulics model, and the initial state is set by the user directly (either through Simulation >Configuration Parameters >Data Import-Export >Initial state , or by passing it as an argument to Simulink linearization commands such as linmod  and dlinmod ), no warning or error results when the dependent dynamic states are inconsistent.?This issue does not arise for models without dependent dynamic states, or for models having dependent dynamic states if the initial states are computed by Simscape using information from initial conditions on individual components (which is the recommended technique).
Workaround: To avoid this issue, user-specified initial state vectors should be taken from some output time in another simulation run.?You can use?the output at the start time only if the model was not run with user-specified initial states in that simulation run.

Summary: num2str may have errors interacting with functions named "u"
Description: If num2str  is called and a function "u" is already loaded, the following error may occur:??? Error: File: num2str.m Line: 150 Column: 13 "u" previously appeared to be used as a function or command, conflicting with its use here as the name of a variable. A possible cause of this error is that you forgot to initialize the variable, or you have initialized it implicitly using load or eval.
Workaround: Avoid naming your functions "u".

Summary: Calling the watershed function on an array with more than 2^31 elements can cause a segmentation violation
Description: When running MATLAB on a 64-bit platform, calling the watershed  function on an array with more than 2^31 elements can cause a segmentation violation.
Workaround: To workaround this issue, replace the bwlabel2 MEX-file in your current installation with the updated version contained in the attached file. Choose the version of the MEX-file that is appropriate for you 64-bit platform. For example, if you use a 64-bit Windows system, choose bwlabel2.mexw64.Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of your existing bwlabel2 MEX-file in the following directory. matlabroot\toolbox\images\images\privatematlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension.Download the attached file, extract the version appropriate for your platform, and store it in the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Warnings may be issued when opening a model in Simulink R14sp3 that was created in R2007a, but saved as R14sp3
Description: Warnings such as the following?will be issued when using R14sp3 to open a model that was originally created in R2007a but saved as R14sp3:   Warning: bus_merge_error14sp3.mdl, line 454: This model contains objects of class 'hdlcoderui.hdlcc' that can not be instantiated. If you save this model, the information associated with these objects will be lost. > In general\private\openmdl at 13 ?? In open at 141 ?? In uiopen at 181 Warning: bus_merge_error14sp3.mdl, line 581: Invalid setting in SubSystem block 'Subsystem' for parameter 'ShowPortLabels'. > In general\private\openmdl at 13 ?? In open at 141 ?? In uiopen at 181 An older version of Simulink may not be capable of recognizing all of the new features present in a later release.? Because the precise message is determined by model features which Simulink does not recognize, the error message you receive may not exactly match the above example.  ?
Workaround: These warnings may be ignored.?To eliminate these types of errors, first save the model in the older version of Simulink, then reload the file.Note: If the R14sp3 model is brought back into R2007a, the information lost in the conversion from R2007a to R14sp3 will not be restored.

Summary: "Invalid RSRCNAME" error occurs when you use Tektronics USB VISA Resource strings
Description: If you use Tektronics VISA, you may encounter errors when the RSRCNAME begins with USB:>> v = visa('tek', 'USB0::1689::874::C033273::0::INSTR');  A typical error may be: ??? Error using ==> visa.visa Invalid RSRCNAME specified. Type 'instrhelp visa' for more information.
Workaround: You can work around the problem by adding a colon to the end of your RSRCNAME as shown in the following example:>> v = visa('tek', 'USB0::1689::874::C033273::0::INSTR:');This workaround will work in versions of MATLAB up to and including 2007b. ?

Summary: Segmentation violation sometimes caused by figure?WindowButtonDownFcn called within a WindowButtonUpFcn

Summary: Assignment to a property of an uninitialized structure in a libpointer might cause an access violation.

Summary: Creating and deleting interface objects repeatedly results in a memory leak and MATLAB stops responding
Description: When you create and delete GPIB and TCPIP objects repeatedly, it results in memory leaks. This can eventually lead to MATLAB not responding.
Workaround: Avoid repeatedly creating interface objects if possible.For example instead of the following code:while 1     t = tcpip('localhost',7);     fopen(t);     fclose(t);     delete(t);     pause(0.1); end You should create the object outside the loop:t = tcpip('localhost',7); while 1     fopen(t);     fclose(t);     pause(0.1); end delete(t);

Save the following files as cfr_paragraph.p.old and cfr_titlepage.p.old, respectively:matlabroot/toolbox/matlab/iofun/@rptgen/@cfr_paragraph/cfr_paragraph.pmatlabroot/toolbox/matlab/iofun/@rptgen/@cfr_titlepage/cfr_titlepage.p2. Summary: Plot Tools does not update information after calls to slice
Description: If you run the following?code, x1 = [-1:0.1:1]; x2 = [-1:0.1:1]; x3 = [-1:0.1:1]; [X1, X2, X3] = meshgrid(x1, x2, x3); plottools pause slice(X1,X2,X3,X1+X2+X3,0,0,0); The figure is displayed, but the plot browser does not display the surfaces. Summary: Undefined references during linking of shared RTW libraries using LCC (LCCLIB.EXE) for Simulink models with a large number of files.
Description: If LCC is selected as the compiler environment (the default) LCCLIB.exe will be used to create shared RTW libraries in?the linking process.?However, there is a bug in LCCLIB.exe that if the number of files is exceeding a certain limit (not possible to specify exactly), then it fails to open those files and are thus being ignored. Summary: Simulink crashes during simulation when certain signals are logged in a submodel.
Description: When a parent model is simulated, Simulink will crash if all of the following are true:1. A signal is marked for logging within a referenced model.2. The data type of the logged signal is a user-defined data type.????Example: a?custom data type registered from a user written S-function.
Workaround: Within a referenced model, do not log any signal that has a user-defined data type.

Summary: A model that assigns bus signals using a Bus Assignment block may produce invalid code
Description: Simulink disallows assignment of bus signals by Bus Assignment blocks. Updating a model that assigns a bus signal therefore fails. Simulink posts an error message similar to: Signal 'a' is a bus and a bus cannot be assigned in block 'Model/Bus Assignment1'.  However, generating code for a model that assigns a bus signal appears to succeed. No warning or error message appears, but the generated code is invalid.
Workaround: Do not connect a bus signal to an assignment port of a Bus Assignment block. To check whether such a connection exists, update the model (Edit > Update Diagram or Ctrl-D) before generating code. Simulink will generate an error if any Bus Assignment block assigns a bus signal.

Summary: Adding Stateflow charts to a new model can cause memory segmentation errors or crash MATLAB
Description: Adding Stateflow charts to a new model causes memory leaks that can in turn lead to memory segmentation errors or a MATLAB crash.

Summary: Snapshot linearization may cause false alarms in array bounds checking
Description: False array bounds checking  errors can occur ?when performing snapshot linearization of models containing time-based linearization blocks.
Workaround: Disable array bounds checking to prevent false errors if your model contains time-based linearization blocksTo do this:        From the Configuration Parameters dialog select the Diagnostics Data Validity pane            In the Debugging group, navigate to the Array bounds exceeded option, and select none

Summary: A NullReferenceException is thrown when accessing empty elements in a cell array.
Description: Indexing an empty element of a cell array causes a NullReferenceException  to be thrown. The indexed element contains a null reference rather than a pointer to an empty MWArray  object.
Workaround: Place the indexing of a cell array that might contain an empty element within a try catch block and handle the exception as follows:          int idx= 1;          int lastIdx= cellArray.NumberOfElements;          MWArray arrElement= null;          while (idx <= lastIdx)            {              try                {                  arrElement= cellArray[idx++];                  // do something with arrElement                }              catch (OutOfMemoryException /*NullReferenceException*/ ex)                {                  cellArray[--idx]= MWNumericArray.Empty;                }            }

Summary: FSOLVE, LSQCURVEFIT, and LSQNONLIN error if the vector of nonlinear equation values is sparse
Description: An error occurs inside FSOLVE, LSQCURVEFIT, and LSQNONLIN if the user-supplied system of equations returns their values in an array of sparse storage format. When these solvers are applied to typical problems, this bug will not occur because a sparse array is not memory efficient or is not needed to store the values of the system.This error occurs when displaying the sum of squares of the equation values:??? Error using ==> sprintfFunction is not defined for sparse inputs.
Workaround: To workaround this bug, store the values in a full array when defining the system of equations. Otherwise, the array of equation values can be converted to the full storage class at a later point. For example, if F is a sparse array, convert it as follows:F = full(F);

Summary: LINPROG large-scale algorithm can error if there are fixed variables in the problem definition and lambda is required as output argument.

Summary: Why can I not pass in enumerated data types and return long types to/from my COM object?
Description: I have a function that accepts an enumeration value and returns a long:  dispinterface ITest { properties: methods: [ id(0) ] long?? SetYesNo(ETest eYesNo); }; When I observe this COM method from MATLAB, it reports the signature as:? ETest SetYesNo(handle, int32)
Workaround: Use the invoke command as a workaround.

Summary: LOADLIBRARY does not create function with correct input parameters?when function prototype argument list contains a single blank space.
Description: When LOADLIBRARY reads a function prototype?with a blank input argument list,?it creates a function requiring one input.? For example,?when calling the function foo  declared as: int foo(  );   // blank space between the parentheses the usage displayed indicates that foo requires a VoidPtr  as an input.?MATLAB generates the error? no function with matching signature found. This is an?old-style?C function declaration?for?a function with an undefined number of inputs.??In modern?(C99) and ANSI-strict?C, this syntax is?illegal.??To properly declare a function with no inputs?in?C, it must be declared void . For example:int foo(void);
Workaround: Choices for way to work around:Call the function with a dummy input argument.Fix the function declaration.Generate a loader?MATLAB file with the correct function declaration.

Summary: Sporadic crash when using Simulink menus
Description: Using the Simulink menus (in particular, the context menu) can crash MATLAB.? To tell if a MATLAB crash is caused by this problem, examine the the stack trace resulting from the crash. It should contain the following line:Stack Trace: ?? [0] libmwsimulink.dll:bool __cdecl get_selected(struct MWObject_tag * *)

Description: While you are navigating in the Current Directory Browser on a Linux platform, the Command Window might display the following message: Exception in thread "AWT-EventQueue-0" java.lang.NullPointerException ??????? at java.io.File.<init>(Unknown Source) ??????? at com.mathworks.mde.filebrowser.FileView. Summary: The quivergroup ShowArrowHead property loses its effect when the Visible property changes
Description: Execute the following code:? [X,Y] = meshgrid(-2:0.25:2,-1:0.2:1); Z = X.* exp(-X.^2 - Y.^2); [U,V,W] = surfnorm(X,Y,Z); h=quiver3(X,Y,Z,U,V,W,0.5); set(h,'ShowArrowHead','off') set(h,'visible','off') set(h,'visible','on') The arrows reappear in the plot even though the? ShowArrowHead  property of h  remains 'off' .
Workaround: Manually set the ShowArrowHead property after changing the Visible property of the quivergroup.

Summary: trim function?sometimes?hangs during optimization
Description: The trim  function uses numerical optimization to?compute the steady state, or equilibrium, conditions of a Simulink model.? In some?limited cases, the numerical optimization gets caught in an infinite loop.??You can use ctrl-C to break the infinite loop.? This may leave the Simulink model in a compiled state.? To determine?if you model is in a compiled state, typestatus = get_param(mdlname,'SimulationStatus'); If this command returns 'paused', then your model is compiled.? To terminate the command, type mdlname([],[],[],'term');
Workaround: You can use the?findop function in the Simulink Control Design software to compute steady state operating points.? findop?provides enhanced functionality for computing operating points, also known as trim conditions, for a Simulink model, including?    An enhanced optimization routine with no infinite loop condition.? findop also allows different optimization methods to be selected if the Optimization Toolbox software is installed.    An object oriented interface that simplifies working with models containing a large number of states.?    Support for steady state operating point searches for hybrid simulations and models containing model reference blocks.

Summary: Executing the OnCallback or OffCallback of a uitoggletool causes MATLAB to crash if the callback deletes the uitoggletool object.
Description: If the code of an?OnCallback  or OffCallback  of a uitoggletool object deletes the uitoggletool object itself, clicking on the uitoggletool causes MATLAB to crash.
Workaround: If the callback for the uitoggletool has to be configured such that it will delete the tool object itself, you can use the ClickedCallback instead and take appropriate action based on the value of the 'State' property within the callback.t = uitoggletool;set(t, 'ClickedCallback', 'clickcbk');function clickcbkstate = get(gcbo, 'State');if strcmpi(state, 'On')??? disp('State is On');else??? disp('State is Off');end

Description: Copying to clipboard leaves out signal lines, specifically signal represented as double line i.e. e.g. frame-based signals - basically the lines do not appear on the pasted clipboard data.

Summary: The?properties of a colorbar?are modified when?it refreshes
Description: When a colorbar is created with its Location  property set to either SouthOutside  or NorthOutside , manually changing its Position  property may cause the location of the X-axis of the colorbar to change.figure;subplot(1,2,1)x=rand(100,100);imshow(x);ax1h = gca;cxh = colorbar; set(cxh, 'Location','SouthOutside'); % XAxis Location is Bottom now pos = get(cxh, 'Position') set(cxh, 'Position', pos); % XAxis Location is Top now set(ax1h,'Clim',[-1 1]); % XAxis Location is Bottom now

Summary: for-loops over Fixed-point Toolbox objects behave differently in Embedded MATLAB than in MATLAB.
Description: In MATLAB, for -loops over embedded.fi  arrays?iterate exactly once, regardless of the number of elements in the array.? In Embedded MATLAB, embedded.fi  arrays behave like other numeric arrays, iterating as many times as there are columns in the array.??This bug affects all implementations of Embedded MATLAB, including Embedded MATLAB Function blocks in Simulink, Embedded MATLAB functions in Stateflow, Truth Table Blocks, and the emlmex  command .
Workaround: Rewrite for-loops to use an integer variable instead of the embedded.fi array as the index. Consider the following example:x = fi([1 2 3]);for f = x? ? ... endIn MATLAB, the body of the for-loop executes once; in Embedded MATLAB, it executes three times. To achieve the same behavior in MATLAB and Embedded MATLAB, rewrite the code as follows:x = fi([1 2 3]);for i = 1:3? f = x(i);  ...end

Summary: MATLAB hangs on Ctrl+C?when pausing after getframe
Description: On UNIX, if MATLAB is made to wait with pause  immediately after a call to getframe , pressing Ctrl+C  can cause MATLAB to hang. This sequence can occur when getframe  is used to capture movie frames?and the user types Ctrl+C  to abort the movie loop.?For example,figure('position',[100 100 850 600]) ;Z = peaks; surf(Z); axis tight set(gca,'nextplot','replacechildren'); % Record the movie for j = 1:20     surf(sin(2*pi*j/20)*Z,Z)     F(j) = getframe;     disp('Press space to continue.');     pause;     % Typing control-c in here can cause MATLAB to hang!end [h, w, p] = size(F(1).cdata);

Summary: Invalid tests for models that reference Simulink.Parameter objects
Description: Tests generated by Simulink Design Verifier for models that reference Simulink.Parameter objects or any object that is a subclass or extension of Simulink.Parameter might be invalid. Simulink Design Verifier treats the references to these parameters as free inputs, causing tests to be invalid in cases where the parameter value affects a test objective.

Summary: When filter object's rounding mode is set to?'Round', generated? Verilog code does not always produce correctly rounded results
Description: When Verilog code is generated from?a filter object that has the 'Roundmode' property set to 'Round' ,?results obtained from?the generated code may have small numeric differences when compared to results obtained from?the original filter.?These results differ when the rounding operation removes exactly one bit at the least significant end (for example,?when converting from a fixed-point type with 'WordLength', 18, 'FractionLength, 17 to a type with 'WordLength', 17, 'FractionLength', 16).
Workaround: To work around?this problem in R2007b and earlier releases, make sure that at least two bits are removed during rounding?when generating Verilog code from a filter that has?its ?'RoundMode' property set to 'Round'.

Summary: Software incorrectly flags "Illegal usage of SS_OPTION_RATE_TRANSITION" when S-function block not handling rate transition
Description: Software might incorrectly flag the error "Illegal usage of SS_OPTION_RATE_TRANSITION " for an S-function block that specifies the SS_OPTION_RATE_TRANSITION  option (for example, an Integer Delay block) if either of the following conditions is true: The S-function block is placed in a triggered or function-call subsystem. The S-function block is fed with a constant or continuous signal.  The SS_OPTION_RATE_TRANSITION  option is intended to be used by S-functions with one input and one output port that have a discrete sample time and can handle rate transitions. However, under the listed conditions, the S-function block is not being used as a rate transition block, so its code should ignore the SS_OPTION_RATE_TRANSITION  option instead of issuing an error.
Workaround: To work around this bug, if you know that your S-function block will not be used as a rate transition block, manually remove the SS_OPTION_RATE_TRANSITION option from your S-function source code.

Summary: Bare-board target environment: generated code incorrectly?preempts faster rate, higher priority?task in some cases
Description: When all of the following conditions are true, Real-Time Workshop generated code allows preemption of a faster rate task with higher priority by a slower rate task with lower priority:     A GRT, ERT, or GRT/ERT-derived target is running a bare-board main program under control of a real-time clock without a real-time operating system.     The model is multirate and multitasking.     The model has at least two subrates, with no subrate being a multiple of another.  For example, consider a discrete time model that has 3 rates:     rate_0 : 1 ms     rate_1 : 2 ms     rate_2 : 3 ms  Suppose that task rate_1  executes in 1.2 ms, which is not overrunning since it has 2 ms to execute, and task rate_0  executes in .01 ms. At time = 3.01, the generated main function may start task rate_2 , preempting the higher priority task rate_1  while it is still executing.

Summary: Using the Haver fetch function to request quarterly series data (such as the GDP series) returns non-end-of-quarter dates.
Description: Using the Haver fetch function to request quarterly series data (such as the GDP series) returns non-end-of-quarter dates. The fourth quarter number that is returned denotes the last day of February of the following year, rather than the last day of the year as expected. h = haver('g:\dlx\data\USNA'); haverdata = fetch(h, 'PGDPQ', datenum('1/1/2000'), datenum('1/1/2007')); datestr(haverdata(:,1)) ans = 31-Mar-2000 30-Jun-2000 30-Sep-2000 28-Feb-2001 31-Mar-2001 30-Jun-2001 30-Sep-2001 28-Feb-2002 31-Mar-2002 30-Jun-2002 30-Sep-2002 28-Feb-2003 31-Mar-2003 30-Jun-2003 30-Sep-2003 29-Feb-2004 31-Mar-2004 30-Jun-2004 30-Sep-2004 28-Feb-2005 31-Mar-2005 30-Jun-2005 30-Sep-2005 28-Feb-2006 31-Mar-2006 30-Jun-2006 30-Sep-2006 28-Feb-2007 31-Mar-2007

Summary: Using the Property Inspector (the inspect function)?while in debug mode can crash MATLAB
Description: The following code causes MATLAB 7.4 (R2007a) to exit unexpectedly regardless of whether one continues (F5 or Debug/Run) or stops debugging:?dbstop if error inspect(get(gcf,'objectbeinginspected'));? In debug mode, MATLAB also exits unexpectedly while inspecting a simple object such as a figure handle should?you close the figure while the Inspector panel is still open:?dbstop if error inspect(gcf); close(gcf); This issue only arises when?inspecting objects while in debug mode.

Summary: Changes to model not indicated
Description: When the Periodic sample time constraint  parameter on the Configuration Parameters > Solver  pane is set to Specified , changing the Sample time properties  parameter does not dirty the block diagram. Therefore, you might not know that the model needs to be saved, and if you close the model without saving it, the Simulink software does not warn you that the changes to the model have not been saved.

Summary: Certain Simulink GUI operations crash MATLAB on LINUX systems with recent system libraries
Description: A conflict exists between the version of zlib shipped with MATLAB and the version shipped with recent Linux distributions. This conflict causes MATLAB to crash during many Simulink-related GUI operations, such as opening a block parameter dialog box.

Summary: MLINT warns about using PRINT function to print to a file in deployed applications.
Description: Compiled applications that run under the Microsoft Windows operating system must use DEPLOYPRINT instead of PRINT when printing to a printer. Either DEPLOYPRINT or PRINT may be used to print to a file. With compiler warnings enabled, MLINT warns about all uses of PRINT, even those that send the output to a file, such as 'print -dbmp myfigure.bmp'. MLINT issues the following warning: "MCC requires Windows applications to call DEPLOYPRINT instead of PRINT"  ?
Workaround: To disable the warning message, place the comment %#ok at the end of the line containing the PRINT command. For example,print -dbmp myfigure.bmp %#okPlease note that using %#ok only suppresses the warning message; it does not make it OK to use the PRINT command to send output to a printer in MATLAB Compiler-generated Windows applications. If your code prints to a printer, using 'print -f1' for example, you may suppress the warning with %#ok, but the code will still fail at runtime.

Summary: A model containing a Model block may fail to compile if a bus signal in the referenced model is testpointed
Description: If a model contains a referenced model with a testpointed bus signal, the model may fail to compile or simulate if the following condition is true:1. The testpointed bus signal in the referenced model has a mux signal as a component signalThe error produced will be similar to the following:### Model reference SIM target (referenced_msf.mexa64) for model referencedis out of date because referenced_msf.mexa64 does not exist### Updating model reference SIM target for model: referencedError: File: /tmp/slprj/sim/referenced/referenced.rtw Line: 930 Column: 15Identifier NumRegions multiply defined. Second and succeeding definitions ignored### Real-Time Workshop build procedure for model: 'referenced' aborted due to an error.??? Error: Errors occurred - aborting
Workaround: In the referenced model, do not log or testpoint?any bus signal that has a component mux signal.

Summary: Code generated for?Stateflow chart that invokes abs might incorrectly omit include file stdlib.h
Description: Code generated for an ERT-based Simulink model might incorrectly omit the include file stdlib.h  if all of the following conditions are true: The model contains a Stateflow chart. The Stateflow chart invokes the abs  function. On the Real-Time Workshop > Interface  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box, the Support: floating-point numbers  option is off.
Workaround: To work around this bug, perform one of the following steps:On the Real-Time Workshop > Interface pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box, select the Support: floating-point numbers option.On the Real-Time Workshop > Custom Code pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box, add the following line to the Header file section and regenerate code:#include "stdlib.h"

Summary: MATLAB Time Series Tools (tstool) cannot import logged data that contains newline characters
Description: The MATLAB Time Series Tools (tstool ) cannot import and visualize data logged by Simulink if any logged signal name contains a newline character. Newlines can appear in logged data if either: A logged signal name contains a newline. A logged signal is unnamed and originates from a block whose name contains a newline.  The visualization tool issues an error similar to: Data object with name ('xyz ') could not be interpreted for importing. Use a valid signal name. You may encounter this error when logged data contains newline characters and you: Invoke the Time Series Tools by typing tstool  in the MATLAB Command Window. Invoke the tools by choosing Tools > Inspect Logged Signals  from the Simulink Model Editor window. Use the Configuration Paramaters dialog box to launch the tools: In the Data Import/Export  pane, select Inspect signals when simulation is stopped/paused  Start or restart simulation, then pause simulation or allow it to complete normally.
Workaround: Either change the signal or block name to not contain a newline, or specify a custom name in the Signal Properties dialog box.

Summary: Why do I get awk warnings when I use gcc compiler to compile a MEX file on Solaris platform?
Description: When I try to compile any C-MEX file on a Solaris machine using the GCC compiler, I get the following warnings. The C-code compiles properly and I am able to run the generated MEX-file without any issues.awk: string too long near line 62awk: syntax error near line 62awk: illegal statement near line 62awk: newline in string near line 62awk: string too long near line 87awk: syntax error near line 87awk: illegal statement near line 87awk: newline in string near line 87
Workaround: Ignore the warning. It is harmless.

Summary: Real-Time Windows Target kernel may crash when tunable parameters?exceed available stack space
Description: The Real-Time Windows Target kernel may crash when tunable parameters are too large or numerous to be placed on the stack. When the total sum of memory needed for tunable parameters exceeds the available stack space, memory overflow occurs. The current version of the?Real-Time Windows Target kernel is not able to report this condition gracefully.
Workaround: Select the Inline parameters checkbox in the?Optimization pane of Configuration Parameters. This removes parameters from real-time kernel stack and significantly reduces stack requirements.

Summary: Calls to fixunknowns generates an error.?
Description: The function fixunknowns  generates an error for calls like this example: p1 = [1 NaN 3 2 NaN; 3 1 -1 2 4];[p2,ps] = fixunknowns(p1);
Workaround: The first workaround is to not use fixunknowns, if the training data does not have unknowns.To do this ensure that the string 'fixunknowns' does not appear in the list of function names in network property net.inputs{1}.processFcns.If fixunkowns is required, then change line 96,isfinite_unknowns = isfinite(x(i,:));to:finite_unknowns = isfinite(x(i,:));

Summary: Running Simulink on an X Window system can occasionally crash MATLAB
Description: When Simulink is running on an X Window system, Matlab occasionally crashes, often displaying the error message "Xlib: sequence lost".

Summary: Clicking the Simulink Diagnostic Viewer's Help button causes MATLAB to stop responding
Description: Clicking the Simulink Diagnostic Viewer's Help button causes MATLAB to stop responding.

Summary: Workers on Macintosh sometimes stall or cause a kernel panic when starting
Description: When using Distributed Computing Toolbox and MATLAB Distributed Computing Engine on a Macintosh, sometimes the Macintosh experiences a kernel panic when MATLAB workers are starting. Other times, the workers will not start correctly, and they can enter an "uninterruptible wait" (in a process listing from ps , the state will be shown as U+ ). This problem can occur for workers running under the local scheduler or under a job manager.  We believe that this problem exists only on Macintoshes running OS X versions 10.4.8 and later (Darwin kernel versions 8.8.*).

Summary: MATLAB and Simulink terminate unexpectedly?when certain fonts are present
Description: On some Linux and Windows?platforms, MATLAB and Simulink unexpectedly terminate when certain fonts are present.? This can occur either when you select File > Preferences > Fonts , or when you start Simulink.? After the abnormal termination, MATLAB usually generates an error log report that lists libfontmanager  and MJUtilities.getFontList .?The problem usually happens if you have certain TrueType fonts on your system.?Fonts known to cause this problem are Windows  bar39*.ttf eran.ttf BC C39 2 to 1 ******.ttf ZW Adobe F MOTOROLA7.ttf DPS Schrift Single.ttf LT_21234.TTF  Linux  ttf-bengali-fonts ttf-gujarati-fonts aakar-medium.ttf lohit_bn.ttf padmaa-Bold.0.6.ttf padmaa-Medium-0.5.ttf  For?Linux Ubuntu 7.04, one user reported this problem occurs when using the ATI proprietary fglrx display drivers with a code font size other than the default. The problem does not occur when using the Open Source?ATI drivers. Specifically, with a Radeon X300SE and the Open Source ATI drivers, the user changed the desktop code font to 14 point and then switched to using the ATI proprietary binary drivers, which produced the problem.
Workaround: As a workaround, perform the following:Unzip the contents of the attached zip file and copy the file, fontbug.class, to a directory on the MATLAB path for which you have write access. For example, on Windows, you can use the Documents\MATLAB directory.Start MATLAB.Change the current directory in MATLAB to the one where fontbug.class is.Run the following in the Command Window:system(['"',matlabroot,'\sys\java\jre\win32\jre1.5.0_07\bin\"java -classpath . fontbug'])? % Windowsorsystem(['"',matlabroot,'/sys/java/jre/glnx86/jre1.5/bin/"java -classpath . fontbug']) % LinuxIf you cannot run this statement, see the alternate instructions that follow the last step of this procedure.Java?proceeds through the system font list alphabetically and?displays each font. Java stops processing when it encounters a font that could cause this problem.?There are often several related fonts so you should run the command more than once, until you have removed all fonts that could potentially cause the problem. When there are no longer any fonts that might cause the problem, Java runs uninterrupted through the entire font list.For confirmation, look in the displayed list for the last font listed in the system font directory. For example, if Wingdings3 is the last font in the system font directory, look for it in the displayed list.Restart MATLAB and Simulink.If fonts other than those noted above were causing problems, e-mail the names of those fonts to MathWorks support.If you cannot run the above statement, delete the following known fonts, which could be causing the problem in MATLAB:bar39a.ttfbar39b.ttfbar39c.ttfbar39d.ttfbar39e.ttfbar39f.ttfDWBAR39.ttfFor the Linux Ubuntu 7.04 problem, the user reported this workaround:Revert to the Open Source ATI drivers.Change the font to the desktop default code font and size.Use the binary drivers.

Summary: Propagating signal labels works sporadically
Description: Selecting the "Show propagated signals" option in the Signal Properties dialog box does not always result in signal labels being propagated.

Summary: When multi-selecting many files, MATLAB can return an incorrect pathname and filename list
Description: This rare condition happens when the number of characters in a set of multiply-selected filenames is greater than the internal buffer can hold, and the last character placed in the buffer is a period.
Workaround: If possible, select fewer files at one time.

Summary: Figure window Position property can be affected by platform-specific size and position limits
Description: Certain platforms impose restrictions on window size and position.? For instance, some platforms make sure that a window can be no smaller than some miminum size.? The figure Position  property is affected by these limits and might report values that do no reflect the actual size and position of the window on the screen.

Summary: Given a model with two distinct sample times, if the faster sample time applies only to virtual blocks, then model references generate erroneous code
Description: Given a model consisting of two sets of blocks, A and B, let all of the blocks in A have a single "fast" sample time and let all of the blocks in B have a single "slow" sample time. Further, if the fast sample time applies only to virtual blocks (i.e., if set A consists solely of virtual blocks), then the code generated by the model reference will contain errors. These errors will cause the blocks in set B to run at the fast rate rather than the intended slow rate. Consequently, Simulink will produce incorrect simulation results. ?
Workaround: As a workaround, remove set A, i.e., remove the virtual blocks that have the fast sample time. In general, these blocks have no affect on the model because Simulink ignores their sample times (e.g., Signal Specification blocks).To identify these virtual blocks for removal, perform the following steps:1. Simultaneously compile the model to be referenced and access the array of its sample times by executing the command:[a,b,c,ts]=eval([mdl,'([],[],[],''compile'')']);where the variable mdl is the name of the model to be referenced. The array ts now contains the sample times in the model. Note: If ts has more than 2 rows, your model is not affected by this bug; this bug only occurs in dual-rate models.2. To identify set A, search the model for blocks whose compiled sample time is the faster of the two rates by executing the command:setA = find_system(mdl,'LookUnderMasks','all','FollowLinks','on','CompiledSampleTime',ts(1,:))The list of blocks in set A represent the fast, virtual blocks.3. Complete the compilation of the model by executing the command: eval([mdl,'([],[],[],''term'')'])Note: Turning on Sample Time Colors from the Format menu may help you to understand the role of these virtual blocks within the model.

Summary: Reusing a window to display a masked subsystem can cause a memory error
Description: Using the open_system  command to open a masked subsystem with window-reuse enabled causes a memory segmentation violation.
Workaround: Open the masked subsystem in a separate window and close the first window separately.

Summary: Part of my figure gets cut off when when filling the page in Print Preview
Description: I have a large figure that I would like to export to a PDF file. When I view the print preview and adjust the sizing to fill the page, part of the figure is cut off. The figure has an axes on the left side, and a text box with a LaTeX table on the right. The last column of the table is being cut off when exporting.
Workaround: This figure printing behavior is normal, but is not properly documented. Clicking Fill page changes the PaperPosition to fill the page, allowing objects in normalized units to expand to fill the space. If an object has an absolute size, as the LaTeX table does, it can overflow the page when objects with normalized units expand. The print preview documentation fails to completely explain what happens when objects having normalized and other units are plotted together.

Summary: Multithreading: abnormal termination sometimes silent; does not prompt for Windows error reporting
Description: On Windows platforms when multithreading is enabled, if MATLAB terminates abnormally it cannot try to return control to the Command Window. Instead, a Windows error reporting dialog box should open. Sometimes it does not open, and MATLAB terminates without any notification.
Workaround: Verify that Windows error reporting is enabled:From the Windows desktop, select Start > Settings > Control Panel. The Control Panel folder opens.Double-click System. The System Properties dialog box opens.Click the Advanced tab.Click the Error Reporting button.In the resulting Error Reporting dialog box, select Enable error reporting, and click OK.Click OK to accept the changes and close the System Properties dialog box.Even with Windows error reporting enabled, MATLAB might still terminate without any notification, which is the bug this error report is about.

Summary: Undefined function or variable error message incorrectly appears in the Command Window at times
Description: Error messages such as ??? Error using ==> evalUndefined function or variable 'EXCEL_LINK_TMP_VAR_7055'. sometimes appear in the Command Window for no apparent reason. When these error messages appear, the Workspace browser is often open. These error messages do not cause any problems and you can ignore them.

Summary: DKSYN fails to return valid controller
Description: During an iteration using DKSYN, a controller may fail to be synthesized even when previous iterations produced valid controllers. This causes DKSYN to crash. Instead, DKSYN must return the best synthesized controller that achieves the smallest value of mu. DKSYN has been modified to behave correctly when a controller is not synthesized during an iteration.

Summary: Publishing MATLAB? code to Microsoft? PowerPoint? fails on Microsoft? Office 2007
Description: Attempts to publish MATLAB code to Microsoft? PowerPoint? fails on Microsoft? Office 2007. For instance, issuing the following commands: options = [];options.outputDir = tempdir;options.format = 'ppt'; publish('intro',options) Returns errors such as these: ??? No appropriate method or public field Add for class Interface.Microsoft_PowerPoint_12.0_Object_Library.Slides. Error in ==> codetools\private\mxdom2ppt at 20 slide = presentationSlides.Add(slidesCount,'ppLayoutBlank'); Error in ==> publish at 215 mxdom2ppt(dom,outputAbsoluteFilename);

Summary: Problems in avhrrgoode function
Description: There are multiple problems with the avhrrgoode  function:    If a non-global region  has been specified, the spatial coordinates of the output pixels are displaced by 1/2 pixel (500m for the 1-km resolution datasets)     If the size of the data returned from a global dataset is compared to the size of the data returned from a regional dataset, they may differ by one column.     In some cases, when the specified latitude limits cross the Equator, values of NaN are returned for the equatorial coordinates.     With certain latitude and longitude limits, an error occurs when attempting to read the dataset, even though the limits are valid.     If longitude values are specified outside the range [-180 180] or non-ascending limits have been specified, data is returned from the wrong area.     Parameters for inputs other than region  or filename  may be omitted as stated in the documentation, but cause an error when specified as empty.     The syntax avhrrgoode  and avhrrgoode(region)  are documented, but cause an error.
Workaround: To work around these issues, replace the avhrrgoode.m function file in your installation with the version in the attachment, as follows:    Exit MATLAB.    Make a backup copy of the following file.    MATLABROOT\matlab\toolbox/map\mapformats\avhrrgoode.m    MATLABROOT represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function.    For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension: avhrrgoode.m.old        Copy the version of avhrrgoode.m from the attachment into the directory specified in the previous step.    Restart MATLAB.    Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Solaris? platforms: Some keys not working when MATLAB? software runs remotely
Description: When you run MATLAB remotely on a Sun Microsystems? Solaris platform from a Linux or Microsoft? Windows? platform, some keys such as Enter , <-  (backspace), Delete , Esc , and others, might not work in the Command Window, Editor/Debugger, or other GUI tools. This is due to a known bug in Sun software. Sun has issued a report on this bug (6173972).
Workaround: Some users have had success with these workarounds.Press the Num Lock key to turn off num lock mode.Alternately, set the environment variable _AWT_USE_TYPE4_PATCH, to false before starting MATLAB by runningsetenv _AWT_USE_TYPE4_PATCH false

Summary: MCR installation stops abruptly and displays error message
Description: When installing the MATLAB Component Runtime (MCR) using MCRinstaller.exe , the installation aborts and you receive the following error message: "The wizard was interrupted before MATLAB componentRuntime 7.5 could be completely installed.You system has not been modified.  To complete installation at another time, please run setup again." Depending on which version of the Microsoft installer you have on your system, you might see this error message instead: "Error 1304 writing to file MWArray.dll  Verify that you have access to that directory and later an Internal Error 2350 popup."
Workaround: Download the?Microsoft Visual C++ Redistributable Package. For 32-bit systems, download the SP1 version of vcredist_x86.exe.? For 64-bit systems, download vcredist_x64.exe.? These self-extracting executable files install runtime components of Visual C++ Libraries required to run applications developed with Visual C++ on a computer that does not have Visual C++ 2005 installed.Install the package on the target computer?by double-clicking on the file you downloaded.Re-execute MCRInstaller.exe.NOTE: When you run the MCR installer,?you will see a?pop-up message indicating that .Net Framework is not installed. Click the?option to continue without installing .NET Framework.If you still get this error message after installing the Visual C++ Redistributable Package (vcredist_x86.exe), you're installation of the .NET 1.1 framework is probably corrupt. Reinstall .NET 1.1 (or install .NET 2.0) before reinstalling the MCR.

Summary: MATLAB Report Generator commmands fail in compiled applications
Description: When I compile MATLAB-code containing the REPORT command, I get one of two errors: Failure reading file:/$MCR_ROOT/v74/sys/namespace/docbook/v4/xsl/html/docbook_rptgen.xsl where $MCR_ROOT is the installation directory of the MATLAB Component Runtime (MCR).Or: Conversion from RTF to DOC failedJava exception occurred:java.lang.NullPointerException at com.mathworks.toolbox.rptgencore.output.OutputFormat.getExtension(Out.utFormat.java:132)
Workaround: This problem is due to files missing from the MCR. To workaround this issue, do the following:1. Install the MCR on your computer.2. Add or replace the directory $MCR_ROOT/$VER/sys/namespace/ with the following directory from your MATLAB installation: $MATLABROOT/sys/namespace.Important Notes:$MCR_ROOT is the root directory of MATLAB Component Runtime (MCR) installation.$VER is the version of the installed MCR (for example, v73 or v74).$MATLABROOT is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by typing: matlabrootat the MATLAB command prompt.Copy the directory from your MATLAB installation. Do not delete it.3. Place a copy of the directory $MATLABROOT/sys/jade in the MCR's sys directory ($MCR_ROOT/$VER/sys/).You can now archive this altered version of the MCR installation directory and distribute it with your application.

Summary: Static text uicontrol clips text for default Macintosh font
Description: On the Mac, the text in a static text uicontrol is clipped at the top when the default font and fontsize are used.? This happens because the space allotted for the default font is too small on the Mac.
Workaround: Try using other fonts and/or fontsizes for static text uicontrols.

Summary: subs runs slower than in R2006a (V3.1.4)
Description: The subs  function runs more slowly than it did in R2006a and prior releases. In R2006b, subs  was changed to use variable-precision arithmetic instead of double precision.
Workaround: To perform the substitution in double precision, convert the expression to a MATLAB function handle and evaluate the function. The function handle should use .* and .^ instead of * and ^, respectively and might need other changes in order to evaluate in MATLAB's workspace. For example, to convert the expression x*y^2 to a function handle and substitute x=2, y=3, do the following:>> syms x y>> f = x*y^2;>> f_fun = vectorize(char(f));>> f_fun = @(x,y)eval(f_fun);>> f_fun(2,3)

Summary: Simulink sometimes fails to report errors when it logs mixed-dimension root outport signals to the workspace using Array format.
Description: The Array format for saving outputs to the workspace requires all signals connected to root outports to be scalars or 1-D arrays.?However, Simulink fails to report?an error when the first root outport connects to a scalar but other?root outputs connect to signals with mixed dimensions.
Workaround: In order to detect this problem, do the following:Open the model.From model menu, choose Format > Port/Signal Displays > Signal Dimensions.Simulate the model.If no error is reported, check the dimension of each root outport signal after the first.? Any that is not a scalar or 1-D array?should have generated an?error.?If any of the root outport signals is a wide (non-scalar), N-D (N>1) signal, do one of the following to correct the error:Set Configuration Parameters > Data Import/Export > Format to either Structure or Structure with time.For command line simulation with left-hand-side argument, run the simset command and set the SaveFormat option to either Structure or StructureWithTime.Insert Reshape blocks as needed?and use them to convert all?non-scalar, N-D (N>1)?root outport signals to 1-D signals.

Summary: Error when saving a model: "There are too many objects in the model"
Description: When saving a model containing more than about 100 Configuration Set objects you may see this error message: ?There are too many objects in the model.
Workaround: To eliminate this?error, use the?Model Explorer?to?delete some of the Configuration Set objects.? Attempt to save the mode after each deletion as a test to determine when enough have been removed.

Summary: Some Java objects do not display correctly when in cell arrays, structs, or Java arrays
Description: Some Java objects are displayed incorrectly when viewed as part of a cell array, structure, or Java array.h1 = java.util.Hashtable h1 =    {} h1.put('a',3) ans =      [] h1 h1 =    {a=3.0} carray = {h1} carray =     [Error]

Summary: Error generating code for S-function target for models that contain no L and C elements
Description: There is an error message with generating code for S-function target from a model that contains no L and no C elements. When trying to generate code from the model (S-function target) you will receive errors: Error swfun_inverter_sf.c: .\swfun_inverter_mid.h: 263  unknown field `StateSpace_P3' of `struct _Parameters_swfun_inverter' Error swfun_inverter_sf.c: .\swfun_inverter_mid.h: 263  type error: pointer expected Error swfun_inverter_sf.c: .\swfun_inverter_mid.h: 271  unknown field `StateSpace_P4' of `struct _Parameters_swfun_inverter' Error swfun_inverter_sf.c: .\swfun_inverter_mid.h: 271  type error:
Workaround: In order to generate code, your model must contain at least one inductor or one capacitor element. Try to add one Series RLC Branch block to your model to model dummy inductance L or dummy capacitor C.

Summary: Model reference builds involving Stateflow charts can cause charts to rebuild unnecessarily
Description: Performing model reference builds for referenced models that contain Stateflow charts can cause the charts to rebuild unnecessarily. This behavior occurs under these conditions: The referenced model contains a Stateflow chart. The Model block that references this model is set to Accelerator  mode. A Real-Time Workshop build for the top-level model is invoked, for example, by using CTRL-B.
Workaround: There is no workaround for this inefficiency.

Summary: Running mex filename or mex -setup fails with certain Japanese characters in TEMP environment variable
Description: If you use a Japanese character that has a Katakana So or other 5c character in the $TEMP  variable on a Japanese Windows machine, mex filename  or mex -setup  will error with: "$MATLAB/bin/mex.pl: Error: Could not create directory "$TEMP/mex_#######":Unable to complete successfully".
Workaround: Remove all Japanese characters that have a Katakana So or other 0x5c characters in the $TEMP variable or input paths to mex.

Summary: List box uicontrol does not scroll past lowest entry to extend selection via mouse
Description: In a list box user interface control (uicontrol ) in which multiple selection is enabled, when a block of entries is selected by clicking and dragging the mouse over them such that the selection goes past the bottom edge of the list box, the list box does not scroll automatically to extend the selection.
Workaround: Use another method for selecting contiguous items in a list box. Here are two possibilities:Click the first element you want to select and then while pressing Ctrl+Shift click the last element that you want to select. This selects the two entries you click and all the entries in between.Use the keyboard arrow keys to make the selection. Click the first element you want to select and then while pressing Shift hold the down arrow key to select additional, contiguous entries that follow it.

Summary: Order of signals in linearized model I/O not predictable when linearization I/O is at a virtual bus signal
Description: When a linearization I/O is at an output port of a virtual bus, the actual order that the signals are listed in the I/Os of the resulting linearized model may not be the expected order. The expected order of the signals is the order that the signals are specified in the bus. For nested buses, the expected order of the signals is found by moving signal to signal from the top of the bus through each nested bus (or depth first search).
Workaround: Replace the file [MATLABROOT]\toolbox\slcontrol\slcontrol\@LinearizationObjects\@linearizationutil\getJacobian.m as follows:Download one of the following attached files for the version of MATLAB you are using: getJacobian_R2006b.m, getJacobian_R2007a.m, and getJacobian_R2007b.m for R2006b, R2007a, and R2007b respectively.Replace the file [MATLABROOT]\toolbox\slcontrol\slcontrol\@LinearizationObjects\@linearizationutil\getJacobian.m with the downloaded file.

Summary: S-function target: code generated from model with custom source code may fail to build
Description: Code generated for the S-function target from models with custom source code may be uncompilable. By default, Real-Time Workshop does not include custom source code when generating code for the S-function target.
Workaround: To work around this bug, perform the following steps:Note: In the following instructions, matlabroot represents your MATLAB installation root, as returned by the MATLAB command matlabroot.If you are using R2007b, go to your your MATLAB installation tree and rename the following file, for example, by adding .old to the end of the file name.matlabroot/rtw/c/rtwsfcn/rtwsfcn.tlcDownload the attached file, rtwsfcn.tlc.forR2007b, rename it to rtwsfcn.tlc, and copy it into the same directory as the file you renamed.Skip the next step.If you are using a release earlier than R2007b, copy the following file to a different name and/or location, while retaining the original file in place.matlabroot/rtw/c/rtwsfcn/rtwsfcn.tlcOpen the file matlabroot/rtw/c/rtwsfcn/rtwsfcn.tlc for editing, and manually incorporate the following code segment, such that it appears before the line "rtwgensettings.IsRTWSfcn = 'yes';".rtwoptions(5).prompt = 'Include custom source code';rtwoptions(5).type = 'Checkbox';rtwoptions(5).default = 'off';rtwoptions(5).tlcvariable = 'AlwaysIncludeCustomSrc';rtwoptions(5).tooltip = ...['Always include provided custom source code in the generated code'];Restart MATLAB.Load your model, open the Configuration Parameters dialog box, and go to the Real-Time Workshop > Real-Time Workshop S-Function Code Generation Options pane. You will find a new Include custom source code check box.Select the check box and build the model. The build should now include and compile your custom source code, provided that you have appropriately set up the relevant include path.

Summary: Mouse does not work on target PC
Description: The mouse does not work on the target PC. Setting Target mouse  to any setting on the xPC Target Explorer Configuration > Appearance  node has no effect.

Summary: M-Lint messages are truncated by Windows? task bar when both are on right side of screen
Description: If you position the Microsoft? Windows task bar along the right side of the screen, and the MATLAB? Editor/Debugger window is also along or near the right edge, the M-Lint messages might be truncated. This is due to a known bug in Sun Microsystems? software. Sun has issued a report on this bug (4711713).
Workaround: There are three possible workarounds: Move the Editor/Debugger window away from the edge of the screen.Move the Windows task bar to the bottom or left side of the screen.In properties for the Windows taskbar, clear the option Keep the taskbar on top of other windows.

Summary: Decreased performance of deployed applications on Linux
Description: Performance of a compiled application/component on Linux is degraded. Depending on the MATLAB-code, the compiled application may run up to ten times slower than the same code running in MATLAB.
Workaround: None.

Summary: Macintosh Mac OS X 10.4: MATLAB Freezes when Entering Japanese Characters in Command Window
Description: On Macintosh systems running Mac OS X 10.4 with Java versions prior to 1.4.2_09, MATLAB becomes nonresponsive when you enter a Japanese character in the Command Window.
Workaround: Updating the Mac OS X 10.4 version of Java to 1.4.2_09 using Software Update fixes this problem.You can also download the Java update from http://www.apple.com/support/downloads/java131and142release2.html

Summary: Macintosh OS X 10.4 and Non-US Keyboard: MATLAB Terminates for ^ (Caret) and Other Keys in Command Window
Description: On Macintosh OS X 10.4 systems with a non-US keyboard, MATLAB becomes nonresponsive or terminates unexpectedly when you try to enter certain characters in the Command Window. This is due to a known Java VM problem that prevents the use of any dead key. For many non-US keyboards, you use a dead key to create the caret (^) and the tilde (~) characters.
Workaround: This problem has been fixed with the Java 1.4.2_09 version for Mac OS X 10.4, which is available as an automatic software update from Apple, and can also be downloaded from here:http://www.apple.com/support/downloads/java131and142release2.htmlTo work around the problem without the update, use another application to create the character, and then cut and paste it into the Command Window. Another workaround is to replace a character with its functional equivalent, if possible. For example, use the power function instead of the caret (^) key, and use the not function instead of the tilde (~) key.

Summary: Inaccurate scale distortion value returned by function vinvtran
Description: In addition to converting an azimuth angle from the map coordinate plane to the surface of the reference ellipsoid, function vinvtran  supports a second, optional output: the factor by which the projection would scale a displacement in the direction of that azimuth, which is an indication of the local scale distortion. The basic computational approach takes a simple first difference, which is fast and should be sufficiently accurate for many applications. Unfortunately, however, the current implementation suffers very badly from numerical noise for points near the central meridian of the projection (having a true easting near zero). For such points the scale distortion results can differ from the correct values by 100% or more.
Workaround: To work around this issue, replace the vinvtran.m function file in your current installation with the version in the attachment, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file. matlabroot/toolbox/map/mapproj/vinvtran.mmatlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function.For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension, vinvtran.m.OLD.Copy the version of vinvtran.m from the attachment into the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.?

Summary: Memory leaks can occur when math and indexing operations are performed on large fixed-point objects
Description: Memory leaks can occur when you apply simple math or indexing operations to large fi  objects. For example, a summation between two fi  objects should consume memory temporarily and then release it when done. This does not occur, and the memory after similar math and indexing operations such as addition and permute is not released.

Summary: instrhwinfo returns incorrect vendor driver version
Description: The vendor driver version returned by instrhwinfo  to the VendorDriverVersion  field is incorrect for Tektronix VISA and Agilent VISA.

Summary: Signals can have hidden names that cause model update errors
Description: Editing a model can result in a signal having a hidden name. This can happen when you disconnect a line, delete its label, and then reconnect the line to a port that was previously connected to a labeled line. The rewired line adopts the name of the signal previously connected to the port but does not display the name as a label. If the hidden name is the same as another signal's name, updating the model causes a duplicate signal name error.
Workaround: Avoid deleting labels on disconnected signal lines.

Summary: Serial port fprintf sporadically times out
Description: Serial fprintf  can sporadically time out. The following example reproduces the problem after a few thousand iterations. ? %M-File 'TimeoutError':   g = instrfind;   if ~isempty(g);       fclose(g);   end   teststr = char('scan');   s1 = serial('COM1');   set(s1, 'BaudRate', 9600);   set(s1, 'DataBits', 7);   set(s1, 'FlowControl', 'none');   set(s1, 'RequestToSend','off');   set(s1, 'Parity', 'even');   set(s1, 'Terminator', 'CR');   set(s1, 'Timeout', 10);   set(s1, 'OutputBufferSize', length(teststr) + 1);   fopen(s1);   set(s1, 'RequestToSend', 'on');   ii = 0;   while(1)       ii = ii + 1;       fprintf(s1, teststr);       disp(ii);    end; This is an example of a typical error: ??? Error using ==> serial.fwrite A timeout occurred during the write operation.
Workaround: For MATLAB R2006a through R2007b, save the attached file as serial.zip. Unzip the file in the <MATLABROOT> directory to extract the fix.>> cd(matlabroot);>> unzip('C:\TEMP\serial.zip');To remove the fix, delete SerialComm.class from <MATLABROOT>\java\patch\com\mathworks\toolbox\instrument\SerialComm.class.>> cd(matlabroot); >> !del java\patch\com\mathworks\toolbox\instrument\SerialComm.class?

Summary: Documentation: ERT target option relationships, dependencies, interactions, and error messages not sufficiently covered
Description: The Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder documentation does not adequately describe the following relationships and dependencies among ERT target options on the Real-Time Workshop /Interface  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog: The options GRT compatible call interface  and Single output/update function  are mutually incompatible. The options MAT-file logging  and Suppress error status in real-time model data structure  are mutually incompatible. The options Suppress error status in real-time model data structure  and Support continuous time  are mutually incompatible. Selecting Support non-finite numbers  requires Support floating-point numbers  to be selected. Selecting Support non-inlined S-functions  requires Support floating-point numbers  and Support non-finite numbers  to be selected. Selecting GRT compatible call interface  requires Support floating-point numbers  to be selected. Selecting MAT-file logging  requires Support floating-point numbers , Support non-finite numbers , and Terminate function required  to be selected.  Additionally, some interactions between these options are not fully documented. For example: If you select GRT compatible call interface , Single output/update function  is grayed out and cannot be selected. If you select MAT-file logging , Suppress error status in real-time model data structure  is deselected. If you subsequently select Suppress error status in real-time model data structure , the conflict will be flagged with a message during code generation. If you select Suppress error status in real-time model data structure , Support continuous time  is deselected. If you subsequently select Support continuous time , the conflict will be flagged with a message during code generation.

Summary: PDF Version of Documentation Fails to Open
Description: In the MATLAB Help browser, clicking a link to open a printable (PDF) version of the documentation fails and displays an error in the Command Window for some system configurations.

Summary: Incorrect output from function setpostn near cell boundaries
Description: For certain inputs involving points near boundaries between grid cells, the row and column subscripts returned by function setpostn  are off by 1. For points near the northern and eastern edges of the data grid, but still within the grid, setpostn  can return a subscript value that exceeds the corresponding grid size. See Bug Report 420038  regarding a similar problem in function limitm .
Workaround: To work around this issue, replace the version of the setpostn.m function in your current installation with the updated version available from Bug Report 511979. That version of setpostn fixes both this problem and a residual problem left over from the solution of this bug in R2008a.

Summary: Line trimming in functions geoshow and maptriml
Description: When function maptriml  trims a set of lines to the boundary of a geographic quadrangle, it completely removes line segments that connect a vertex inside the quadrangle to a vertex outside. Subsequent map displays can thus have pieces missing near the edge of the map. Instead, maptriml  should interpolate a new vertex on the bounding meridian or parallel at the location where a given segment crosses out of the quadrangle. Function geoshow  behaves in the same way when displaying lines in a projection with quadrangular frame limits (any conic or cylindrical projection, for example). The problem is most noticeable when the input line vector data is sparsely-sampled relative to the extent of the map frame. geoshow  exhibits a similar problem when used with azimuthal projections (in which the frame limits are circular), although this is rarely noticed because such projections are used mainly for small-scale maps that show large areas of the Earth. The functions plotm  and linem  also exhibit these problems (for both circular and quadrangular frame limits). However, they need to work as they do in order to produce reasonable plots for points as well as lines. The reason is that these and most other plotting functions cannot distinguish vectors of points from vectors of lines. Interpolating new points at the edge of the map when given point data would cause greater problems than discarding points outside the limits given line data, as happens currently.

Summary: Mismatch between GUIDE layout and runtime GUI makes GUI design difficult
Description: When you run a GUI you are designing in GUIDE, the appearance of certain UI components can differ from what they look like in the GUIDE layout area. Such discrepancies can appear whether you start the GUI from within GUIDE or from the MATLAB command line. For example, changes to the foreground color of a radio button have no effect in GUIDE layout area. As a result, you might have to iterate between laying out and running a GUI more times than you should to get the results you want.
